a-blog cms.history: a-blog cms is a Japanese content management system developed and maintained by appleple inc., a web company based in Japan. It has been available since approximately 2008 and is primarily used by Japanese websites.
a-blog cms.summary_abuse: a-blog cms has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted Japanese CMS with limited international presence and no consumer-facing anonymous content hosting service.

A-Frame.description: A-Frame is an open-source web framework that simplifies building cross-platform virtual reality (VR) experiences using HTML and JavaScript.
A-Frame.history: A-Frame was originally conceived within Mozilla's VR team and publicly released in December 2015 by Diego Marcos and Kevin Ngo. The framework is built on top of Three.js and uses HTML-like syntax for building WebVR/WebXR experiences. After Mozilla reduced its VR investment, the core creators spun out to form Supermedium, which now maintains A-Frame. It has grown into one of the largest WebVR/WebXR communities and joined the OpenJS Foundation ecosystem.
A-Frame.summary_abuse: A-Frame is an open-source WebXR framework with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history. It is a frontend library used by developers to build 3D and VR web experiences.

A2Z Events.description: A2Z Events is an event management solution designed by and for event professionals.
A2Z Events.history: A2Z Events was founded in 1998 and has been trusted by event professionals for over 25 years. The platform is owned by Personify, headquartered in Columbia, Maryland. It specializes in trade show, conference, and exposition management for associations and nonprofits.
A2Z Events.summary_abuse: A2Z Events is a B2B event management platform serving associations and nonprofits with no documented abuse history. The platform requires contract-based business accounts and handles exhibitor and attendee management. No consumer-facing anonymous access.

a3 Lazy Load.description: a3 Lazy Load is a mobile oriented, very simple to use plugin that will speed up sites page load speed.
a3 Lazy Load.history: a3 Lazy Load was first released on December 20, 2014, by Steve Truman under the a3rev Software brand. It is a WordPress plugin that adds lazy loading for images, videos, and iframes. No notable ownership changes or forks since release.
a3 Lazy Load.summary_abuse: a3 Lazy Load is a WordPress performance plugin with no notable abuse signal. It operates entirely on the site owner's server and has no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

A8.net.description: A8.net is an affiliate marketing network.
A8.net.history: A8.net was founded in 2000 and is operated by FAN Communications, headquartered in Shibuya, Tokyo. It is Japan's largest affiliate marketing network with over 2 million publishing sites and 15,000 advertisers. The platform has held the highest user satisfaction rating in Japan for 15 consecutive years and serves as the primary gateway for affiliate marketing in the Japanese market.
A8.net.summary_abuse: A8.net is Japan's dominant affiliate marketing network. As with all large affiliate networks, some affiliates may engage in cookie stuffing, misleading endorsements, or spam-based promotion. FAN Communications enforces an affiliate code of conduct and moderates publisher accounts. The platform's scale means occasional abuse incidents occur, but the vendor has active enforcement processes.

Aacio.description: Aacio is an all-in-one platform that integrates multiple applications to offer tools, support, and resources aimed at facilitating business operations and growth.
Aacio.history: Aacio is an all-in-one business platform with e-commerce and operational tools. Limited public history available.
Aacio.summary_abuse: Aacio has no notable abuse signal. It is a small all-in-one business SaaS with no documented abuse patterns.

Aarki.description: Aarki is an AI platform that develops advertising solutions aimed at increasing mobile revenue growth.
Aarki.history: Aarki is an AI-driven mobile advertising platform founded around 2010 in San Francisco, CA. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes documented.
Aarki.summary_abuse: Aarki is a mobile advertising network. Ad tech platforms can be misused for ad fraud, malvertising, and user tracking without consent, though Aarki is a legitimate B2B platform with standard industry practices and no documented specific abuse incidents.

Aasaan.description: Aasaan is a no-code e-commerce platform that helps digitalize businesses to grow.
Aasaan.history: Aasaan is an Indian no-code e-commerce platform founded around 2020, targeting small businesses in India looking to digitalize. No notable ownership changes documented.
Aasaan.summary_abuse: Aasaan is an e-commerce platform builder. Like all e-commerce enablement tools, it could be used to set up fraudulent storefronts, but there is no documented pattern of specific abuse on this platform.

AB Tasty.description: AB Tasty is a customer experience optimisation company. AB Tasty offers AI-driven experimentation, personalisation, and product optimisation platforms for user testing.
AB Tasty.history: Founded in 2009 as web analytics agency Liwio, co-founders Remi Aubert and Alix de Sagazan rebranded as AB Tasty in 2011 to launch a SaaS A/B testing platform. The company grew to become a leading European digital experience optimization platform. In January 2026, Indian company Wingify acquired AB Tasty as part of a Everstone Capital-led combination valued at over $100 million in combined ARR.
AB Tasty.summary_abuse: AB Tasty is an enterprise A/B testing and personalization platform with no documented abuse history. The platform injects JavaScript on client websites for experimentation, which requires careful implementation to avoid performance or security issues. No evidence of use in malicious campaigns.

AbanteCart.description: AbanteCart is an open-source ecommerce platform designed to help businesses create and manage online stores with built-in product management, order processing, and customer engagement features.
AbanteCart.history: AbanteCart is a free, open-source PHP ecommerce platform first released around 2011. Built on PHP8, HTML5, Bootstrap 5, jQuery, and MVC architecture, it allows merchants to create and manage online stores. Development is maintained by an enthusiast community on GitHub with ongoing releases covering payment extensions, Unicode database support, and form manager improvements.
AbanteCart.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents specific to AbanteCart. As a self-hosted open-source ecommerce solution, security depends on individual operator configuration. No major CVEs or exploited vulnerabilities widely reported. The software follows standard ecommerce security practices with PCI DSS guidance.

AbhiCMS.description: AbhiCMS is a lesser-known content management system that may not have a significant user base or active development community.
AbhiCMS.history: AbhiCMS is a very minor PHP/MySQL-based CMS with an extremely small user base and no public development community. No notable ownership or deprecation events are documented.
AbhiCMS.summary_abuse: AbhiCMS has no notable abuse signal; it is an obscure CMS used by very few sites with no documented history of being targeted or exploited by scammers at scale.

Abicart.description: Abicart is an ecommerce platform developed by the Swedish company Abicart AB.
Abicart.history: Abicart is a Swedish ecommerce platform developed by Abicart AB and Textalk AB, active for over 25 years. It provides full-featured online stores with multi-language and multi-currency support, fixed monthly pricing with no transaction fees, and strong GDPR compliance as a Swedish company. It serves thousands of Nordic businesses.
Abicart.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents or security violations. Abicart has a 100 out of 100 trust score on gridinsoft with no malware or phishing blacklist detections. As a regulated Swedish ecommerce provider with strong privacy policies, it maintains a clean track record.

Able CDP.description: Able CDP is a customer journey tracking system that monitors and analyzes user interactions across multiple touchpoints to provide insights into engagement and behavior.
Able CDP.history: Able CDP is a customer data platform that tracks user journeys across multiple digital touchpoints. Limited public history available.
Able CDP.summary_abuse: Able CDP has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B customer data platform with no consumer-facing surface that would attract fraudsters.

ABLyft.description: ABlyft is an A/B-Testing Platform made for developers.
ABLyft.history: ABlyft is a German A/B testing platform developed for professional developers and experienced conversion rate optimization specialists. The platform positions itself on privacy compliance and performance, storing all data in aggregated form without personal data retention. Founding year is not prominently documented publicly.
ABLyft.summary_abuse: ABlyft is a professional A/B testing platform with privacy-by-design principles and no documented abuse history. The platform does not store personal data and targets enterprise-level testing professionals. No consumer-facing attack surface.

ABOUT YOU Commerce Suite.description: ABOUT YOU Commerce Suite is an enterprise ready infrastructure solution designed for ecommerce companies.
ABOUT YOU Commerce Suite.history: ABOUT YOU was founded in 2014 in Hamburg as a subsidiary of the Otto Group, Germany's second-largest ecommerce company. The ABOUT YOU Commerce Suite (branded as SCAYLE) was spun off as a B2B enterprise commerce platform offering the technology that powered ABOUT YOU's own marketplace to other brands. In January 2025, Zalando launched a public takeover offer, and the acquisition was completed in July 2025, making ABOUT YOU a Zalando subsidiary.
ABOUT YOU Commerce Suite.summary_abuse: ABOUT YOU Commerce Suite is an enterprise ecommerce platform used by established fashion and retail brands. No documented abuse history. The platform serves enterprise clients under managed contracts with full identity verification. Now part of Zalando group.

AboutMyClinic.description: AboutMyClinic is a website builder designed exclusively for doctors to create and manage professional medical practice websites.
AboutMyClinic.history: AboutMyClinic is a website builder specialized for medical practices. Limited public history available.
AboutMyClinic.summary_abuse: AboutMyClinic has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche medical practice website builder with no documented abuse patterns.

ABP Framework.description: ABP Framework is a complete infrastructure to create modern web applications by following the best practices and conventions of software development.
ABP Framework.history: ABP Framework was developed by Volosoft, a Turkish software company, evolving from the earlier ASP.NET Boilerplate project first released around 2013. The modern ABP Framework (abp.io) was launched circa 2019 as a complete rewrite for ASP.NET Core. It follows a dual-license model: open-source MIT for the framework, with commercial modules and support available. ASP.NET Boilerplate official support is ending in May 2026, with ABP Framework as the migration path.
ABP Framework.summary_abuse: ABP Framework is an open-source ASP.NET Core application framework with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history. It is used by enterprise developers to build web applications.

Abralytics.description: Abralytics is a privacy-focused alternative to Google Analytics that provides email reports.
Abralytics.history: Abralytics is a privacy-focused analytics platform providing email-based reports as an alternative to Google Analytics. Limited public history available.
Abralytics.summary_abuse: Abralytics has no notable abuse signal. It is a privacy-focused analytics tool that does not collect intrusive data and has no documented abuse patterns.

Absorb.description: Absorb is a cloud-based learning management system.
Absorb.history: Absorb LMS was founded in 2003 in Calgary, Canada. It provides a cloud-based learning management system for corporate training with an emphasis on user experience, reporting, and integrations. The platform uses per-active-learner pricing negotiated through enterprise contracts. It is well-regarded in mid-market and enterprise segments and has received strong ratings on G2 and Capterra.
Absorb.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents or data breaches. Absorb LMS is a reputable enterprise learning platform with SOC 2 Type 2 compliance and HIPAA compliance for healthcare clients. No regulatory violations or security incidents publicly documented.

Accentuate Custom Fields.description: Accentuate Custom Fields is the professional and de facto solution to easily extend your Shopify store with your own custom fields such multi-language text fields, images, checkboxes, dates, selection list and custom JSON objects.
Accentuate Custom Fields.history: Accentuate Custom Fields is a Shopify app that extends store product pages with over 50 custom field types including metafields and metaobjects. It offers bulk editing, import/export, image transformations, and dynamic field values. The app holds a 4.9 rating from 121+ reviews and is well regarded as a leading metafield management solution for Shopify merchants. Plans range from free to $50/month.
Accentuate Custom Fields.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. The app is listed on the official Shopify App Store and has passed Shopify's review process. No data breaches or fraudulent activity reported. Highly rated by merchants for reliability.

Acceptd.description: Acceptd is a premier application and audition management platform designed to streamline the submission, review, and selection process for organizations and educational institutions.
Acceptd.history: Acceptd was founded in 2011 by Don Hunter and Derek Brown in Columbus, Ohio, as an audition and application management platform for arts organizations. The company raised $2.82M over three rounds with backing from NCT Ventures and TechColumbus. In June 2021, Acceptd was acquired by Togetherwork Holdings, a portfolio company aggregating software for membership-based communities, where it continues to operate as a standalone product serving hundreds of arts organizations including Carnegie Hall.
Acceptd.summary_abuse: Acceptd has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche B2B SaaS platform used by arts organizations and educational institutions for managing auditions and applications. The platform requires institutional accounts, has no anonymous signup, and has no consumer-facing surface that would attract phishers or fraudsters. No documented abuse incidents or security research reports reference this platform.

Accertify.description: Accertify is a platform specialising in fraud prevention and chargeback management.
Accertify.history: Accertify was founded in 2007 and acquired by American Express in 2010. It provides enterprise fraud prevention, payment gateway, and chargeback management solutions using AI/ML models combined with configurable rules and device intelligence. Clients include StubHub, Greyhound, and JetBlue. As a subsidiary of American Express, it benefits from extensive financial data and institutional backing.
Accertify.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. Accertify is designed to prevent fraud rather than facilitate it. As a subsidiary of American Express operating in the financial services fraud prevention space, it has strong oversight. No data breaches or regulatory violations publicly documented.

AccessAlly.description: AccessAlly is a customizable platform designed for scalable growth, offering tools for membership management, online courses, and automation.
AccessAlly.history: AccessAlly is a WordPress-based LMS and membership site plugin founded around 2015. It offers unlimited members and courses for a flat annual fee of $1,290/year, with no transaction fees. It integrates with email marketing tools and CRMs and is designed for creators, coaches, and educators who want a self-hosted alternative to platforms like Kajabi.
AccessAlly.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents or data breaches. AccessAlly is a legitimate membership and LMS plugin used by course creators and educators. No regulatory violations or deceptive practices documented.

AccessiBe.description: AccessiBe is an accessibility overlay which claims to provide ADA and WCAG compliance. The system scans and analyzes a website, and applies adjustments which they claim make your website ADA and WCAG 2.1 compliant.
AccessiBe.history: AccessiBe is an Israeli web accessibility overlay company founded in 2018. It faced a $1 million FTC fine in January 2025 for deceptive claims that its AI overlay could make any website WCAG 2.1 AA compliant within 48 hours. The FTC also found it paid for fake reviews. Over 700 accessibility professionals have publicly opposed overlay-based solutions as inadequate. Auto-renew and unexpected price increase complaints were also documented.
AccessiBe.summary_abuse: In January 2025 the FTC fined AccessiBe $1 million for deceptive marketing claims, including false WCAG compliance guarantees and undisclosed paid reviews. Users report unexpected auto-renewals, price increases, and difficulty cancelling. The product has been widely criticized by the accessibility community for not fixing underlying issues.

Accessibility Toolbar Plugin.description: Accessibility Toolbar Plugin is an accessibility component without dependencies (clean javascript), including a variety of tools.
Accessibility Toolbar Plugin.history: Accessibility Toolbar Plugin is a lightweight vanilla JavaScript library created by Mikhail Sedov (mickidum) hosted on GitHub. It provides a dependency-free accessibility toolbar that users can add to websites to improve accessibility for visitors with disabilities. It supports multiple languages including English, Hebrew, Russian, and French.
Accessibility Toolbar Plugin.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. The plugin is a simple open-source accessibility utility with no commercial affiliation. No security vulnerabilities or malicious use cases documented.

Accessible360.description: Accessible360 is a web accessibility company based in Edina, Minnesota.
Accessible360.history: Accessible360 was founded in 2016 in Edina, Minnesota, as a web accessibility services company offering ADA and WCAG compliance auditing, remediation, and managed accessibility services for websites and digital properties. The company remains independently operated and focuses on legal compliance and ongoing monitoring for enterprise and mid-market clients.
Accessible360.summary_abuse: Accessible360 has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B accessibility compliance service with no anonymous signup, no user-generated content hosting, and no consumer-facing features that would attract fraudsters. The service is entirely focused on helping organizations meet legal accessibility requirements.

Accessibly.description: Accessibly is an app which is designed to assist with meeting certain requirements of WCAG 2.1 using an overlay solution.
Accessibly.history: Accessibly is a WCAG 2.1 accessibility overlay app developed by On The Map Marketing. It is an accessibility compliance widget added to websites. No notable ownership changes.
Accessibly.summary_abuse: Accessibly has no notable abuse signal. It is a web accessibility overlay widget with no content hosting features.

AccessiWay.description: AccessiWay is a solution focused on digital accessibility and compliance for organizations seeking to meet established standards.
AccessiWay.history: AccessiWay is an Italian digital accessibility compliance solution launched around 2019. No notable ownership changes documented.
AccessiWay.summary_abuse: AccessiWay has no notable abuse signal. It is a digital accessibility compliance tool with no consumer-facing content hosting features.

Accesso.description: Accesso provides ticketing, ecommerce and Point-of-Sale (PoS) solutions.
Accesso.history: Accesso is a UK-listed (London Stock Exchange) technology company founded in 2000, headquartered in Lake Mary, Florida. It provides ticketing, virtual queuing (LoQueue), POS, and guest experience management for attractions, ski resorts, theme parks, live entertainment venues, and theatres. The platform charges per ticket sold and supports enterprise-scale operations for high-volume venues.
Accesso.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents or data breaches. Accesso is a publicly listed entertainment technology company with strong institutional oversight. No regulatory violations or security incidents documented. The company holds PCI DSS compliance relevant to its ticketing operations.

AccessTrade.description: AccessTrade is an affiliate marketing platform based on the CPA model developed by Interspace Co.
AccessTrade.history: AccessTrade is a CPA-based affiliate marketing platform operated by Interspace Co., Ltd. (Japan), launched in Southeast Asian markets. It has been operating since approximately 2001 in Japan and expanded globally.
AccessTrade.summary_abuse: AccessTrade is an affiliate marketing network. Affiliate platforms can be used for fraudulent commissions, cookie stuffing, or fake referrals, though AccessTrade operates as a managed B2B network with publisher vetting. No documented specific abuse incidents.

Acconsento.click.description: Acconsento.click is a software solution designed to assist users in achieving cookie policy compliance for their websites.
Acconsento.click.history: Acconsento.click is a German cookie compliance solution helping websites meet EU cookie policy requirements. Limited public history available.
Acconsento.click.summary_abuse: Acconsento.click has no notable abuse signal. It is a cookie consent management tool with no consumer-facing content hosting.

Accredible.description: Accredible is a branded digital certificates provider that supports learner and program growth through secure, verifiable credentialing.
Accredible.history: Accredible was founded in 2013 and is the world's leading digital credential platform. It enables education and training organizations to issue verifiable digital badges and certificates with per-recipient pricing starting at $45/month. It offers white-label branding, automated email campaigns, and integration with LMS platforms. Used by major universities and certification bodies globally.
Accredible.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents or data breaches. Accredible is a well-established credential verification platform used by reputable educational institutions. No regulatory violations or fraudulent credential issuance incidents documented. SOC 2 Type 2 certified.

AccuWeather.description: AccuWeather provides weather forecasts and warnings and additional weather products and services.
AccuWeather.history: AccuWeather was founded in 1962 by Joel Myers and is headquartered in State College, Pennsylvania. It is one of the world's largest private weather companies, providing weather data APIs to developers and enterprises globally. The developer API offers plans from free to paid tiers starting at $25/month based on call volume, with enterprise data services negotiated directly.
AccuWeather.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents or security violations. AccuWeather is a well-established weather data provider with over 60 years of history. The mobile app faced criticism in 2017 for location data sharing with advertisers, but this practice was not unique to AccuWeather in the app industry. No current regulatory violations documented.

Ace.description: Ace is an embeddable code editor written in JavaScript.
Ace.history: Ace is a standalone embeddable code editor written in JavaScript, developed as the primary editor for Cloud9 IDE and successor to Mozilla Skywriter (Bespin). First released around 2010, it supports syntax highlighting for over 120 languages, over 20 themes, and can be embedded in any webpage. It is hosted on GitHub under ajaxorg and released under the BSD-3-Clause license.
Ace.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents. Ace is a widely trusted open-source code editor library used in many legitimate web IDE and developer tool applications. No known security vulnerabilities actively exploited. No malicious use cases documented.

Acecounter.description: Acecounter is a Korean analytics service that consolidates website analytics data into a single platform for streamlined access and analysis.
Acecounter.history: Acecounter is a Korean web analytics platform that has been operating since approximately 2000. It consolidates website analytics for Korean enterprises. No notable ownership changes documented.
Acecounter.summary_abuse: Acecounter has no notable abuse signal. It is a Korean analytics service with a B2B focus and no consumer-facing content hosting features.

AceShop.description: AceShop is a full-featured ecommerce component for Joomla that enables online store creation and management within the Joomla content management system.
AceShop.history: AceShop is a Joomla ecommerce extension developed by JoomAce, offering full-featured online store creation within the Joomla CMS. It requires Joomla and jQuery and is available under a freemium model. The extension targets Joomla users who need ecommerce functionality without switching platforms.
AceShop.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. AceShop is a Joomla ecommerce extension with no documented security breaches or malicious use. As a GPL-licensed CMS extension, security depends on the underlying Joomla installation and operator configuration.

Ackee.description: Ackee is a self-hosted, Node.js based analytics tool with a focus on privacy.
Ackee.history: Ackee is a self-hosted, open-source website analytics tool written in Node.js with MongoDB, created by Tobias Reich (electerious) and first released in 2019. It uses no cookies and does not collect personal data, making it inherently GDPR-compliant. It provides basic analytics including views, referrers, and visit durations, and can be deployed via Docker or cloud platforms.
Ackee.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents. Ackee is a privacy-first analytics tool explicitly designed to avoid collecting personal data. No security vulnerabilities or malicious use cases documented.

Acoustic Experience Analytics.description: Acoustic Experience Analytics (Tealeaf), formerly known as IBM Tealeaf Customer Experience on Cloud, is a SaaS-based analytics solution that delivers Tealeaf core capabilities in an managed cloud environment. Tealeaf captures and manages each visitor interaction on your website and mobile applications.
Acoustic Experience Analytics.history: Acoustic Experience Analytics, formerly IBM Tealeaf, is a digital behavior analytics platform offering session replay, heatmaps, and AI-powered anomaly detection. Originally developed by Tealeaf Technology, acquired by IBM in 2012, then spun off into Acoustic in 2019 as an independent marketing technology company. It serves enterprise clients needing deep digital experience insights.
Acoustic Experience Analytics.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents or data breaches. Acoustic is a well-established enterprise analytics vendor with IBM heritage. No regulatory violations documented. The platform collects detailed session behavioral data which is standard for enterprise digital experience tools.

Acquia Campaign Factory.description: Acquia Campaign Factory is centralized marketing management system powered by Mautic.
Acquia Campaign Factory.history: Acquia Campaign Factory is an add-on to Acquia Campaign Studio that enables orchestration of multiple Campaign Studio instances from a central platform. It allows agencies and large enterprises to deploy, manage, and track multiple marketing automation environments, push brand guidelines to downstream instances, and aggregate cross-instance analytics. It requires Campaign Studio and is part of the broader Acquia Digital Experience Platform built on Drupal.
Acquia Campaign Factory.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. Acquia is a well-established enterprise Drupal platform company with strong compliance credentials including SOC 2, HIPAA, and FedRAMP. No regulatory violations or security incidents documented.

Acquia Cloud IDE.description: Acquia Cloud IDE is a browser-based source code editor and a Drupal development stack running on the Acquia Cloud Platform.
Acquia Cloud IDE.history: Acquia Cloud IDE is a browser-based source code editor and Drupal development environment provided as part of the Acquia Cloud Platform. It allows developers to write, test, and deploy Drupal code directly in the browser without requiring local environment setup. It is part of Acquia's enterprise DXP offering and inherits Acquia's security and compliance posture.
Acquia Cloud IDE.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. Acquia Cloud IDE is a developer tooling component within the Acquia enterprise platform, used exclusively by paying enterprise customers. No security incidents or regulatory violations documented.

Acquia Cloud Platform.description: Acquia Cloud Platform is a Drupal-tuned application lifecycle management suite with an infrastructure to support Drupal deployment workflow processes.
Acquia Cloud Platform.history: Acquia Cloud Platform is a Drupal-optimized application lifecycle management suite providing 99.99% uptime SLA, FedRAMP and HIPAA compliance, and automated deployment from version-controlled code repositories. It includes CDN, Varnish, Memcache, and BigPipe caching at all layers. Founded by Dries Buytaert (Drupal creator) in 2007.
Acquia Cloud Platform.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. Acquia is a well-established enterprise Drupal platform company with strong compliance credentials including SOC 2, HIPAA, and FedRAMP. No regulatory violations or security incidents documented.

Acquia Cloud Platform CDN.history: Acquia Cloud Platform CDN is the integrated content delivery network component of Acquia Cloud Platform, providing Drupal-optimized edge caching and delivery for enterprise Drupal sites. It is built on top of Acquia's Drupal-specific infrastructure and inherits the platform's SOC 2, HIPAA, and FedRAMP compliance posture.
Acquia Cloud Platform CDN.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. Acquia Cloud Platform CDN is an enterprise infrastructure service used exclusively by paying Acquia Cloud Platform customers. No security incidents or regulatory violations documented.

Acquia Cloud Site Factory.description: Acquia Site Factory is a multisite platform for Drupal.
Acquia Cloud Site Factory.history: Acquia Site Factory is a multisite management platform for Drupal that enables enterprises to deploy and manage hundreds or thousands of Drupal sites from a central dashboard. It is part of Acquia's enterprise DXP and is used by large organizations with complex multi-site needs such as universities and media companies.
Acquia Cloud Site Factory.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. Acquia is a well-established enterprise Drupal platform company with strong compliance credentials including SOC 2, HIPAA, and FedRAMP. No regulatory violations or security incidents documented.

Acquia Content Hub.description: Acquia Content Hub is a cloud-based, centralized content distribution and syndication service.
Acquia Content Hub.history: Acquia Content Hub is a cloud-based centralized content distribution and syndication service within the Acquia platform. It allows organizations to share and syndicate content across multiple Drupal websites and digital properties from a centralized repository, enabling enterprise-scale content operations.
Acquia Content Hub.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. Acquia is a well-established enterprise Drupal platform company with strong compliance credentials including SOC 2, HIPAA, and FedRAMP. No regulatory violations or security incidents documented.

Acquia Customer Data Platform.description: Acquia Customer Data Platform (formerly AgilOne) is a customer data platform for Drupal.
Acquia Customer Data Platform.history: Acquia Customer Data Platform, formerly AgilOne, is an enterprise CDP that provides unified customer profiles and AI-powered analytics. Acquired by Acquia in 2019, it integrates with Drupal and the Acquia DXP to enable personalization and cross-channel marketing. It helps organizations consolidate customer data from multiple touchpoints.
Acquia Customer Data Platform.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. Acquia is a well-established enterprise Drupal platform company with strong compliance credentials including SOC 2, HIPAA, and FedRAMP. No regulatory violations or security incidents documented.

Acquia Personalization.description: Acquia Personalization (formerly Acquia Lift) lets you track customers' behavior throughout your website.
Acquia Personalization.history: Acquia Personalization, formerly Acquia Lift, is a behavioral personalization engine within the Acquia DXP. It tracks visitor behavior across digital properties to enable content personalization and A/B testing in Drupal-based sites. Part of Acquia's enterprise offering for organizations needing sophisticated content targeting.
Acquia Personalization.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. Acquia is a well-established enterprise Drupal platform company with strong compliance credentials including SOC 2, HIPAA, and FedRAMP. No regulatory violations or security incidents documented.

Acquia Site Studio.description: Site Studio (formerly Cohesion) is a low-code, Drupal add-on page builder.
Acquia Site Studio.history: Acquia Site Studio, formerly Cohesion, is a low-code drag-and-drop page builder add-on for Drupal websites. It allows non-technical users to create and manage Drupal site layouts without writing code, using a visual component-based interface. Acquired by Acquia and integrated into its enterprise DXP product line.
Acquia Site Studio.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents. Acquia is a well-established enterprise Drupal platform company with strong compliance credentials including SOC 2, HIPAA, and FedRAMP. No regulatory violations or security incidents documented.

Acquire Cobrowse.description: Acquire Cobrowse is a safe and secure method of interacting with a customer's browser without downloading any additional software.
Acquire Cobrowse.history: Acquire Cobrowse is the co-browsing module of the Acquire.io customer support platform. Acquire.io was founded around 2016 and offers multi-channel customer support including live chat, video, and co-browsing features.
Acquire Cobrowse.summary_abuse: Acquire Cobrowse has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B customer support co-browsing tool with no consumer-facing content hosting that would attract fraudsters.

Acquire Live Chat.description: Acquire is a multi-channel customer support platform designed to provide real-time customer support to customers.
Acquire Live Chat.history: Acquire Live Chat is the live chat module of the Acquire.io multi-channel customer support platform, founded around 2016.
Acquire Live Chat.summary_abuse: Acquire Live Chat has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B customer support live chat tool with no consumer-facing content hosting.

Act-On.description: Act-On is a cloud-based SaaS product for marketing automation.
Act-On.history: Founded in 2008 by Raghu Raghavan in Portland, Oregon. Initially sold exclusively through Cisco. Raised over $70M in VC funding from Norwest Venture Partners, US Venture Partners, and others. Acquired by Boston Capital Ventures for $53 million in February 2024. Kate Johnson has been CEO since 2018.
Act-On.summary_abuse: Act-On has minimal abuse signal. As a B2B marketing automation SaaS with a starting price of $900/month and no free tier, it presents high barriers to anonymous abuse. Act-On publishes an Acceptable Use Policy and is a member of the Email Sender and Provider Coalition (ESPC) and the Direct Marketing Association, indicating active engagement with anti-spam practices. The platform holds ISO 27001 certification and HIPAA compliance, reflecting formal security and compliance programs. No disproportionate abuse patterns have been documented.

ActBlue.description: ActBlue is an online fundraising platform that facilitates secure donations to Democratic candidates and progressive causes, streamlining the process of processing and distributing campaign contributions.
ActBlue.history: ActBlue was founded in 2004 as a nonprofit tech organization to facilitate online fundraising for Democratic candidates and progressive causes in the United States. It has grown into the dominant small-dollar fundraising platform for the American left, processing billions of dollars in donations per election cycle.
ActBlue.summary_abuse: ActBlue is a political fundraising platform. It has been the target of donation fraud schemes where stolen credit cards are used to make small test donations. ActBlue has deployed fraud detection systems and cooperated with law enforcement on such cases. The platform itself is legitimate; the abuse pattern involves external bad actors misusing its payment processing surface rather than ActBlue enabling fraud.

actionhero.js.history: Actionhero.js was created by Evan Tahler around 2012 as a multi-transport Node.js API server framework. It has remained an independent open-source project maintained primarily by its original creator with no notable acquisitions or forks.
actionhero.js.summary_abuse: Actionhero.js is a backend Node.js framework with no notable abuse signal. It is a server-side tool with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Actirise.description: Actirise is a monetization and business intelligence platform.
Actirise.history: Actirise is an AI-driven publisher ad monetization platform that originated as part of Bricks Founders, a French AdTech startup studio. The parent entity rebranded to Sparteo, positioning itself as an AdTech suite for publishers. Actirise focuses on display monetization and audience analytics for web publishers.
Actirise.summary_abuse: Actirise is a publisher ad monetization platform embedded on websites to serve display ads. As an ad network, it can serve low-quality or deceptive ad creatives from third-party demand partners if moderation is insufficient. Abuse is primarily indirect via ad creatives rather than the platform being directly exploited for phishing or fraud.

Actito.description: Actito is an agile SaaS marketing automation platform.
Actito.history: Actito was founded in 2000 in Louvain-la-Neuve, Belgium by Benoit De Nayer, Kenya Rose, and Pierre De Nayer. The company provides a marketing automation and CRM platform primarily serving mid-market businesses in the Benelux and French-speaking markets. It has remained independent and Belgium-headquartered throughout its history.
Actito.summary_abuse: Actito is a B2B marketing automation platform primarily used for email marketing and customer engagement by enterprises. As an email delivery platform it could be misused for spam if account controls are insufficient, but Actito enforces permission-based marketing policies. No significant abuse incidents have been publicly reported.

Activ8 Commerce.description: Activ8 Commerce is a sales solution tailored for winery and distillery businesses.
Activ8 Commerce.history: Founded in 2003 as Active Club Solutions, Inc. by Arne Haugland, initially focused on wine club management software. Rebranded as Activ8 Commerce in 2019 to reflect expansion into a full end-to-end POS, ecommerce, club management, fulfillment, and CRM platform for wineries, distilleries, and breweries. The company is headquartered in Pleasanton, California and is privately held. No acquisitions or ownership changes have occurred. The company added Shopify integration in 2023-2025.
Activ8 Commerce.summary_abuse: Activ8 Commerce is a B2B sales platform (POS, ecommerce, club management) specifically for wineries, distilleries, and breweries. It operates as a closed, invite-only business system for verified alcohol beverage retailers. The platform does not offer anonymous signup, custom-domain content hosting, or mass-email features accessible to the general public. As a specialized industry SaaS with business verification required, its abuse surface for individual bad actors is negligible. The platform is PCI DSS compliant and uses secure cloud infrastructure. No notable public abuse incidents or security reports were found.

ActiveCampaign.description: ActiveCampaign is email and marketing automation software.
ActiveCampaign.history: ActiveCampaign was founded on March 1, 2003 by Jason VandeBoom in Chicago as an on-premises email marketing tool. It was bootstrapped for its first 13 years before receiving a $20 million funding round in 2016. The company transitioned to SaaS and reached unicorn status in 2021. In 2022 it acquired Postmark, a transactional email service, and in 2023 acquired Onesend for multi-location marketing.
ActiveCampaign.summary_abuse: ActiveCampaign is a major email marketing and CRM platform serving over 180,000 customers. As a bulk email platform it is occasionally misused for spam or phishing campaigns, but ActiveCampaign enforces anti-spam policies, requires permission-based lists, and responds to abuse complaints. Abuse is a small fraction of overall use.

ActivEngage.description: ActivEngage is an automotive chat service that enables real-time website messaging between dealerships and customers.
ActivEngage.history: ActivEngage was founded in 2007 by Todd Smith and Ted Rubin as the first managed live chat solution specifically for automotive dealerships. The company pioneered real-time messaging between car buyers and dealerships and expanded to include omnichannel communication features. In 2022, Cox Automotive announced an exclusive partnership with ActivEngage to integrate its messaging capabilities into the Cox Automotive ecosystem, though ActivEngage retained its independent corporate structure.
ActivEngage.summary_abuse: ActivEngage has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B managed live chat service exclusively for automotive dealerships, with all client accounts vetted as legitimate businesses. The platform handles real-time consumer inquiries but requires dealer accounts with no anonymous or public-facing signup, limiting its exposure to fraud or phishing abuse.

Acuity Scheduling.description: Acuity Scheduling is a cloud-based appointment scheduling software solution.
Acuity Scheduling.history: Acuity Scheduling was founded in 2006 and grew for 13 years as a standalone online appointment booking platform. In April 2019 it was acquired by Squarespace in that company's first-ever acquisition. Squarespace initially rebranded the product for new customers as Squarespace Scheduling before eventually consolidating back under the Acuity name.
Acuity Scheduling.summary_abuse: Acuity Scheduling is an online appointment booking platform with no significant abuse history. Its customer base consists of legitimate service businesses. Now owned by Squarespace, a public company, its security and compliance posture is supported by substantial corporate infrastructure.

AcuityAds.description: AcuityAds offers automatic solutions to marketers willing to connect through clients across mobile, social, and online display advertising campaigns.
AcuityAds.history: AcuityAds is a Canadian programmatic advertising platform founded in 2009. It is publicly traded and serves enterprise marketers across mobile, social, and display advertising. No major acquisitions documented.
AcuityAds.summary_abuse: AcuityAds is an ad tech platform. Programmatic advertising infrastructure can be exploited for malvertising and fraudulent ad impressions, but AcuityAds is a legitimate publicly-traded B2B company with standard industry fraud controls. No documented specific abuse incidents.

AD EBiS.description: AD EBiS is an advertising and marketing platform that offers advertisement effectiveness measurement, access and user analysis.
AD EBiS.history: AD EBiS is a Japanese ad measurement and marketing analytics platform launched in 2004, operated by YRGLM Inc. It holds the highest adoption rate among Japanese ad measurement tools, with over 10,000 implementations across finance, HR, ecommerce, and B2B industries. The platform integrates with Salesforce, Marketo, and major Japanese ad platforms.
AD EBiS.summary_abuse: AD EBiS is a Japanese marketing analytics platform used by enterprise companies. The platform tracks ad effectiveness and conversion paths and collects behavioral data. As a Japanese service, it operates under Japanese privacy law rather than GDPR. No specific documented abuse incidents.

Ad Lightning.description: Ad Lightning is an programmatic ads monitoring and audit service.
Ad Lightning.history: Ad Lightning was founded in 2016 by Scott Moore, Kate Reinmiller, and Drake Callahan in Kirkland, Washington, spinning out of Pioneer Square Labs. The company focused on ad quality monitoring and blocking for publishers and ad exchanges. It later rebranded to Boltive to reflect its expanded focus on privacy and compliance solutions.
Ad Lightning.summary_abuse: Ad Lightning (now Boltive) is an ad quality and privacy protection platform used by publishers to detect and block malicious or low-quality ads. It operates on the defensive side of ad security rather than being a vector for abuse.

Ada.description: Ada is an automated customer experience company that provides chat bots used in customer support.
Ada.history: Ada was founded in 2016 by David Hariri and Mike Murchison in Toronto, Canada after the pair spent a year working as customer service agents to understand the industry firsthand. The company has powered over 4 billion customer interactions for enterprise clients including Meta, Verizon, and Shopify. Ada raised $130 million in 2021 achieving unicorn status.
Ada.summary_abuse: Ada is an enterprise AI customer service platform with strong security controls including SOC 2 Type 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, and HIPAA compliance. It serves large enterprises with verified identities and has no documented history of being misused for fraud, phishing, or spam. Its trust and safety infrastructure is mature for its segment.

Adabra.description: Adabra is a SaaS omnichannel marketing automation platform to help boost sales. Adabra allows you to manage user segmentation, create workflow and campaigns through email, social, SMS and more.
Adabra.history: Adabra was founded in 2011 in Arezzo, Italy by Lucia Milighetti and Gian Mario Infelici as an ecommerce personalization and omnichannel marketing platform. The company raised $1.15 million in seed funding in 2016. It has since evolved and rebranded to Blendee, positioning itself as a Customer Data Platform.
Adabra.summary_abuse: Adabra is an Italian marketing automation and ecommerce personalization platform with no notable abuse history. Its B2B focus and European regulatory environment reduce risk of misuse for fraud.

Adally.description: Adally provides real-time website accessibility solutions, including free accessibility scans and widgets, to help websites comply with ADA, WCAG 2.1, and Section 508 standards.
Adally.history: Adally is a website accessibility compliance platform that provides real-time accessibility scans and widgets to help websites comply with ADA, WCAG 2.1, and Section 508 standards. It offers a freemium model including a free accessibility scan tool.
Adally.summary_abuse: Adally is a website accessibility compliance tool with no documented abuse history. Its purpose is to help websites become more accessible, and it has no consumer-facing surface that would make it a meaningful target for fraud or phishing.

Adalo.description: Adalo is a no-code platform enabling the creation of mobile and web applications.
Adalo.history: Adalo was founded in 2018 by David Adkin and Ben Haefele in St. Louis, Missouri as a no-code app builder enabling drag-and-drop creation of native mobile and web apps. The company raised $3.2 million in seed funding in 2020 led by Tiger Global, followed by an $8 million Series A. In 2021 it acquired No-Code Conf.
Adalo.summary_abuse: Adalo is a no-code app platform that enables users to build mobile and web apps without coding. Like all no-code platforms, it could theoretically be used to build scam or phishing apps, but it requires a paid account, has terms of service prohibiting abuse, and is primarily used by entrepreneurs and small businesses for legitimate apps.

Adalte.description: Adalte is an online solution designed for travel agencies, tour operators, and DMCs, offering travel management capabilities.
Adalte.history: Adalte is an Italian travel technology company founded around 2015 that provides a B2B marketplace and inventory management platform for tour operators, incoming agencies, and destination management companies. The platform enables online booking and XML connectivity for travel product distribution.
Adalte.summary_abuse: Adalte is a niche B2B travel technology platform used by professional travel operators. It has no documented abuse history and its verified-business model limits exposure to consumer-facing fraud.

Adalyser.description: Adalyser is an online platform offering the tools needed to get up and running with TV advertising.
Adalyser.history: Adalyser was founded in 2012 by David Cloudsdale in Manchester, UK as a TV advertising analytics and attribution platform. The company helps media agencies and advertisers measure the impact of TV advertising on digital response metrics, and has expanded to cover broadcast VOD and connected TV measurement.
Adalyser.summary_abuse: Adalyser is a TV advertising measurement platform used by UK media agencies and advertisers. It has no documented abuse history and operates in a B2B data analytics capacity with no consumer-facing surface that could be exploited for fraud.

ADAPT.description: ADAPT is a subscription-based app that allows anyone to create video focused online store in minutes on their phone.
ADAPT.history: ADAPT (adapt.ws) is a subscription mobile e-commerce app builder enabling merchants to create video-focused online stores from their phones. Limited public history.
ADAPT.summary_abuse: ADAPT has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche mobile store builder with no documented fraud patterns.

Adaptix.description: Adaptix is a marketing automation platform that uses AI to help businesses create personalized customer experiences.
Adaptix.history: Adaptix is an AI-driven marketing automation platform that combines email marketing, SMS, landing pages, automation flows, and analytics in one platform. The company targets small to midsize businesses with AI-assisted personalization features. Specific founding details are limited in public records.
Adaptix.summary_abuse: Adaptix is a marketing automation and email platform. As an email sending tool, it could theoretically be misused for spam campaigns. No notable abuse incidents have been publicly documented.

Adara.description: Adara is a tourism advertising system.
Adara.history: Adara was founded around 2009 in Palo Alto, California by Charles Mi as a travel data cooperative that aggregates anonymized travel intent and purchase data from airlines, hotels, and booking platforms. In January 2023, Adara was acquired by RateGain Travel Technologies for $16.1 million.
Adara.summary_abuse: Adara is a travel data platform that aggregates anonymized behavioral data from travel companies for advertising targeting. It is classified as a data broker in the advertising sense, collecting and commercializing travel intent signals. This places it at moderate privacy risk, though it is not known to be misused for phishing or fraud.

AdaSiteCompliance.description: AdaSiteCompliance is a web accessibility solution, making websites compliant and accessible to WCAG 2.1 and section 508 compliance standards.
AdaSiteCompliance.history: ADA Site Compliance was founded by Scott Trachtenberg as a digital accessibility consulting and compliance firm focused on ADA and WCAG standards for websites. In September 2024, the company was acquired by AudioEye, a publicly traded digital accessibility technology company.
AdaSiteCompliance.summary_abuse: ADA Site Compliance is a legitimate website accessibility consulting service. It is not known to be used for fraud or phishing. The company focuses on helping organizations become legally compliant with disability access laws.

AdBridg.description: AdBridg is a bidding solutions provider for publishers looking to maximize their programmatic revenues.
AdBridg.history: AdBridg was founded in 2014 in San Francisco by former publisher ad operations executives. The company was built to help large digital publishers manage their ad stack and header bidding configurations, drawing on years of hands-on publisher-side ad ops experience.
AdBridg.summary_abuse: AdBridg is a publisher ad stack management platform used by large media companies to optimize programmatic advertising. It is not known to be misused for phishing or fraud. Its B2B publisher focus significantly limits consumer-facing abuse surface.

AdCalls.description: AdCalls is a call tracking software designed to monitor and analyze inbound and outbound calls for performance and attribution.
AdCalls.history: AdCalls was founded in 2013 by Tamer Saglambilek and Jeffrey Verhoek in Amsterdam, Netherlands as a call tracking platform for measuring how online marketing campaigns drive phone calls. The company serves as a market leader in the Netherlands for call tracking and analytics.
AdCalls.summary_abuse: AdCalls is a call tracking SaaS used by marketers to measure phone call conversions from digital advertising. No notable abuse history is documented. Its B2B nature and phone call analytics focus make consumer-facing abuse unlikely.

adCAPTCHA.description: adCAPTCHA is a security tool that integrates marketing and user experience to block bots while enhancing brand visibility, using dynamic content for varied challenges without collecting user data.
adCAPTCHA.history: adCAPTCHA was founded in 2024 by Alfie Scarborough in London, UK, launching with £1 million in angel funding. It differentiates itself from traditional CAPTCHAs by using dynamic advertising content as the verification challenge, claiming not to collect user data.
adCAPTCHA.summary_abuse: adCAPTCHA is a CAPTCHA and bot-protection service with no notable abuse signal. It is designed to prevent automated bot activity and has no consumer-facing content-hosting features that would attract fraudsters.

Adcash.history: Adcash was founded in 2007 by Christophe Avignon and Thomas Padovani in Tallinn, Estonia. The company was started without direct funding and formally incorporated as Adcash OÜ in 2011. It grew quickly, reaching €25 million in revenue by 2013 and winning Estonian business awards in 2015. It now reaches over 280 million unique users daily across 195 countries.
Adcash.summary_abuse: Adcash is a global ad network serving over 280 million users daily with pop-under, push, and native ads. The platform has documented history of delivering malware and scam ad creatives, particularly through its popunder and push notification formats, which are commonly exploited by bad actors. Security researchers have flagged Adcash as a conduit for malvertising campaigns. The network operates in grey-area ad formats that attract a higher-than-average share of fraud-adjacent traffic.

AddEvent.description: AddEvent is used to Add to Calendar and event tools for websites and newsletters.
AddEvent.history: AddEvent was founded in 2015 by Michael Nilsson in San Mateo, California. The company built the first widely-adopted add-to-calendar button for websites and has grown to become the leading calendar marketing tool. The company is bootstrapped with about $2 million in annual revenue as of 2025.
AddEvent.summary_abuse: AddEvent is a calendar marketing and event management platform with no documented abuse history. It is used by businesses to reduce no-shows at events. Its functionality is narrow and does not expose significant surface area for fraud or phishing.

Addi.description: Addi is a service that allows users to make purchases and pay for them in installments over time.
Addi.history: Addi was founded in 2018 in Bogota, Colombia by Santiago Suarez, Daniel Vallejo, and Elmer Ortega to bring buy now, pay later (BNPL) credit access to Latin America. The company raised over $788 million from investors including Goldman Sachs, BBVA Spark, Union Square Ventures, and Fasanara Capital. It operates in Colombia and Brazil.
Addi.summary_abuse: Addi is a LatAm BNPL fintech company subject to regulation in Colombia and Brazil. Like all consumer lending platforms, it carries some risk of fraud attempts such as synthetic identity fraud, but the company employs credit risk controls and identity verification. No major public fraud incidents have been specifically attributed to the platform.

Addsearch.description: Addsearch is a site search solution for small and large websites.
Addsearch.history: AddSearch was founded in 2013 in Helsinki, Finland by Antti Ala-Ilkka, Mikko Nurminen, and Pasi Ilola. The company built a hosted site search solution positioned as a replacement for Google Site Search. In June 2023, AddSearch was acquired by SaaS.Group, a portfolio of bootstrapped B2B SaaS companies.
Addsearch.summary_abuse: AddSearch is a site search platform for websites and applications with no documented abuse history. Its B2B nature and narrow use case make it an extremely low-risk platform for fraud or misuse.

AddShoppers.description: AddShoppers is the social media marketing command center for small-medium online retailers.
AddShoppers.history: AddShoppers was founded in 2011 by Jon West and is headquartered in New York. The company is a social commerce platform for online merchants, and has raised $3.64 million in funding. It acquired ShareYourCart and Sociaby to expand its social sharing and referral marketing capabilities.
AddShoppers.summary_abuse: AddShoppers is a social sharing and referral marketing platform for ecommerce stores. It tracks customer behavior across websites for referral attribution. No major abuse incidents have been documented. The tracking nature of the platform raises moderate privacy concerns.

AddThis.description: AddThis is a social bookmarking service that can be integrated into a website with the use of a web widget.
AddThis.history: AddThis was founded in 2004 by Hooman Radfar, Austin Fath, and Dominique Vonarburg. In 2008 it was acquired by Clearspring Technologies, which later renamed itself AddThis in 2012. Oracle acquired AddThis in January 2016 for $200 million primarily to use its 1.9 billion monthly unique user tracking for Oracle's Data Cloud ID graph. Oracle shut down all AddThis services on May 31, 2023.
AddThis.summary_abuse: AddThis was a widely-embedded social sharing and audience tracking tool that reached 1.9 billion unique visitors monthly across 15 million websites. Privacy researchers documented that it performed cross-site tracking even when users had not interacted with sharing buttons. After Oracle acquired it in 2016 for audience data, it was eventually shut down in May 2023. Its tracking practices were subject to scrutiny from privacy regulators.

AddToAny.description: AddToAny is a universal sharing platform that can be integrated into a website by use of a web widget or plugin.
AddToAny.history: AddToAny was launched in 2006 by Pat Diven II as the first universal social sharing platform, pioneering the share button concept. The company was bootstrapped and never took outside financing. In July 2011 it was acquired by Lockerz, but was reacquired by the original founder Pat Diven II in June 2013. It continues to operate as an independent bootstrapped company.
AddToAny.summary_abuse: AddToAny is a widely deployed social sharing widget that monetizes through selling anonymous aggregate sharing data to advertisers. It performs some cross-site tracking to enable its analytics. It is not known to be a major abuse vector for phishing or fraud, though its data collection model raises privacy concerns.

AddToAny Share Buttons.description: AddToAny Share Buttons plugin for WordPress increases traffic and engagement by helping people share your posts and pages to any service.
AddToAny Share Buttons.history: AddToAny Share Buttons is the WordPress plugin version of the AddToAny universal sharing platform, first launched in 2006 by Pat Diven II. The plugin version has been widely deployed across WordPress sites, making it one of the most installed sharing plugins in the WordPress ecosystem.
AddToAny Share Buttons.summary_abuse: AddToAny Share Buttons is a WordPress plugin that adds social sharing buttons and performs cross-site behavioral analytics. It monetizes through anonymous aggregate data. No notable abuse or fraud incidents are documented, though its tracking capabilities raise moderate privacy concerns.

Adenzo.description: Adenzo is a B2B sales and marketing platform for managing customer relationships, automating outreach, and analyzing performance to support lead generation and sales operations.
Adenzo.history: Adenzo was incorporated in San Francisco in 2013 as a B2B sales and marketing platform focused on lead generation and outbound sales automation. The company uses AI-powered web crawling to maintain a database of over 25 million companies for B2B targeting.
Adenzo.summary_abuse: Adenzo is a B2B sales and marketing platform with no notable abuse history. Its focus on B2B lead generation for professional sales teams limits consumer-facing fraud risk.

Adevole.description: Adevole is an ecommerce website builder for creating and managing online stores.
Adevole.history: Adevole is an ecommerce website builder founded around 2014 in Mumbai, India. The company provides tools for creating and managing online stores, likely built on existing ecommerce frameworks.
Adevole.summary_abuse: Adevole is a small ecommerce website builder with no documented abuse history. Its niche and small scale make it a low-risk platform.

Adex.description: Adex is a data management platform (DMP) system utilized for organizing and analyzing large volumes of data to optimize targeted advertising campaigns.
Adex.history: The ADEX was founded in 2013 in Berlin, Germany by Dino Bongartz as a data management platform (DMP) serving advertisers, agencies, and publishers. The company raised $3.07 million in a Series A round in 2015 from Capnamic Ventures. It focuses on EU-hosted data processing to serve European privacy-conscious customers.
Adex.summary_abuse: The ADEX is a data management platform used by advertisers to organize and target audiences for digital campaigns. As a DMP, it aggregates and commercializes behavioral audience data, which is a privacy-relevant operation. No major fraud or phishing incidents have been attributed to the platform.

AdFixus.description: AdFixus is a privacy-focused solution that enables businesses to manage their data and empower individuals with consent control, addressing the decline of third-party cookies.
AdFixus.history: AdFixus was founded in 2014 by Marko Markovic in Lindfield, Australia. The company developed a patented Anonymous Federated Identity system that enables publishers and advertisers to connect audiences without storing or commercializing personal data, positioning itself as a privacy-first identity resolution alternative to cookies.
AdFixus.summary_abuse: AdFixus is a privacy-first identity resolution platform that specifically avoids storing personal data. Its privacy-centric design limits abuse potential. No notable incidents of misuse have been documented.

Adform.description: Adform is an all-in-one platform for digital advertising.
Adform.history: Adform was founded in 2002 in Copenhagen, Denmark by Gustav Mellentin, Jakob Toftgaard Behind, and Stefan Juricic. It became one of the largest independent advertising technology companies in Europe, building a full-stack DSP/DMP/ad server platform. The company expanded internationally starting in 2005 and now operates with around 900 employees across 30+ offices. Danica Pension made a 150 million DKK investment in 2015.
Adform.summary_abuse: Adform is a major European enterprise ad tech platform used by agencies and brands for programmatic advertising. It operates with enterprise contracts and compliance requirements typical of large ad tech companies. No specific major abuse incidents have been publicly attributed to the platform itself, though programmatic advertising inherently carries ad fraud exposure.

ADFOX.description: ADFOX is an advertising management platform for media publishers.
ADFOX.history: ADFOX was founded in 2005 as an independent Russian ad-tech company providing services for planning, managing, and analyzing online advertising campaigns. In September 2014, Yandex acquired ADFOX, integrating its ad management capabilities into Yandex's advertising technology portfolio. The ADFOX team joined Yandex's advertising technologies group. Being part of Yandex, ADFOX's status has been impacted by Western sanctions following Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine.
ADFOX.summary_abuse: ADFOX is a Yandex-owned Russian ad management platform serving publishers and advertisers. As part of Yandex, it operates under Russian data law and is subject to sanctions restrictions in Western markets. The platform collects extensive behavioral tracking data. Being Russian-hosted and operating under Russian jurisdiction raises data sovereignty concerns for non-Russian businesses. No specific documented abuse incidents beyond standard ad-tech tracking.

AdGlare.description: AdGlare is an ad serving solution designed for advertisers, publishers, and agencies.
AdGlare.history: AdGlare was founded in 2013 in Eindhoven, Netherlands as a self-hosted and SaaS ad server platform for publishers and advertisers. The company has remained independent with no external funding, offering ad serving for display, native, and video formats.
AdGlare.summary_abuse: AdGlare is an ad server platform used by publishers and advertisers to serve display and video ads. As an ad serving platform, it can potentially be used to deliver malicious ad creatives if clients have poor content moderation. No specific public abuse incidents have been documented for AdGlare specifically.

Adimo.description: Adimo is a platform that integrates content with commerce, enabling CPG brands to create a seamless ecommerce experience.
Adimo.history: Adimo was founded in 2012 in Glasgow, UK by Colin Brown and Richard Kelly to make marketing shoppable for consumer packaged goods (CPG) brands. The company raised £1 million in 2017 and £1.54 million from Maven in 2021. It has expanded to offices in London, Singapore, Dubai, and New York, serving major brands like Nestlé and PepsiCo.
Adimo.summary_abuse: Adimo is a shoppable marketing platform for CPG brands used by major multinationals. It is not known to be misused for fraud. Its B2B enterprise focus and limited consumer surface area keep abuse risk low.

AdInfinity.history: AdInfinity is an Australian publisher advertising technology platform built by Ashraf Montaser and Kartik Rao, both veterans of large Australian online publishers including News.com.au and Ninemsn. The company developed ViziAds, a viewability-based ad serving product, and partnered with Adstream to expand globally. The platform appears to have limited recent activity.
AdInfinity.summary_abuse: AdInfinity is a niche Australian publisher ad serving platform with no notable abuse history. Its small scale and B2B publisher focus limit exposure to consumer-facing fraud or misuse.

Adition.description: Adition is a provider of programmatic advertising solutions.
Adition.history: Adition technologies AG was formally founded in 2004 in Germany, building on ad serving technology developed since 2001. The company grew to become a significant European ad server and programmatic platform. It is part of the Virtual Minds group, a collection of European ad tech companies.
Adition.summary_abuse: Adition is a German enterprise ad serving platform used by publishers and agencies primarily in Europe. Its enterprise customer base and European regulatory compliance focus reduce abuse risk. No major fraud incidents have been attributed to the platform.

Adjust.description: Adjust is the mobile marketing analytics platform.
Adjust.history: Adjust was founded in 2012 in Berlin, Germany as a mobile app measurement and attribution platform. The company became one of the leading mobile measurement partners globally. In 2021, Adjust was acquired by AppLovin, a publicly traded mobile technology company (NASDAQ: APP).
Adjust.summary_abuse: Adjust is a leading mobile attribution platform used by app marketers to measure campaign performance. It handles significant user behavioral data for advertising attribution. Adjust maintains SOC 2 Type 2, ISO 27001, and GDPR compliance. No notable misuse for fraud or phishing has been documented.

Adline.description: Adline is an advertising & analytics software that helps launch multichannel ads with automatic ad optimization.
Adline.history: Adline was founded in 2019 in Oslo, Norway by Dag Honningsvåg, Jo Hansæl, and Carl-Wilhelm Vedvik on the foundation of a previous company BylineMe. The company provides an AI-based advertising campaign management platform for managing ads across Facebook, Google, YouTube, and Instagram, targeted at small business marketers.
Adline.summary_abuse: Adline is a small Norwegian advertising automation platform for SMBs. It has no documented abuse history and operates as a legitimate marketing tool.

Adloox.description: Adloox is a European-born buy-side ad verification and insights company.
Adloox.history: Adloox was founded in 2009 in Paris, France by Anthony Dufoi, Romain Bellion, and Bruno Galeppe as an ad verification and fraud detection platform. The company became the first European business to receive MRC accreditation for advanced mobile and video ad fraud detection. In November 2024, Adloox was acquired by Scope3, a carbon measurement company focused on sustainable advertising.
Adloox.summary_abuse: Adloox is an ad fraud detection and verification platform that helps advertisers detect and prevent fraudulent ad impressions. It operates on the defensive side of digital advertising security and has no documented history of being misused for fraud or phishing.

AdminBuy.description: AdminBuy is an open-source content management system from China designed for building and managing websites.
AdminBuy.history: AdminBuy is a Chinese open-source CMS for building and managing websites. It originates from China and is primarily known within the Chinese web development community. Specific founding details are limited in public English-language records.
AdminBuy.summary_abuse: AdminBuy is a Chinese open-source CMS. Chinese CMS platforms with minimal international oversight can sometimes be used to host phishing pages or malicious content, particularly if deployed by low-sophistication actors. However, no specific documented incidents have been attributed to AdminBuy specifically.

Adminer.description: Adminer is a lightweight, web-based database management tool that supports multiple database systems and provides a simple interface for managing databases, tables, and running SQL queries.
Adminer.history: Adminer (originally phpMinAdmin) was created by Jakub Vrána in July 2007 as a lightweight single-file PHP alternative to phpMyAdmin. It gained attention at SourceForge in 2008 and has been continuously maintained as an open-source project. The tool supports MySQL, MariaDB, PostgreSQL, SQLite, MS SQL, Oracle, Elasticsearch and MongoDB.
Adminer.summary_abuse: Adminer is a legitimate open-source database management tool that has no notable abuse as a service. However, exposed Adminer instances without authentication have been exploited by attackers to access underlying databases in server compromise scenarios. This is a self-hosting risk rather than a platform abuse issue.

Admiral.description: Admiral is a Visitor Relationship Management (VRM) platform.
Admiral.history: Admiral was founded in 2015 in Gainesville, Florida by Dan Rua, James Gray, Tyler Deitz, and Chris Jones, spinning out of Grooveshark to address publisher revenue loss from ad blockers. The company expanded from adblock recovery to a full visitor relationship management platform covering consent management, subscriptions, and email capture. It has raised $28 million total, including a $19 million round in 2024.
Admiral.summary_abuse: Admiral is a publisher monetization platform that includes adblock bypass and consent management features. Adblock recovery technology is sometimes associated with intrusive practices. Admiral claims to use permission-based approaches and is a recognized IAB CMP vendor. No major abuse incidents are documented.

Admitad.description: Admitad is an affiliate network that acts as an intermediary between advertisers and publishers.
Admitad.history: Admitad was founded in 2009 by Alexander Bachmann in Germany and officially launched in 2010 with its first office in Munich and a development office in Minsk. The company expanded to Russia, Turkey, India and other markets, eventually growing to over 900 employees in nine countries. In 2021, Admitad acquired Tapfiliate to expand its affiliate and referral marketing capabilities.
Admitad.summary_abuse: Admitad is a large global affiliate marketing network connecting advertisers and publishers. Affiliate networks have a documented history of abuse including cookie stuffing, fake conversions, and promotion of scam or counterfeit products by rogue affiliates. Admitad has anti-fraud measures in place but affiliate networks inherently attract some bad actors. The platform has moderate abuse risk due to the nature of affiliate marketing.

Admixer.description: Admixer is an independent adtech company developing an ecosystem of full-stack programmatic solutions.
Admixer.history: Admixer was founded in 2008 in Kyiv, Ukraine by Alexey Boltivets. The company built what it claims to be the largest supply-side platform in Eastern Europe. It operates across 100+ supply and demand partners with 200+ employees in offices across Ukraine, Kazakhstan, Russia, Belarus, Georgia, Latvia, and Moldova.
Admixer.summary_abuse: Admixer is an Eastern European ad tech platform and SSP. Like all programmatic platforms, it carries standard ad fraud and malvertising risk associated with the programmatic ecosystem. Operating in Eastern Europe with less stringent oversight compared to Western markets carries some elevated risk. No specific major incidents have been attributed to the company.

Admo.tv.description: Admo.tv is a company developing a TV and radio analytics platform.
Admo.tv.history: Admo.tv was founded in 2014 in Paris, France by Baptiste Brunin, Pierre Figeat and Nicolas De Busschere. The company launched in January 2015 and provides TV attribution analytics that links television advertising exposure to online consumer behavior. It raised €300K seed funding in 2014, €1M Series A in 2016, and €6M Series B in 2019 led by Runa Capital. Major clients include BNP Paribas, Accor, Meetic, and Peugeot. The platform partners with data providers like Samba TV to track TV viewership and correlate it with digital actions.
Admo.tv.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Admo.tv is a legitimate B2B TV analytics platform focused on advertising attribution. It collects behavioral data to link TV ad exposure to web/app activity, which involves some privacy considerations but operates within normal analytics bounds under GDPR. No public reports of data misuse, malware distribution, or deceptive practices found.

Admost.description: Admost is a full-cycle monetisation system for app publishers, featuring mediation, revenue analytics, and cross-promotion through a single SDK.
Admost.history: Admost was founded around 2015-2016 in Istanbul, Turkey by a team who were originally app publishers and identified weaknesses in ad monetization workflows. They built their own ad mediation platform to address gaps in the market. The platform grew to optimize over 12 billion monthly impressions and integrate with 40+ ad networks, becoming a notable player in the Turkish and regional mobile ad tech ecosystem.
Admost.summary_abuse: Admost is a B2B mobile ad mediation SDK primarily used by app publishers for revenue optimization. As an ad network aggregator, it funnels advertising through multiple third-party networks, which inherently involves significant data collection on end users for targeting purposes. No specific public reports of Admost being weaponized for malware or fraud. The main concern is data privacy, as the SDK collects behavioral and device signals across many apps simultaneously.

AdNabu.description: AdNabu is a software solution for managing product feeds on Shopify, enabling organization and optimization for online stores.
AdNabu.history: AdNabu was founded in 2014 by Salil Panikkaveettil and Suvodhoy Sinha, based in Newark, Delaware with operations in Bangalore. The company provides AI-powered Google Shopping feed management software specifically for Shopify merchants, serving over 10,000 active stores.
AdNabu.summary_abuse: AdNabu is a product feed management tool for Shopify merchants with no known abuse history. Its purpose is optimizing Google Shopping feeds for legitimate e-commerce businesses, and misuse for fraud is not a documented concern.

Adnegah.description: Adnegah is a digital marketing and internet advertising agency.
Adnegah.history: Adnegah is a digital marketing and internet advertising agency founded in 2015 in Tehran, Iran. It operates primarily in the Iranian digital market, offering services including banner advertising, Google Ads management, SEO, social media marketing, and website development.
Adnegah.summary_abuse: Adnegah is a regional Iranian digital advertising agency with no notable abuse signals in public security research. Operating primarily within Iran, the platform serves local businesses with standard advertising services. Due to international sanctions affecting Iran, some advertising practices may not align with Western compliance frameworks, but no specific reports of malicious activity, phishing infrastructure, or fraud facilitation have been documented.

Adnymics.description: Adnymics is a platform that enables personalization in ecommerce by tailoring content, recommendations, and experiences to individual users based on their behavior and preferences.
Adnymics.history: Adnymics GmbH is a Munich, Germany-based ecommerce personalization platform specializing in personalized parcel inserts and direct mail campaigns for online retailers. The company uses AI to tailor physical marketing materials sent inside customer parcels, targeting individual shoppers based on their purchase history. Notable customers include Marc O'Polo, AboutYou, Babble, and ShareNow.
Adnymics.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Adnymics is a B2B ecommerce personalization service focused on physical parcel insert campaigns. It processes customer behavioral data for targeting, which involves privacy considerations but operates within GDPR constraints as a German company. The service does not host user-generated content or provide infrastructure that would attract malicious actors.

Adobe Analytics.description: Adobe Analytics is a web analytics, marketing and cross-channel analytics application.
