Adobe Analytics.history: Adobe Analytics originated as Omniture SiteCatalyst, launched in 1996 by Omniture Inc. in Orem, Utah. It was one of the earliest enterprise web analytics platforms and grew to be an industry leader. Adobe acquired Omniture in October 2009 for approximately $1.8 billion and rebranded the product as Adobe SiteCatalyst, later renaming it Adobe Analytics. It became a core part of the Adobe Marketing Cloud (now Adobe Experience Cloud). It competes directly with Google Analytics 360 and is the dominant choice for Fortune 500 enterprises.
Adobe Analytics.summary_abuse: Adobe Analytics is an enterprise web analytics platform used by major corporations worldwide. Its analytics tracking scripts are occasionally spoofed or mimicked by phishing pages attempting to appear legitimate, but the platform itself has no notable history of facilitating abuse. Adobe maintains a comprehensive security program, responsible disclosure policy, and compliance certifications. Legitimate use substantially overwhelms any incidental misuse.

Adobe Audience Manager.description: Adobe Audience Manager is a versatile audience data management platform.
Adobe Audience Manager.history: Adobe Audience Manager (AAM) is a data management platform (DMP) that Adobe acquired as part of its purchase of Demdex Inc. in January 2011. Demdex had developed one of the earlier third-party DMP solutions. Adobe integrated it into the Marketing Cloud as a core audience segmentation and data management tool. As of the early 2020s, Adobe signaled a shift toward its newer Customer Data Platform (Adobe Real-Time CDP) as the preferred audience management solution, putting AAM in a maintenance-oriented phase.
Adobe Audience Manager.summary_abuse: Adobe Audience Manager is a data management platform for enterprise advertisers enabling audience segmentation and targeting. As a DMP, it collects and processes large volumes of behavioral data on website visitors, which carries inherent privacy implications. Adobe complies with GDPR, CCPA, and other data regulations. No notable reports of AAM being directly implicated in fraud or malicious campaigns. Legitimate enterprise use dominates.

Adobe Client Data Layer.description: Adobe Client Data Layer is a framework of JavaScript objects on your site that contains all variable values used in your implementation.
Adobe Client Data Layer.history: Adobe Client Data Layer (ACDL) is an open-source JavaScript library released by Adobe around 2020 to provide a standardized data layer implementation for web properties, particularly those using Adobe Experience Manager. It follows the W3C Customer Experience Digital Data Layer specification. The project is maintained on GitHub under the Adobe organization.
Adobe Client Data Layer.summary_abuse: No abuse signal. Adobe Client Data Layer is a small open-source JavaScript utility for standardized data layer management on websites. It has no consumer-facing surface and no mechanism for hosting, redirecting, or facilitating malicious content.

Adobe ColdFusion.description: Adobe ColdFusion is a server-side scripting platform for building web applications and APIs, using a language called CFML (ColdFusion Markup Language).
Adobe ColdFusion.history: ColdFusion was created by Allaire Corporation in 1995 as one of the first web application server platforms. Macromedia acquired Allaire in 2001, and Adobe subsequently acquired Macromedia in 2005, bringing ColdFusion into the Adobe portfolio. Despite declining popularity relative to newer platforms like PHP, Node.js, and Java frameworks, Adobe has continued releasing major versions. ColdFusion has a history of critical security vulnerabilities, including multiple high-severity CVEs over the years, leading to attacks on government and enterprise servers. Adobe released ColdFusion 2023 as the latest major version.
Adobe ColdFusion.summary_abuse: ColdFusion has a documented history of serious security vulnerabilities that have been actively exploited by attackers to compromise web servers. High-profile CVEs have led to real-world breaches at government agencies, enterprises, and universities. CISA has issued multiple alerts about ColdFusion vulnerabilities being used in ransomware and espionage campaigns. Organizations running ColdFusion need a rigorous patching regimen. The platform itself is not used to facilitate abuse, but unpatched instances are a significant attack vector.

Adobe DTM.description: Dynamic Tag Management (DTM) is a tag management solution for Adobe Experience Cloud applications and others.
Adobe DTM.history: Adobe Dynamic Tag Management (DTM) was launched by Adobe in 2012 as a tag management system bundled with Adobe Marketing Cloud subscriptions. It was Adobe's answer to Google Tag Manager and Tealium. Adobe officially deprecated DTM in favor of Adobe Experience Platform Launch (now Adobe Tags), with the sunset date set for December 31, 2020. Organizations that had not migrated by that date lost DTM support. The replacement, Adobe Launch, is built on an extensible open-source architecture.
Adobe DTM.summary_abuse: Adobe DTM is a deprecated tag management system with no active consumer surface. During its active lifespan, DTM scripts could theoretically be hijacked to inject malicious tags, a risk common to all tag managers. Adobe deprecated DTM in 2020, and sites still running it are at elevated risk due to lack of security updates, but no specific DTM-targeted attacks were documented publicly.

Adobe Dynamic Media Classic.description: Adobe Dynamic Media Classic is a platform that enables customers to manage, enhance, publish, and deliver dynamic rich media content and personal experiences to consumers across all channels and devices, including web, print material, email campaigns, desktops, social, and mobile.
Adobe Dynamic Media Classic.history: Adobe Dynamic Media Classic originated from Scene7, a rich media SaaS platform founded in 1996 that specialized in web-scale image and video delivery. Adobe acquired Scene7 in May 2007 and rebranded the platform as Scene7/Adobe Scene7, eventually renaming it Adobe Dynamic Media Classic as part of the Adobe Experience Cloud suite. Adobe has since developed a newer Dynamic Media offering integrated directly with Adobe Experience Manager, positioning Dynamic Media Classic as a legacy product for existing customers.
Adobe Dynamic Media Classic.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Adobe Dynamic Media Classic is an enterprise digital asset management and rich media delivery platform used by large retail and media organizations. It serves image and video assets at scale but does not provide public hosting or user-generated content capabilities that would attract malicious actors. Legitimate enterprise use dominates entirely.

Adobe Experience Manager.description: Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) is a content management solution for building websites, mobile apps and forms.
Adobe Experience Manager.history: Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) has its roots in Day Software, a Swiss company that developed the CQ5 (Communique) CMS platform based on the Java Content Repository (JCR) standard. Adobe acquired Day Software in October 2010 for approximately $240 million and rebranded CQ5 as Adobe CQ and subsequently Adobe Experience Manager. AEM became a flagship product in the Adobe Experience Cloud, competing with Sitecore, Salesforce Experience Cloud, and other enterprise CMS platforms. It is widely adopted by Fortune 500 companies for large-scale content management.
Adobe Experience Manager.summary_abuse: Adobe Experience Manager is an enterprise CMS platform serving major corporations. It has had CVEs documented over the years — notably SSRF and XSS vulnerabilities in older versions — that could be exploited if unpatched. Adobe has a mature security patching process and publishes regular cumulative fixes. AEM-hosted websites do not inherently expose more abuse surface than other enterprise web stacks. Legitimate enterprise use dominates entirely.

Adobe Experience Manager Edge Delivery S.description: Edge Delivery Services in Adobe Experience Manager Sites as a Cloud Service, also know as Helix, Franklin, Next-Generation Composability is a fast and lightweight content management system from Adobe.
Adobe Experience Manager Edge Delivery S.history: Adobe Experience Manager Edge Delivery Services (EDS) is a relatively new content delivery infrastructure from Adobe, launched around 2022 as part of the AEM as a Cloud Service offering. It provides a document-based authoring experience (originally developed as Project Franklin) combined with an edge CDN delivery layer, enabling extremely high performance web publishing. It is positioned as an alternative authoring workflow to traditional AEM Sites, targeting marketing teams who prefer Google Docs or SharePoint as content sources.
Adobe Experience Manager Edge Delivery S.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Adobe Experience Manager Edge Delivery Services is an enterprise content delivery infrastructure used by large organizations. It does not provide user-generated content hosting or anonymous access capabilities that would invite misuse. All traffic serves through Adobe's CDN infrastructure with enterprise-level access controls.

Adobe Experience Manager Franklin.description: Adobe Experience Manager Franklin, also known as Project Helix or Composability, is a new way to publish AEM pages using Google Drive or Microsoft Office via Sharepoint. Instead of components, Franklin uses blocks to build pages. Blocks are pieces of a document that will be transformed into web page content.
Adobe Experience Manager Franklin.history: Adobe Experience Manager Franklin was the original project codename for what became Adobe Experience Manager Edge Delivery Services. Developed around 2020-2021 internally at Adobe, Project Franklin introduced a document-based authoring model using Google Drive and Microsoft SharePoint as content sources, combined with an edge delivery layer for fast web performance. The product was publicly released under the Franklin name and later rebranded to AEM Edge Delivery Services as it became generally available.
Adobe Experience Manager Franklin.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Adobe Experience Manager Franklin is a legacy codename for what became AEM Edge Delivery Services. It served as an enterprise content publishing tool for large organizations and had no user-generated content or hosting features that would invite abuse.

Adobe Experience Platform Identity Servi.description: Adobe Experience Platform Identity Service creates identity graphs that hold customer profiles and the known identifiers that belong to individual consumers.
Adobe Experience Platform Identity Servi.history: Adobe Experience Platform Identity Service (formerly Adobe Experience Cloud ID Service or Marketing Cloud ID Service) is a persistent cross-solution identity layer introduced by Adobe around 2014-2015 as part of the Marketing Cloud. It assigns a unique visitor ID (ECID/MID) across all Adobe Experience Cloud solutions to enable unified visitor profiles. It is deployed via Adobe Launch or as a standalone JavaScript library. The service has evolved through multiple renames as Adobe rebranded its cloud product suite.
Adobe Experience Platform Identity Servi.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Adobe Experience Platform Identity Service is a backend identity coordination library used exclusively within Adobe Experience Cloud deployments. It sets first-party cookies and transmits visitor IDs to Adobe servers for cross-product analytics; this carries standard enterprise analytics privacy implications. No public reports of the service being exploited for malicious purposes.

Adobe Experience Platform Launch.description: Adobe Experience Cloud Launch is an extendable tag management solution for Adobe Experience Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, and other applications.
Adobe Experience Platform Launch.history: Adobe Experience Platform Launch (now branded as Tags in Adobe Experience Platform) was launched in 2017 as the successor to Adobe Dynamic Tag Management (DTM). Built on an open-source extensible architecture, it allowed third-party vendors to build extensions, similar to the Google Tag Manager marketplace. It replaced DTM fully when Adobe sunset DTM at end of 2020. Launch is deeply integrated with Adobe Experience Platform and is the standard tag management system for Adobe Experience Cloud customers.
Adobe Experience Platform Launch.summary_abuse: Adobe Experience Platform Launch is an enterprise tag management system. Like all tag managers, it deploys third-party JavaScript to websites, meaning a misconfigured or compromised Launch property could introduce malicious scripts to production sites. Adobe provides role-based access controls and environment-level approvals to mitigate this. No specific documented public incidents of Adobe Launch being weaponized. Legitimate enterprise use dominates.

Adobe Flash.description: Adobe Flash is a multimedia software platform used for production of animations, rich web applications and embedded web browser video players.
Adobe Flash.history: Adobe Flash (originally Macromedia Flash) was created by FutureWave Software in 1993 as SmartSketch, rebranded as FutureSplash Animator, then acquired by Macromedia in 1996 and renamed Flash. Adobe acquired Macromedia in 2005. At its peak Flash powered interactive web experiences, games, and video across nearly all desktop browsers. However, its closed plugin architecture, poor mobile support, and catastrophic security record led to its decline after Apple blocked Flash on iOS in 2010 and HTML5 emerged as an alternative. Adobe officially deprecated Flash Player on December 31, 2020, and actively blocks Flash content from running.
Adobe Flash.summary_abuse: Adobe Flash has a severe abuse history spanning its entire lifespan. It was the most exploited browser plugin throughout the 2000s and 2010s, with hundreds of critical CVEs used in drive-by download attacks, ransomware delivery, and zero-day exploitation. Fake Flash update prompts became one of the most prolific malware distribution vectors. After Adobe's EOL in December 2020, any sites or tools distributing Flash Player installers are almost certainly malicious. Adobe actively blocked the plugin from running after EOL. Flash is a landmark case in browser plugin insecurity.

Adobe Fonts.description: Adobe Fonts is a web-based service providing access to a vast library of high-quality fonts for web and print design.
Adobe Fonts.history: Adobe Fonts (previously Adobe Typekit) started as Typekit, a font delivery service co-founded by Jeffrey Veen and Ryan Carver in 2008. Adobe acquired Typekit in September 2011 and integrated it into Creative Cloud. In 2018, Adobe rebranded the service as Adobe Fonts and expanded the library significantly, making all fonts available to Creative Cloud subscribers. Adobe Fonts serves thousands of typefaces from major foundries and is one of the largest web font delivery networks.
Adobe Fonts.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Adobe Fonts is a web font delivery CDN serving licensed typefaces to Creative Cloud subscribers. It does not provide user-generated content hosting or domain customization features that would attract malicious actors. Adobe has strong content policies and legitimate use dominates entirely.

Adobe GoLive.description: Adobe GoLive is a WYSIWYG HTML editor and web site management application.
Adobe GoLive.history: Adobe GoLive was originally developed by GoNet Communications as CyberStudio, then acquired by Adobe in 1998 and renamed GoLive CyberStudio, later shortened to Adobe GoLive. It was a WYSIWYG web design and site management application competing with Macromedia Dreamweaver. When Adobe acquired Macromedia in 2005, it gained both products and subsequently discontinued GoLive in 2008, directing users to migrate to Dreamweaver.
Adobe GoLive.summary_abuse: No abuse signal. Adobe GoLive was a desktop web design application discontinued in 2008. It produced HTML files locally and had no consumer-facing surface or hosting capability that could be exploited for malicious purposes.

Adobe Muse.description: Adobe Muse is a no code offline website builder used to create fixed, fluid, or adaptive websites, without the need to write code.
Adobe Muse.history: Adobe Muse was a website design application launched in 2011 as part of Adobe Creative Cloud that allowed designers to create websites without writing code. It was positioned for designers who wanted a drag-and-drop interface without needing to learn HTML/CSS. Adobe announced in March 2018 that it would cease development of Muse, with final end-of-life in March 2020. Adobe directed customers to use Adobe XD and other tools instead.
Adobe Muse.summary_abuse: No abuse signal. Adobe Muse was a discontinued desktop web design application. It produced static HTML/CSS output and had no server-side hosting or content distribution features that would attract malicious actors.

Adobe Portfolio.description: Adobe Portfolio is an Adobe platform that allows you to create a web page where you can show your projects, creations, and the services you offer.
Adobe Portfolio.history: Adobe Portfolio originated as Behance ProSite, a portfolio hosting feature for creative professionals. Adobe acquired Behance in December 2012 for approximately $150 million, gaining the portfolio-building tool alongside the social showcase platform. Adobe rebranded it as Adobe Portfolio and included it as a benefit for Creative Cloud subscribers around 2015. The service lets creatives build and host portfolio websites using Behance project data.
Adobe Portfolio.summary_abuse: Minimal abuse signals. Adobe Portfolio is a portfolio website builder bundled with Creative Cloud. As a hosted website platform it could theoretically host content that violates Adobe's terms, but Adobe enforces content policies actively. Portfolio sites run under adobe.com or custom domains and could be used for copyright-infringing art displays, but targeted malicious abuse is not a documented pattern.

Adobe RoboHelp.description: Adobe RoboHelp is a Help Authoring Tool (HAT) that allows you to create help systems, e-learning content and knowledge bases.
Adobe RoboHelp.history: Adobe RoboHelp is a technical documentation and help authoring tool originally developed by Blue Sky Software in the early 1990s. eHelp Corporation acquired Blue Sky and the product, and Macromedia subsequently acquired eHelp in 2003. Adobe acquired Macromedia in 2005 and inherited RoboHelp. Adobe has continued developing it for technical writers and documentation teams, and it remains one of the leading help authoring tools on the market.
Adobe RoboHelp.summary_abuse: No abuse signal. Adobe RoboHelp is a desktop technical documentation authoring tool used by technical writers in enterprises. It produces help files and documentation websites and has no consumer-facing hosting or interactive features that would attract malicious actors.

Adobe Target.description: Adobe Target is an A/B testing, multi-variate testing, personalisation, and optimisation application
Adobe Target.history: Adobe Target originated from Omniture Test&Target, which Adobe acquired as part of the Omniture acquisition in October 2009 for approximately $1.8 billion. Test&Target was a web A/B testing and personalization platform. Adobe rebranded and integrated it deeply into what became Adobe Experience Cloud. Over time Adobe Target evolved from simple A/B testing to a full AI-driven personalization engine leveraging Adobe Sensei for automated recommendations and targeting.
Adobe Target.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Adobe Target is an enterprise A/B testing and personalization platform used by large corporations. It collects visitor behavioral data for targeting and personalization, involving standard ad-tech privacy considerations. Adobe complies with GDPR, CCPA, and other regulations. No public reports of Adobe Target being implicated in fraud or malicious campaigns.

AdOcean.history: AdOcean is an ad server platform that is part of Gemius, a European research and technology company. Founded in 2004 and based in Poland, AdOcean has grown to operate in over 20 countries, serving publishers primarily in Europe, Asia, and Africa with cross-device ad serving capabilities.
AdOcean.summary_abuse: AdOcean is a Gemius-owned ad server used by publishers in Europe and emerging markets. As an ad serving platform embedded in publisher sites, it can carry ad creatives that may occasionally be low quality or deceptive, but it is not specifically known as a vector for phishing or malware distribution.

Adomik.description: Adomik is a tool that assists digital publishers in enhancing their programmatic and real-time bidding (RTB) yield management, functioning as a programmatic ad network.
Adomik.history: Adomik was founded in 2012 in Paris, France by Nicolas Schueller and Jean-Francois Bernard. The founders previously worked in digital media yield management and built Adomik to address the informational asymmetry between digital media buyers and sellers. The company raised €3.04 million Series B in 2015 co-led by Elaia Partners and IRIS. Notable clients include Microsoft, Cox Automotive, and eBay Kleinanzeigen. By 2024 the company had approximately 44 employees and $5.9M in annual revenue.
Adomik.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Adomik is a B2B programmatic advertising yield analytics platform used by digital publishers. It processes advertising transaction data from publisher ad stacks; this involves some user behavioral data in an aggregated form but the platform does not directly collect end-user PII. No public reports of abuse, fraud facilitation, or malicious activity.

AdonisJS.description: AdonisJS is a Node.js web application framework that follows the MVC pattern, simplifying web development with features like ORM, authentication, and WebSockets.
AdonisJS.history: AdonisJS is a Node.js MVC web framework created by Harminder Virk from India, first released on GitHub in 2015. Inspired by Laravel (PHP), it provides a batteries-included approach to Node.js development including ORM, authentication, validation, and CLI scaffolding. The framework has a dedicated following and has seen continued major version releases. AdonisJS 6 introduced significant improvements and is actively maintained by its creator with community contributions.
AdonisJS.summary_abuse: No abuse signal. AdonisJS is an open-source Node.js server-side framework. Developers use it to build backend applications; it has no consumer surface and no hosting capabilities that would enable malicious content distribution.

AdOpt.description: AdOpt is a consent tool that prioritises privacy and usability towards the LGPD.
AdOpt.history: AdOpt (goadopt.io) is a cookie consent and privacy compliance platform supporting GDPR, LGPD, CCPA, and other regulations. The company has positioned itself as an easy-to-deploy consent management platform for websites. No notable acquisition or ownership change history is documented.
AdOpt.summary_abuse: AdOpt is a cookie consent management platform designed to help websites comply with privacy laws. It has no documented abuse history and its function is to reduce privacy violations rather than enable them.

Adoric.description: Adoric is a lead generation tool for personalized website engagement and conversion optimisation (CRO) platform.
Adoric.history: Adoric was founded in 2013 in Tel Aviv, Israel by Tal Revivo. It is a conversion rate optimization and website personalization platform primarily serving ecommerce merchants, with a Shopify app integration. The company remained small with approximately 16 employees as of 2026.
Adoric.summary_abuse: Minimal documented abuse signals. Adoric is a popup and conversion tool embedded on ecommerce sites. Popup-based tools can sometimes be used to display misleading urgency messages or deceptive exit-intent overlays, which Adoric's feature set technically supports. However, responsibility lies with the merchant deploying the popups. No specific reports of Adoric's platform being weaponized for malware delivery or credential phishing.

AdRecover.description: AdRecover is a tool that helps online publishers monetise their Adblock inventory.
AdRecover.history: AdRecover was founded in 2016 in Palo Alto, California and was backed by Microsoft Ventures. The company provides adblock bypass and ad recovery services for publishers. It was later acquired by AdPushup and operates as a subsidiary under the Zelto adtech umbrella.
AdRecover.summary_abuse: AdRecover delivers ads to users who have installed adblockers by serving compliant ads meeting Acceptable Ads Standards. While adblock bypass technology is sometimes associated with intrusive practices, AdRecover claims to respect user preferences. The technology can be used to circumvent privacy tools which raises moderate trust concerns.

AdRiver.description: AdRiver is a company which provide internet advertising management and audit software.
AdRiver.history: AdRiver was founded in 2001 in Moscow, Russia at the beginning of the Russian internet advertising market. The company became a leader in ad management and auditing for the Russian-speaking internet. It serves major Russian websites and advertisers, processing billions of ad requests daily.
AdRiver.summary_abuse: AdRiver is a Russian ad serving platform predominantly used by publishers in Russia and CIS countries. As an ad network operating in a jurisdiction with limited international regulatory oversight, there is elevated risk of low-quality or deceptive ad creatives. No specific major abuse incidents have been publicly documented.

AdRoll.description: AdRoll is a digital marketing technology platform that specialises in retargeting.
AdRoll.history: AdRoll was founded in 2006 by Aaron Bell, Jared Kopf, and Adam Berke in San Francisco, originally as Semantic Sugar Inc. The company pivoted to retargeting advertising and became one of the first large-scale retargeting platforms accessible to small businesses. It raised $70 million in a 2014 Series C round. AdRoll later became part of the NextRoll parent company umbrella.
AdRoll.summary_abuse: AdRoll is a retargeting and marketing automation platform that places tracking pixels on websites to serve personalized ads. It collects significant behavioral data across the web, which is standard for ad-tech but raises privacy concerns. The platform is legitimate and widely used by businesses, with no specific history of being a major fraud vector.

AdRoll CMP System.description: AdRoll CMP System is a consent management solution.
AdRoll CMP System.history: The AdRoll CMP System is a consent management platform developed by AdRoll (NextRoll) to help advertisers and publishers comply with GDPR and CCPA privacy regulations. It was built as part of AdRoll's broader advertising compliance toolkit. AdRoll itself was founded in 2006.
AdRoll CMP System.summary_abuse: The AdRoll CMP System is a consent management solution for privacy compliance. It is designed to help websites collect and manage user consent for data processing. No documented abuse history exists for this specific product.

AdScale.description: AdScale is a cloud-based, AI-powered performance optimisation platform which utilises machine learning to automate and optimise AdWords campaigns across Google Search, Google Shopping, Google Display, and YouTube.
AdScale.history: AdScale is an AI-powered advertising automation platform for ecommerce businesses focused on Google and Meta campaigns. The company primarily serves Shopify and BigCommerce merchants. No major acquisition or ownership change events have been publicly documented.
AdScale.summary_abuse: AdScale is an advertising automation tool for legitimate ecommerce merchants. It is not known to be misused for fraud or phishing. Its primary use is automating paid search and social advertising for online stores.

AdThrive.description: AdThrive is an online advertising network aka ad provider for bloggers for blog monetisation.
AdThrive.history: AdThrive was founded in 2013 by Kelly and Andrew Wilder to help their food blog monetize with ads. It grew into a leading ad management network for lifestyle and food creators. In 2016 it was acquired by CafeMedia. In April 2023, CafeMedia, AdThrive, and their other brands were unified under the new name Raptive.
AdThrive.summary_abuse: AdThrive (now Raptive) is an ad management network for content creators. It embeds third-party advertising on publisher websites, which can occasionally include low-quality or deceptive ad creatives from programmatic demand partners. The platform is legitimate and focused on premium publishers, with no history as a major fraud vector.

Adtriba.description: Adtriba is a platform that captures all marketing touchpoints and calculates the profitability of each campaign using big data technology and AI.
Adtriba.history: Adtriba was founded in 2015 in Hamburg, Germany by Janos Moldvay (CEO) and Ludwig Ostrowski (CTO), both marketing intelligence and data science experts. The company built a cross-channel marketing attribution platform using machine learning. In June 2024, Adtriba was acquired by Funnel Operations AB, a Swedish marketing intelligence platform, to expand Funnel's attribution analytics capabilities.
Adtriba.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Adtriba is a B2B marketing attribution analytics platform used by advertisers to understand channel performance. It processes advertising data and does not host user-generated content or provide consumer-facing services that would attract malicious activity.

Adtribute.description: Adtribute is a tool designed to track users accurately and unify data, enhancing outcomes in marketing attribution, business intelligence, and data activation.
Adtribute.history: Adtribute is a marketing attribution and first-party data platform targeting ecommerce brands and performance marketing agencies. It positions itself as a server-side tracking solution immune to ad blockers and browser cookie restrictions, combining multi-touch attribution (MTA) with media mix modeling (MMM). Limited public information is available about the company's founding date and exact headquarters.
Adtribute.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Adtribute is a small B2B marketing attribution SaaS used by ecommerce companies and agencies for ad performance tracking. It collects first-party behavioral data but does not host consumer-facing content or enable user-generated material that would invite malicious activity.

Advally.description: Advally is an advertising platform for publishers.
Advally.history: Advally is a digital advertising network and publisher monetization platform founded in 2016 and headquartered in Montreal, Canada. The company offers programmatic advertising, eCPM optimization, and ad layout services to website publishers.
Advally.summary_abuse: Advally is an ad network that serves programmatic advertising to publisher websites. Ad networks of this type can carry malvertising risk if ad quality controls are insufficient, as malicious ads may be injected through the programmatic supply chain. No specific public security incidents involving Advally have been documented, but the ad-tech category generally carries elevated malvertising risk.

Advanced Custom Fields.description: Advanced Custom Fields is a WordPress plugin which allows you to add extra content fields to your WordPress edit screens.
Advanced Custom Fields.history: Advanced Custom Fields (ACF) was created by Australian developer Elliot Condon in 2010 as a WordPress plugin to extend the default custom fields functionality. Condon served as sole developer for the first decade while the plugin grew to over 1 million active installs. In June 2021, Delicious Brains acquired ACF. In June 2022, WP Engine acquired ACF along with other Delicious Brains plugins. ACF became briefly controversial in September 2024 when WordPress.org forked the plugin as Secure Custom Fields (SCF) during the WordPress vs. WP Engine dispute led by Matt Mullenweg.
Advanced Custom Fields.summary_abuse: ACF itself has no direct abuse role, but as one of the most widely installed WordPress plugins, it has a meaningful attack surface. Vulnerabilities in ACF (such as authenticated SQL injection and XSS bugs patched over the years) have been used to compromise WordPress sites. The plugin's widespread installation base makes any unpatched vulnerability high-value. WP Engine maintains security patching and the plugin has a responsible disclosure process.

Advert Stream.history: Advert Stream (advertstream.com) is a French digital advertising and SSP platform operated under the Adthink group, which was founded in 2001. The company started with video streaming advertising and evolved into a broader programmatic advertising platform offering supply-side and RTB services. It operates primarily in the French and European digital advertising markets.
Advert Stream.summary_abuse: Advert Stream is a programmatic advertising and SSP platform. As an ad network serving display and video ads, it carries inherent malvertising risk common to the ad-tech sector — malicious creatives can be injected through programmatic supply chains. No specific documented public incidents involving Advert Stream have been found, but the category warrants moderate vigilance.

Adverticum.description: Adverticum is the developer and operator of Hungary's market leading online ad serving solution, the Adverticum AdServer.
Adverticum.history: Adverticum was founded in 2001 in Hungary, making it one of the oldest independent ad-serving companies in Central and Eastern Europe. It operates as Hungary's market-leading ad server platform, serving 16 billion ad impressions per month to approximately 5 million internet users across Hungary, Slovakia, Romania, and Croatia. The company provides an independent third-party ad serving platform for campaign management, inventory optimization, and analytics.
Adverticum.summary_abuse: Adverticum is a regional ad server and display ad network operating in Central and Eastern Europe. As an ad serving platform it carries the standard malvertising risk of the ad-tech sector, where malicious creatives can potentially enter through RTB supply chains. No specific public reports of Adverticum being implicated in targeted malware campaigns have been found.

Advin.description: Advin is a provider of ecommerce solutions and tailored web information systems for clients.
Advin.history: Advin began as a web and information systems specialist around 2003 and was formalized as ADVIN base s.r.o. in 2011 through a merger of long-term collaborators in Olomouc, Czech Republic. The company expanded into custom e-shop programming as the Czech ecommerce market grew. No notable acquisitions, rebrands, or ownership changes since.
Advin.summary_abuse: Advin is a small regional Czech ecommerce web solutions provider with no notable abuse signal. Its services are custom-built websites and e-shops for local clients, with no anonymous sign-up, no free public hosting tier, and no mass-email or URL-shortening features that would attract fraudsters. No documented cases of abuse or security incidents have been found.

Adyen.description: Adyen allows businesses to accept ecommerce, mobile, and point-of-sale payments.
Adyen.history: Adyen was founded in 2006 in Amsterdam, Netherlands by Pieter van der Does and Arnout Schuijff. The company built a single global payment platform from scratch, targeting enterprise merchants frustrated by fragmented legacy payment systems. It grew rapidly, processing payments for Booking.com, Spotify, Netflix, and many Fortune 500 companies. Adyen IPO'd on Euronext Amsterdam in June 2018, one of the most anticipated European tech IPOs of the decade. The company is considered a flagship of European fintech.
Adyen.summary_abuse: Adyen has a strong fraud prevention posture as a regulated payment institution. As a payments infrastructure provider, it is occasionally used by fraudulent merchants who attempt to abuse the platform to process illegitimate transactions or facilitate scams. Adyen maintains robust KYC/KYB onboarding, risk scoring, and fraud detection systems, and works closely with regulators and law enforcement. Adyen's response to abuse reports is considered prompt relative to industry standards.

Aedi.description: Aedi is a provider of AI solutions for ecommerce.
Aedi.history: Aedi (aedi.ai) is an AI solutions provider for ecommerce. Limited public information is available about its founding date or history. The platform offers AI-powered tools for online retailers.
Aedi.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Aedi is a small B2B AI platform for ecommerce with no consumer-facing surface that would attract malicious actors.

Aegea.description: Aegea is a blog engine created by Ilya Birman.
Aegea.history: Aegea is a lightweight blog engine created by Russian UI designer and developer Ilya Birman, published at blogengine.ru. It is a minimal self-hosted blogging platform primarily targeting Russian-speaking bloggers. The project is maintained by a single developer.
Aegea.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal. Aegea is a self-hosted blog engine with no hosted service. Users run it on their own servers and content moderation is entirely the operator's responsibility. No documented patterns of abuse related to the Aegea software itself.

Aero Commerce.description: Aero Commerce is a performance-based platform designed with the evolving needs of retailers in mind.
Aero Commerce.history: Aero Commerce was founded in 2017 in Middlesbrough, England by Richard Bendelow, Matt Burton, and Tim Spratt — who previously built and sold Visualsoft, one of the UK's largest ecommerce agencies. Aero is a high-performance, headless ecommerce platform positioned as an alternative to Shopify and Magento for retailers serious about performance. The company raised £750,000 in seed funding from Maven Capital Partners in 2021.
Aero Commerce.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Aero Commerce is a B2B ecommerce platform used by UK retailers. It operates as a managed platform without user-generated content hosting capabilities that would attract malicious activity. No public security incidents documented.

Affilae.description: Affilae is an affiliate marketing platform that enables brands to connect, collaborate with influencers and affiliates.
Affilae.history: Affilae was founded in 2012 by Pierre Bagoze and Alexandre Dos Santos in Bordeaux, France. The company launched with a differentiated pricing model that avoided setup fees and commissions on campaigns, offering instead a flat SaaS subscription for affiliate program management. It serves primarily the French and European affiliate marketing market.
Affilae.summary_abuse: Affilae is a B2B affiliate program management platform. Affiliate networks can attract abuse in the form of fraudulent affiliate traffic, click fraud, and cookie stuffing — issues common to the affiliate marketing sector. Affilae provides tracking and fraud detection tools but ultimate responsibility for publisher quality lies with advertisers. No specific documented incidents of Affilae being implicated in large-scale fraud campaigns.

Affiliate B.description: Affiliate B is an advertising system that allows site operators (HP, blogs, e-mail newsletters, etc.) to place advertiser advertisements on their own sites.
Affiliate B.history: Affiliate B (affiliate-b.com) is a Japanese advertising system that allows website operators, blogs, and email newsletters to display advertiser ads on their own sites. It operates as a relatively small affiliate advertising network primarily serving the Japanese market.
Affiliate B.summary_abuse: Affiliate B is a small Japanese affiliate advertising network. Like all ad networks it carries inherent malvertising risk where low-quality ad creatives could theoretically be served through the network. Limited public information available about its abuse posture or security policies.

Affiliate Future.description: Affiliate Future is a provider of advertisers with marketing solution through its affiliate network and tools.
Affiliate Future.history: Affiliate Future was founded in 1999 in the UK as one of the early affiliate marketing networks during the rise of internet commerce. It was acquired by Internet Business Group plc, then merged into Progressive Digital Media Group (later GlobalData plc) through a series of ownership changes. In October 2025, Adtraction Group AB acquired Affiliate Future from GlobalData plc, bringing it under the Adtraction umbrella to strengthen Adtraction's UK market presence.
Affiliate Future.summary_abuse: Affiliate Future is a UK affiliate marketing network connecting advertisers with publishers. Affiliate networks carry inherent risk of fraudulent traffic including click fraud, cookie stuffing, and incentivized installs. Affiliate Future has standard publisher vetting processes. No specific documented large-scale abuse incidents attributed to Affiliate Future.

Affiliatly.description: Affiliatly is an affiliate marketing software for ecommerce store owners.
Affiliatly.history: Affiliatly is a Shopify affiliate tracking app that has been in operation for over 10 years, likely launched around 2014-2015. It provides ecommerce store operators with affiliate program management tools including referral tracking by link, coupon, email, and SKU.
Affiliatly.summary_abuse: Affiliatly is a Shopify affiliate app. Affiliate tracking tools can be exploited for fraudulent affiliate traffic, cookie stuffing, and self-referrals. As a platform, Affiliatly provides tracking tools; preventing affiliate fraud is primarily the merchant's responsibility. No specific documented incidents of Affiliatly being abused at scale.

Affilio.description: Affilio is an Iranian affiliate marketing platform.
Affilio.history: Affilio (affilio.ir) is an Iranian affiliate marketing platform. It operates primarily in the Iranian digital market, connecting advertisers with publishers for performance-based marketing campaigns.
Affilio.summary_abuse: Affilio is a regional Iranian affiliate marketing platform. Like all affiliate networks it carries inherent risk of fraudulent traffic including click fraud. Operating primarily in Iran, the platform is subject to international sanctions that may affect its compliance framework. No specific documented abuse incidents found in public sources.

Affilo.description: Affilo is an all-in-one solution for referrals and affiliate marketing.
Affilo.history: Affilo (affilo.io) is an all-in-one referral and affiliate marketing SaaS platform. Limited public information is available about its founding date or history.
Affilo.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Affilo is a small affiliate and referral marketing SaaS. Affiliate tools can be used to generate fraudulent referral traffic, but no specific documented incidents involving Affilo have been found.

Affirm.description: Affirm is a loan company that allows users to buy goods or services offered by online merchants and pay off those purchases in fixed monthly payments.
Affirm.history: Affirm was founded in 2012 in San Francisco by Max Levchin (PayPal co-founder), Nathan Gettings, Jeffrey Kaditz, and Alex Rampell as a buy now, pay later (BNPL) fintech. Levchin became CEO in 2014. The company was incubated within HVF, Levchin's startup studio. Affirm aimed to create transparent consumer credit without hidden fees, in contrast to traditional credit cards. It partnered with major retailers including Walmart and Amazon. Affirm IPO'd on Nasdaq in January 2021 under ticker AFRM. As of 2025 it processes $37 billion annually and serves 27 million users.
Affirm.summary_abuse: Affirm is a regulated BNPL payments company and is subject to consumer lending regulations. Fraud patterns typical of BNPL platforms include synthetic identity fraud and first-party fraud where consumers take items on BNPL and dispute payments. Affirm runs machine learning-based fraud detection and risk underwriting on every transaction. No specific documented patterns of Affirm being disproportionately abused compared to peer BNPL providers.

Aforest LMS.description: AFOREST LMS is a training management system that supports the entire training cycle, including client needs assessment, course organization, learner tracking, quality control, assessments, certification, and e-learning integration, with invoicing capabilities.
Aforest LMS.history: AFOREST LMS is a French training management system that supports the full training lifecycle including client needs assessment, course organization, learner tracking, quality control, assessments, certification, e-learning integration, and invoicing. It is developed by Groupe Aforest.
Aforest LMS.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. AFOREST LMS is a B2B corporate training management system used by organizations to manage internal training programs. It has no consumer-facing hosting or public content features that would attract malicious actors.

Afosto.history: Auglio was founded in 2022 in Komarno, Slovakia, as an augmented reality virtual try-on SaaS platform for e-commerce. It generates 3D product models from 2D product images to enable customers to virtually try on eyewear, cosmetics, and accessories. It is available as a Shopify app and as a plug-and-play integration for other e-commerce platforms.
Afosto.summary_abuse: Auglio is a niche AR virtual try-on platform for e-commerce retailers with no documented abuse signal. The service processes camera data from users' devices for real-time try-on but does not store or sell user data as a primary function. No reports of misuse for phishing or fraud have been documented.

AfterBuy.description: AfterBuy is a software company that specialises in ecommerce software for small to enterprise level businesses.
AfterBuy.history: AfterBuy originated in 2002 as an eBay sales processing tool developed by ViA-Online GmbH, which itself was founded in 1998. The product evolved into a full multichannel ecommerce management platform serving over 120,000 registered merchants. In June 2021, ViA-Online GmbH and DreamRobot GmbH merged under the holding company ECOMMERCE ONE.
AfterBuy.summary_abuse: AfterBuy is a German B2B multichannel ecommerce management platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used by legitimate online retailers to manage orders, inventory, and marketplace listings. The platform does not expose anonymous hosting or mass-send capabilities that are commonly exploited for fraud.

Afterpay.description: Afterpay is a 'buy now, pay later' platform that makes it possible to pay off purchased goods in fortnightly instalments.
Afterpay.history: Afterpay was co-founded in 2014 by Nick Molnar and Anthony Eisen in Sydney, Australia. It listed on the ASX in May 2016 with a $140M valuation and rapidly became one of the world's largest buy now, pay later (BNPL) services, reaching 24 million users and processing $27.3B in annual payments. In August 2021, Block, Inc. (then Square) acquired Afterpay for A$39B, the largest acquisition in Australian corporate history.
Afterpay.summary_abuse: Afterpay as a BNPL payment method has been noted in fraud contexts where stolen payment credentials are used to make purchases split into installments, bypassing traditional fraud checks. The vendor has invested in fraud detection systems and works with merchants to limit chargebacks. Its relatively light identity verification at signup has been flagged as a factor in account takeover fraud patterns.

AfterShip.description: AfterShip provides automated shipment tracking as a service.
AfterShip.history: AfterShip was founded in 2012 by Dante Tsang, Teddy Chan, Andrew Chan, and Jeremy Michaud as a shipment tracking platform for online sellers. The company raised $1M Series A from IDG Capital in 2014, and then a $66M Series B from Tiger Global in 2021, achieving unicorn status with a valuation exceeding $1 billion. AfterShip has since expanded into a comprehensive post-purchase experience suite.
AfterShip.summary_abuse: AfterShip is a B2B post-purchase platform primarily used by legitimate ecommerce businesses. Tracking pages generated by AfterShip could theoretically be used for phishing simulations, but no notable abuse campaigns have been documented. The vendor serves enterprise clients and has standard abuse reporting processes.

AfterShip Returns Center.description: AfterShip Returns Center is an interactive self-service return solution.
AfterShip Returns Center.history: AfterShip Returns Center is a product module within the AfterShip platform, which was founded in 2012. It provides automated returns management for ecommerce businesses, allowing customers to self-service returns and exchanges. The product has grown alongside AfterShip's broader post-purchase suite following the company's 2021 unicorn funding round.
AfterShip Returns Center.summary_abuse: AfterShip Returns Center is a B2B returns management tool used by ecommerce retailers with no documented abuse signal. The platform handles returns logistics for legitimate merchants and does not expose surfaces that would facilitate phishing, spam, or other abuse.

AFThemes CoverNews.description: AFThemes CoverNews is a clean and elegant free WordPress theme that is perfect for online blog and magazine.
AFThemes CoverNews.history: AFThemes is a WordPress theme and plugin developer that has been creating themes for over a decade, trusted by 100,000+ WordPress users. CoverNews is one of their flagship free themes, designed for news portals and blogs. The company appears to be based in Nepal. No notable ownership changes documented.
AFThemes CoverNews.summary_abuse: AFThemes CoverNews is a free WordPress magazine theme with no documented abuse history. As a self-installed WordPress theme, sites using it are subject to standard WordPress security risks if not kept updated. The theme itself ships no trackers. No specific vendor-related abuse incidents.

Age Gate.description: A plugin to check the age of a visitor for Wordpress.
Age Gate.history: Age Gate is a WordPress plugin for age verification available on the official WordPress.org plugin repository. It provides configurable age restriction for entire sites or specific content, targeting use cases like alcohol, adult content, and gambling sites. The plugin is maintained by agegate.io and has over 5 million+ active installations.
Age Gate.summary_abuse: Age Gate is a WordPress age verification plugin with no notable abuse signal. It is used by legitimate websites to comply with age restriction requirements for alcohol, adult, and gambling content. The plugin itself does not collect or transmit personal data beyond what is set by the site administrator.

Agendize.description: Agendize is a next-generation, all-in-one platform that streamlines scheduling, secures data, and improves the performance and efficiency of appointment management.
Agendize.history: Agendize was founded in 2003 in Lyon, France, as an online appointment scheduling and customer engagement platform. The company has maintained a proven technological foundation for over 20 years and raised $9.48M from investors including BNP Paribas Private Equity. It is positioned for large enterprises, multi-site networks, and public institutions.
Agendize.summary_abuse: Agendize is a B2B appointment scheduling SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It serves enterprises and public institutions for legitimate scheduling workflows and does not expose anonymous booking surfaces that would be exploited for spam or fraud at scale.

AgentFire.description: AgentFire is a platform designed for the real estate industry, offering tools and features that help agents capitalize on digital opportunities for marketing, lead generation, and client engagement.
AgentFire.history: AgentFire was founded in 2013 and is headquartered in Glen Cove, New York. It is a 100% bootstrapped company that built a WordPress-based IDX website builder and hyperlocal SEO platform for real estate agents and teams across North America. The company has grown to a 70+ person team.
AgentFire.summary_abuse: AgentFire is a niche real estate website platform with no documented abuse signal. It serves licensed real estate agents and teams with professional websites, and the closed-signup B2B model limits exposure to anonymous or abusive use.

Agile CRM.description: Agile CRM is a software for Customer Relationship Management (CRM) that provides sales and marketing automation features for businesses.
Agile CRM.history: Agile CRM was launched in 2013 by Manohar Chapalamadugu, who previously founded ClickDesk. The company is a bootstrapped, self-funded SaaS based in Dallas, Texas, with Indian roots, and targets small and medium-sized businesses with an integrated sales, marketing, and service CRM platform. It has grown to serve over 15,000 customers.
Agile CRM.summary_abuse: Agile CRM is a small-business CRM SaaS with limited abuse signal. The platform offers email marketing and contact management features that could theoretically be used for spam, but the vendor targets SMBs and has standard anti-spam policies in place. No notable misuse campaigns have been documented.

Agility CMS.description: Agility CMS is an enterprise-level content management system provider offering scalable solutions for managing digital content across multiple platforms.
Agility CMS.history: Agility CMS traces its roots to 2002 when Jonathan Voigt and Michael Assad founded Edentity in Toronto, Canada. In 2005, Joel Varty, Mike Assad, and Jon Voigt built a SaaS-based decoupled CMS, making it an early headless CMS pioneer. In 2018, the company made a strategic decision to go all-in on headless CMS and stopped building websites, refocusing as a pure SaaS platform hosted on Azure.
Agility CMS.summary_abuse: Agility CMS is a headless CMS SaaS platform with no notable abuse signal. As a B2B content management platform used by enterprises and development teams, it does not expose consumer-facing anonymous content hosting that would typically attract phishing or spam abuse.

Agillic.description: Agillic is a Nordic marketing automation platform that delivers scalable, personalized campaigns while ensuring operational efficiency and full GDPR compliance.
Agillic.history: Agillic was founded in 1999 in Copenhagen, Denmark, by Carsten Hyldahl. The company developed into an omnichannel marketing automation platform serving over 100 clients across the Nordics and European markets. Agillic listed on the Nasdaq First North Growth Market Denmark in 2018. The platform is built on EU-based infrastructure to support GDPR compliance.
Agillic.summary_abuse: Agillic is a B2B marketing automation platform serving Nordic and European enterprises. As a regulated SaaS serving established companies with CRM data, it has standard email marketing anti-spam policies. No notable misuse campaigns have been documented against the platform.

Agoda.description: Agoda is an online travel platform offering its services globally via its app and website.
Agoda.history: Agoda was co-founded in 2005 by Michael Kenny and Robert Rosenstein in Phuket, Thailand. In November 2007, it was acquired by Priceline.com (now Booking Holdings), becoming a subsidiary. Agoda expanded from Southeast Asian focus to a global online travel booking platform and now claims over 2 million+ properties and 150 million daily active users.
Agoda.summary_abuse: Agoda is a major online travel platform subsidiary of Booking Holdings. Hotel booking platforms like Agoda are occasionally used in travel scams where fraudsters create fake listings or redirect payments, but Agoda has active fraud prevention and KYC processes for property partners. No specific systematic abuse campaigns have been documented against Agoda itself.

Agora.description: Agora is a real-time engagement platform that provides infrastructure for interactive communication across applications.
Agora.history: Agora.io was founded in 2014 by Tony Zhao, a former member of WebEx and former CTO of YY.com. The company built a global real-time engagement platform providing video, voice, and live interactive streaming SDKs. Agora went public on NASDAQ (API) and has its SDKs installed on over 500 million devices worldwide.
Agora.summary_abuse: Agora is a real-time video and audio SDK used by developers to build communications applications. The platform has been noted in a small number of reports as potentially being used to relay communications for malicious apps or surveillance tools, particularly in mobile applications. The vendor publishes an AUP and works with partners on abuse cases, but embedded SDK use makes enforcement challenging.

Agorize.description: Agorize is a platform that enables businesses to identify and connect with talent through innovation challenges and collaborative programs.
Agorize.history: Agorize was founded in 2011 in Paris, France. The platform specializes in organizing online innovation challenges and hackathons, connecting large companies with startups, students, and developers. The company raised 13M euros in 2019 and worked with clients including Microsoft, Amazon, and L'Oreal. In November 2024, Agorize was acquired by Bloomflow.
Agorize.summary_abuse: Agorize is an open innovation challenge platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves enterprise clients running structured innovation programs and hackathons under controlled conditions, limiting exposure to anonymous or abusive participants.

Ahoy.description: Ahoy is a Ruby gem that provides simple and powerful analytics for Ruby on Rails applications.
Ahoy.history: Ahoy (also known as ahoy_matey) is an open-source Ruby gem created by Andrew Kane for first-party visit and event analytics in Ruby on Rails applications. The library stores tracking data directly in the application's database rather than sending it to third-party services, making it a privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics for Rails developers.
Ahoy.summary_abuse: Ahoy is a server-side Rails analytics library with no abuse signal. It runs entirely within the application server and database owned by the developer, with no third-party data sharing. It cannot be exploited for phishing, spam, or fraud.

Ahrefs.description: Ahrefs is an online toolset utilised for search engine optimisation (SEO) and competitor analysis, which permits users to analyse their website's performance, track keyword rankings, identify backlink opportunities, and research competitors' websites, among other features.
Ahrefs.history: Ahrefs was founded in 2011 by Dmitry Gerasimenko in Singapore, with Ukrainian roots. The company grew entirely on its own revenue without outside investors, reaching $149M ARR in 2024 with a team of about 170. Ahrefs operates one of the world's largest web crawlers and is known for its Site Explorer link analysis and keyword research tools, making it a dominant SEO platform.
Ahrefs.summary_abuse: Ahrefs is a widely-trusted SEO toolset with no notable abuse signal. It provides analysis of third-party websites rather than hosting user content, so it does not expose surfaces commonly exploited for phishing or fraud. SEO tools in general can theoretically be used for competitive intelligence gathering, but Ahrefs has no documented misuse campaigns.

AI LOG.description: AI LOG is a marketing automation and fraud click prevention tool.
AI LOG.history: AI LOG is a Japanese marketing automation and click fraud prevention tool. Limited public history available.
AI LOG.summary_abuse: AI LOG has no notable abuse signal. It is a click fraud prevention and marketing tool with no documented abuse patterns itself.

Aidbase.description: Aidbase is an AI-driven support ecosystem designed to enhance user experiences.
Aidbase.history: Aidbase is an AI-powered customer support platform founded circa 2023, targeting SaaS startups and ecommerce businesses. The platform claims over 10,000 users and combines AI chatbots, ticket management, email handling, and knowledge bases. It is a relatively new entrant in the AI customer service space with no notable ownership changes.
Aidbase.summary_abuse: Aidbase is a small AI customer support SaaS with no documented abuse signal. As a B2B tool that requires business account setup, it does not expose anonymous consumer surfaces for phishing or spam abuse.

Aiden.description: Aiden is a tool designed for ecommerce, guiding customers in finding the right products, similar to an in-store experience, which helps improve conversion rates.
Aiden.history: Aiden is a European guided selling and product recommendation platform for ecommerce brands, positioned as Europe's leading product finder solution. The platform integrates with Shopify, BigCommerce, Magento, and Google Shopping feeds to help online retailers convert hesitant shoppers through interactive product advisors.
Aiden.summary_abuse: Aiden is a B2B product recommendation platform for ecommerce with no notable abuse signal. It helps retailers configure product finders and does not expose anonymous content hosting surfaces that would be exploited for phishing or fraud.

Aimbase.description: Aimbase is a marketing automation platform designed to streamline campaign management, customer engagement, and data-driven decision-making.
Aimbase.history: Aimbase is a marketing automation and CRM platform based in Fenton, Missouri, designed for manufacturers with dealer networks such as powersports and outdoor power equipment companies. It has been operating since the late 1990s and became associated with Rollick, a dealer-network commerce platform.
Aimbase.summary_abuse: Aimbase is a niche B2B CRM and marketing automation tool for manufacturers and dealer networks with no notable abuse signal. Its targeted industry focus limits exposure to mass-send abuse scenarios.

AIMEOS.description: Aimeos is a PHP ecommerce framework designed for custom online shops, multi-vendor marketplaces, and complex B2B applications.
AIMEOS.history: Aimeos is a PHP ecommerce framework developed in Hamburg, Germany since 2015 by a worldwide community. It integrates with Laravel, Symfony, and TYPO3. Available under MIT and LGPLv3 licenses for open-source use, with commercial licensing for enterprise features. Used by thousands of companies globally for high-performance online shops and marketplaces.
AIMEOS.summary_abuse: Aimeos is an open-source PHP ecommerce framework. As a self-hosted framework requiring developer installation, it has no specific consumer-facing abuse surface beyond standard ecommerce platform vulnerabilities. No documented vendor-specific abuse incidents.

Aimerce.description: Aimerce is an AI-powered, privacy-focused solution that utilises first-party data to support Shopify brands in a cookieless environment, enhancing data potential and increasing marketing revenue.
Aimerce.history: Aimerce was founded in early 2023 in San Francisco by Yiqi Wu, formerly an engineering manager at Facebook where she worked on Instagram Stories and Direct Messaging. The platform provides server-side tracking and first-party data solutions for Shopify ecommerce brands, helping merchants restore lost conversion data after iOS 14 privacy changes.
Aimerce.summary_abuse: Aimerce is a server-side tracking tool for ecommerce brands with no documented abuse signal. The privacy-first first-party data approach actually reduces third-party tracking exposure. No misuse campaigns have been documented.

Aimtell.description: Aimtell is a cloud-hosted marketing platform that allows digital marketers and businesses to deliver web-based push notifications.
Aimtell.history: Aimtell was founded in 2015 by DJ Swanepoel in Mission Viejo, California, as a web push notification platform. In September 2019, it was acquired by PushPROS. In February 2021, it was acquired again by Digital Media Solutions. Aimtell has processed over 80 billion push messages for over 20,000 websites.
Aimtell.summary_abuse: Web push notification platforms like Aimtell carry meaningful abuse risk because they enable mass unsolicited browser notifications that users may have been tricked into accepting through deceptive consent flows. Push notifications have been widely used for scam and malware-related campaigns. While Aimtell has AUP policies, push notification abuse is a persistent industry-wide challenge, and the platform enables mass-send to opted-in subscribers.

Aimy.description: Aimy is a salon management platform that provides scheduling tools, a virtual assistant, and calendar maintenance features to streamline salon operations.
Aimy.history: Aimy is a booking and appointment scheduling software serving salons and service businesses in the Netherlands and broader EU. Founded by Derk Jan Statema, the platform focuses on European markets and has partnered with Bird for WhatsApp appointment messaging capabilities.
Aimy.summary_abuse: Aimy is a salon and service booking platform with no documented abuse signal. It operates in the EU with GDPR compliance focus and serves local service businesses through a closed B2B model.

AINIRO.description: AINIRO is a provider of AI chatbots, AI agents, and custom AI solutions designed for various applications.
AINIRO.history: AINIRO was founded in 2023 by Thomas Hansen, a software developer with 25 years of experience, registered in Cyprus. The company offers the AINIRO Magic Cloud platform, which provides AI chatbot and low-code development capabilities powered by ChatGPT and OpenAI. It is a small company that received between 500K-1M EUR in early funding.
AINIRO.summary_abuse: AINIRO is a post-2023 AI chatbot and low-code platform with characteristics typical of AI-wrapper services built on top of OpenAI APIs. As a small, relatively new company, verification of claims and compliance posture is limited. No specific abuse incidents documented, but the generic AI chatbot SaaS space presents moderate risk for low-effort reselling.

Air360.description: Air360 is a technology company that specialises in performance-enhancing, mobile and ecommerce experience analytics.
Air360.history: Air360 was founded in 2016 by Florent Defontis, initially with headquarters in France. The platform provides digital experience analytics and CRO tooling for ecommerce teams. In October 2020, Air360 was acquired by Scalefast, a direct-to-consumer ecommerce platform.
Air360.summary_abuse: Air360 is a B2B digital analytics tool with no notable abuse signal. It helps ecommerce teams analyze user behavior and does not expose consumer-facing surfaces that would be exploited for phishing or fraud.

Airbridge.description: Airbridge is a marketing attribution tool that helps you measure and attribute growth to your marketing campaigns.
Airbridge.history: Airbridge was founded in 2015 in Seoul, South Korea, by AB180. The company provides mobile marketing attribution and deep linking analytics for app developers and marketers. Airbridge has joined Google's App Attribution Partner Program and Facebook's Mobile Measurement Partner Program, and serves over 150 advertisers primarily in Korea and Asia Pacific.
Airbridge.summary_abuse: Airbridge is a mobile attribution and analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves legitimate app marketing use cases and requires business account setup. No misuse campaigns have been documented.

Aircall.description: Aircall is a cloud-based phone system for customer support and sales teams.
Aircall.history: Aircall was founded in 2014 in Paris, France by Olivier Pailhes, Jonathan Anguelov, Xavier Durand, and Pierre-Baptiste Bechu. The company provides a cloud-based phone system integrated with CRM, helpdesk, and productivity tools. In June 2021, Aircall raised a $120M Series D led by Goldman Sachs Asset Management, reaching a unicorn valuation above $1 billion and becoming France's 16th unicorn. By 2022, the company surpassed $100M in ARR. Total funding exceeded $226M.
Aircall.summary_abuse: No significant abuse incidents have been reported for Aircall. The platform is a business phone system used by legitimate sales and support teams, with SOC2 Type 2 and ISO 27001 certifications. Aircall handles call recordings and customer communication data, which it processes under GDPR compliance with appropriate data processing agreements.

Airdata.description: Airdata is a ticket booking system for agents in Vietnam, offering unique features to maximize benefits for businesses in managing and selling flight tickets.
Airdata.history: AirData UAV was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in El Dorado Hills, California. The company built the largest online drone fleet management and real-time flight streaming platform, serving over 260,000 users in 232 countries with more than 27 million flights logged. AirData integrates with DJI and other major drone platforms.
Airdata.summary_abuse: AirData is a drone fleet management platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves commercial drone operators and enterprises for legitimate flight tracking and analytics. The platform does not expose consumer-facing surfaces that would attract phishing or fraud campaigns.

Airee.description: Airee offers scalable web hosting solutions tailored for internet shops and websites, with enhanced performance, DDoS protection, high availability, and detailed speed and security analytics.
Airee.history: Airee is a Russian website acceleration and availability optimization service associated with the WEBO Group. The platform offers website caching, speed optimization, and high-availability infrastructure services, distributing content across nodes in USA, Canada, Europe, Russia, and Asia.
Airee.summary_abuse: Airee is a niche Russian website acceleration service with limited public documentation. Its small scale and regional focus result in no notable documented abuse patterns, though its Russian infrastructure may present geopolitical risk considerations for some users.

Airform.description: Airform is a functional HTML forms for front-end developers.
Airform.history: Airform is a lightweight tool providing functional HTML form handling for front-end developers, hosted as an open-source project. It appears to have limited recent activity.
Airform.summary_abuse: Airform has no notable abuse signal. It is a simple HTML form utility library with no hosted content service that would attract fraudsters.

AirRobe.description: AirRobe partners with brands and retailers to power the circular fashion economy.
AirRobe.history: AirRobe was founded in 2020 by Hannon Comazzetto in Australia as a circular fashion recommerce platform. The company pioneered Circular Wardrobe Technology that integrates directly into online retail stores, allowing customers to save purchases for later resale, rental, or recycling. AirRobe launched a US headquarters and has partnered with leading Australian fashion retailers.
AirRobe.summary_abuse: AirRobe is a recommerce platform for fashion brands with no notable abuse signal. It enables legitimate secondhand fashion transactions and is used by established Australian and international retailers. The marketplace model requires identity verification for transactions.

Airship.description: Airship is an American company that provides marketing and branding services. Airship allows companies to generate custom messages to consumers via push notifications, SMS messaging, and similar, and provides customer analytics services.
Airship.history: Airship was founded in 2009 in Portland, Oregon, as Urban Airship by Steven Osborn, Scott Kveton, Adam Lowry, and Michael Richardson. The company sent the first commercial push notification and pioneered mobile app messaging. It raised $25M in 2013 from Foundry Group, Intel Capital, True Ventures, and Verizon. In June 2019, Urban Airship rebranded as Airship to reflect its expanded mobile engagement platform beyond push notifications.
Airship.summary_abuse: Airship is a mobile messaging platform used by enterprise brands. Mobile push notification infrastructure can be abused if app developer accounts are compromised, but Airship works directly with verified enterprise clients rather than offering self-serve anonymous access. The platform has documented AUP and security practices.

Airship CRM.description: Airship CRM is a hospitality-focused platform that centralizes data, enables tailored email communication, and enhances visit frequency.
Airship CRM.history: Airship CRM is a UK-based data-driven loyalty CRM platform built specifically for the hospitality industry. It provides customer engagement, loyalty programs, and marketing automation for restaurant groups and hospitality brands. The company is separate from the US-based Airship mobile messaging company.
Airship CRM.summary_abuse: Airship CRM is a UK hospitality loyalty CRM with no notable abuse signal. It serves verified hospitality businesses and collects customer loyalty data within a B2B context. The platform does not expose anonymous content hosting or mass-email surfaces that would be exploited for fraud.

Airtable.description: Airtable is a low-code platform for building collaborative apps.
Airtable.history: Airtable was founded in 2012 by Howie Liu, Andrew Ofstad, and Emmett Nicholas in San Francisco. The company launched an invite-only beta in 2014 and grew into one of the leading no-code database and collaborative workflow platforms. It raised over $1.36B in funding including $270M Series E in 2021, reaching a $11B valuation. Airtable serves over 500,000 organizations.
Airtable.summary_abuse: Airtable is a widely-trusted no-code database platform with minimal abuse exposure. Some phishing kits have used Airtable's free tier to create publicly accessible data stores for collecting stolen credentials, a pattern seen across many free-tier platforms. Airtable has AUP enforcement and abuse reporting mechanisms, and this type of misuse represents a small fraction of overall use.

AiSpeed.description: AiSpeed is a shopify app focused on improving site speed.
AiSpeed.history: AiSpeed is a Shopify speed optimization app founded in 2021 in London. It is an unfunded, small-team company offering AI-based performance improvements including conversion management, SEO, and website management for Shopify merchants.
AiSpeed.summary_abuse: AiSpeed is a small Shopify app with no documented abuse signal. As a niche speed optimization tool for merchants, it does not expose consumer-facing surfaces that would attract phishing or spam abuse.

AiTrillion.description: AiTrillion is a marketing automation platform for ecommerce, offering tools for email campaigns, customer segmentation, loyalty programs, and product recommendations.
AiTrillion.history: AiTrillion was founded in 2018 by Sachin Dhanotiya and is a bootstrapped ecommerce marketing automation platform for Shopify merchants. It combines 11+ marketing modules including email, SMS, loyalty programs, and product reviews into a single platform. The company reached $3.8M ARR by 2025 and is profitable.
AiTrillion.summary_abuse: AiTrillion is a Shopify marketing automation platform with email and SMS send capabilities, which can theoretically be misused for spam if accounts are compromised. The platform targets small merchants and has standard AUP policies. No notable abuse campaigns have been documented.

AitThemes.description: AitThemes is a customisable theme suitable for business, ecommerce, content, and directory websites.
AitThemes.history: AitThemes was founded in 2009 in Bratislava, Slovakia, as a WordPress theme development company. The company specializes in directory and business WordPress themes and has created over 70 themes and 30 plugins. It earned over $1M on ThemeForest marketplace and is known for its CitadelaWP directory theme.
AitThemes.summary_abuse: AitThemes sells self-hosted WordPress themes with no notable abuse signal. As a theme vendor rather than a hosting platform, it does not directly expose surfaces for phishing or spam. Users install themes on their own hosting environments.

Aiva.description: Aiva is a real estate lead conversion platform that qualifies and engages leads 24/7.
Aiva.history: AIVA (Artificial Intelligence Virtual Artist) was founded in February 2016 in Luxembourg by Pierre Barreau, Denis Shtefan, Arnaud Decker, and Vincent Barreau. In 2017, AIVA became the first AI recognized as a composer by SACEM, the French copyright society. The platform can compose music in over 250 styles and is used for soundtracks in film, games, and commercials.
Aiva.summary_abuse: AIVA is an AI music composition platform with no notable abuse signal. Copyright and intellectual property concerns around AI-generated music are ongoing industry debates, but AIVA itself is used by legitimate creators and media producers. No fraud or phishing abuse campaigns have been documented.

Aivo.description: Aivo is a conversational AI platform offering omnichannel tools and live agent solutions to automate customer experiences.
Aivo.history: Aivo was founded in 2012 in Buenos Aires, Argentina, as an AI-driven customer service chatbot platform. The company became a leader in the Latin American market, serving enterprises like BBVA, Visa, and Movistar across nine countries. In 2023, Aivo was acquired by Engageware, a US customer engagement company.
Aivo.summary_abuse: Aivo is a B2B AI customer service platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves enterprise clients in regulated industries like banking and telecommunications, and does not expose consumer-facing surfaces that would typically be exploited for phishing or fraud.

Ajax.NET Professional.description: Ajax.NET Professional is a legacy framework that generates JavaScript proxies to call .NET server-side methods via AJAX.
Ajax.NET Professional.history: Ajax.NET Professional (AjaxPro) was created by German developer Michael Schwarz in 2005, making it one of the first AJAX frameworks for Microsoft ASP.NET. It enabled web server method calls from client-side JavaScript before Microsoft released its own AJAX library. The project is available on GitHub but is largely superseded by modern frameworks.
Ajax.NET Professional.summary_abuse: Ajax.NET Professional is an early ASP.NET AJAX library with no abuse signal. It is a backend library that runs within the hosting application's server and does not expose consumer-facing attack surfaces.

Akamai.description: Akamai is global content delivery network (CDN) services provider for media and software delivery, and cloud security solutions.
Akamai.history: Akamai was incorporated on August 20, 1998, by MIT professor Tom Leighton and student Daniel Lewin, with a team that won MIT's $50K entrepreneurship competition. The company pioneered content delivery network technology and grew into the world's largest CDN. Daniel Lewin was killed on September 11, 2001. Akamai is publicly traded on NASDAQ and remains a dominant internet infrastructure provider.
Akamai.summary_abuse: Akamai is a foundational CDN and internet infrastructure provider. Like all large CDN providers, some fraction of malicious content transits Akamai's network, but the company actively operates security products (Bot Manager, WAF, DDoS protection) and has documented takedown processes. Akamai actively fights abuse rather than enabling it.

Akamai Bot Manager.description: Akamai Bot Manager detect bots using device fingerprinting bot signatures.
Akamai Bot Manager.history: Akamai Bot Manager is a security product developed by Akamai Technologies, which was founded in 1998.
