The product uses supervised and unsupervised machine learning to identify and manage bot traffic, protecting websites and APIs from credential stuffing, inventory grabbing, scraping, and DDoS attacks. It is part of Akamai's broader Cloud Security product portfolio.
Akamai Bot Manager.summary_abuse: Akamai Bot Manager is a defensive security product designed to detect and block abusive bot traffic, not enable it. No abuse signal documented for this product itself.

Akamai Connected Cloud.description: Akamai Connected Cloud is a distributed cloud platform that combines core compute, edge services, and security to run applications at global scale.
Akamai Connected Cloud.history: Akamai Connected Cloud is Akamai Technologies' cloud computing and CDN platform, launched following Akamai's acquisition of Linode in 2022. Akamai, founded in 1998, expanded from pure CDN into full cloud services by integrating Linode's developer-focused cloud infrastructure. The combined offering provides compute, storage, networking, and security services globally.
Akamai Connected Cloud.summary_abuse: Akamai Connected Cloud is a general-purpose cloud platform that, like all cloud providers, can host both legitimate and malicious workloads. Akamai has strong AUP enforcement, active abuse reporting processes, and security products. Its enterprise focus and strict compliance posture reduce but do not eliminate the risk of hosting malicious content.

Akamai mPulse.description: Akamai mPulse is a real user monitoring (RUM) solution that enables companies to monitor, find, and fix website and application performance issues.
Akamai mPulse.history: Akamai mPulse is a real user monitoring (RUM) solution developed by Akamai Technologies, which was founded in 1998. The product collects real-world user performance data and maps it to business outcomes like revenue and conversion rates. It uses the BoomerangJS open-source library for beacon collection and provides 200+ performance and business metrics per session.
Akamai mPulse.summary_abuse: Akamai mPulse is a real user monitoring product that collects web performance data from visitors' browsers. As a Akamai enterprise product, it is deployed exclusively by verified enterprise clients with contractual data handling obligations. The data collection is focused on performance metrics rather than advertising tracking.

Akamai Web Application Protector.description: Akamai Web Application Protector is designed for companies looking for a more automated approach to web application firewall (WAF) and distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) security.
Akamai Web Application Protector.history: Akamai Web Application Protector is a WAF and DDoS protection product built on Akamai Technologies' CDN infrastructure, which was founded in 1998. The product protects websites from application-layer attacks including SQL injection, XSS, and DDoS. It is part of Akamai's edge security suite.
Akamai Web Application Protector.summary_abuse: Akamai Web Application Protector is a defensive security product designed to protect websites from attacks. It does not enable abuse and is used exclusively in defensive configurations by verified enterprise clients.

Akaunting.description: Akaunting is a free and online accounting software.
Akaunting.history: Akaunting is an open-source accounting software built with Laravel, targeting small businesses and freelancers. It was founded in 2017. The project is available as both a self-hosted option and a cloud SaaS, with over 300 integrations through Zapier. The company has headquarters in Germany.
Akaunting.summary_abuse: Akaunting is an open-source accounting platform with no notable abuse signal. As accounting software primarily used by small businesses, it does not expose consumer-facing surfaces for phishing or fraud.

Akavita.description: Akavita is a Russian-based service offering analytics and advertising solutions.
Akavita.history: Akavita is a Russian-based web analytics and advertising service. Limited public history available.
Akavita.summary_abuse: Akavita is a Russian analytics and advertising service. Russia-based ad tech services can carry elevated risk related to tracking without adequate consent mechanisms and potential data transfer concerns under GDPR. No specific abuse incidents documented, but geographic origin warrants consideration for data handling.

Akero.description: Akero is a platform that provides marketing and admissions technology designed for educational institutions.
Akero.history: Akero is a marketing automation and student recruitment platform founded in 2019, built by Net Natives. The platform uses AI to improve digital advertising performance for higher education institutions, helping universities improve student enrollment through predictive ad targeting and lead management.
Akero.summary_abuse: Akero is a B2B marketing platform serving the higher education sector with no notable abuse signal. The platform focuses on student recruitment marketing and does not expose anonymous content hosting or mass-send features to unverified users.

Akia.description: Akia is a platform offering AI-powered communication tools designed to streamline messaging and guest interactions within the hospitality industry.
Akia.history: Akia was founded in 2018 by former Facebook engineers with machine learning backgrounds. The company is headquartered in San Francisco and builds an AI-powered guest messaging platform for hotels, enabling contactless check-in, SMS/WhatsApp communication, and automated guest service. Akia raised $6M in 2023 to grow its automated customer engagement platform.
Akia.summary_abuse: Akia is a B2B hospitality messaging platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves hotels and accommodation businesses through verified business accounts and does not expose anonymous SMS or messaging surfaces that could be exploited for spam.

Akilli Ticaret.description: Akilli Ticaret is an all-in-one ecommerce platform from Turkey.
Akilli Ticaret.history: Akilli Ticaret (Akilli Ticaret, meaning Smart Commerce in Turkish) has been operating in the Turkish ecommerce sector since 2011. The platform provides ERP-integrated ecommerce solutions with marketplace integrations for Turkish merchants, targeting small and medium-sized businesses that want to sell online.
Akilli Ticaret.summary_abuse: Akilli Ticaret is a Turkish SMB ecommerce platform with no notable documented abuse signal. It serves verified Turkish merchants and operates as a regional platform with limited international exposure.

Akinon.description: Akinon is a cloud-based headless commerce platform with an integrated application suite including omnichannel and marketplace capabilities, mobile and in-store solutions, and an OMS.
Akinon.history: Akinon was founded in 2016 in Istanbul, Turkey, as a cloud-native headless digital commerce platform. The company serves about half of Turkey's top 100 retailers including Lacoste, Marks & Spencer, Sephora, and Gap. Akinon raised $25.7M from investors including Actera Group, Revo Capital, and SmartFin, and reached $59.4M in revenue in 2024.
Akinon.summary_abuse: Akinon is a B2B ecommerce platform serving major Turkish and global retailers with no notable abuse signal. The enterprise-focused model with known brand clients reduces the risk of the platform being used for fraudulent storefronts.

Akismet.description: Akismet is a service that filters spam from comments, trackbacks, and contact form messages.
Akismet.history: Akismet was launched on October 25, 2005, by Matt Mullenweg as a comment spam filtering service for WordPress. It was created so Mullenweg's mother could blog safely. Akismet is owned by Automattic and is included by default in all WordPress installations. It has blocked over 500 billion spam comments since launch and has over 5 million active installations.
Akismet.summary_abuse: Akismet is an anti-spam service that actively fights abuse rather than enabling it. It uses a global spam database to filter comment spam on WordPress and other platforms. The service itself does not expose attack surfaces for fraud or phishing.

Akka HTTP.history: Akka HTTP was first previewed in June 2014 as part of the Akka toolkit, built on top of Akka Streams and the Akka actor model. The toolkit was created by Jonas Boner (creator of Akka) and the company Lightbend (formerly Typesafe), co-founded by Scala creator Martin Odersky. In September 2022, Lightbend changed Akka's license from Apache 2.0 to Business Source License (BSL), requiring a commercial license for large enterprise production use, causing controversy in the Scala community.
Akka HTTP.summary_abuse: Akka HTTP is a server-side Scala/Java HTTP library with no abuse signal. It runs within applications on servers controlled by developers and has no consumer-facing attack surface.

Aklamio.description: Aklamio is a solution for enterprise level referral marketing and customer incentivisation.
Aklamio.history: Aklamio was founded in 2011 in Berlin, Germany, by Robert Wetzker, Holger Grosse, and Marcus Suiter. The company built Europe's leading customer incentive and referral marketing platform, serving brands like Virgin Media, Vodafone, PayPal, and Endesa. It was ranked 2nd fastest-growing technology company in Germany in 2019 and named one of Europe's fastest-growing companies by the Financial Times.
Aklamio.summary_abuse: Aklamio is a B2B referral marketing platform with low abuse risk. While referral program mechanics can theoretically be exploited for referral fraud, Aklamio serves verified enterprise clients with contractual terms. No notable systematic fraud campaigns have been documented against Aklamio's platform.

AkoCommerce.description: AkoCommerce is a Shopify partner based in Taiwan that provides website planning and construction, as well as ecommerce store opening consultation services.
AkoCommerce.history: AkoCommerce is a Shopify Partner company based in Taipei, Taiwan, that became a Shopify Partner in 2015. The company specializes in helping merchants build and manage Shopify stores with localized services for Taiwan and Southeast Asian markets, including Taiwan convenience store pickup integrations.
AkoCommerce.summary_abuse: AkoCommerce is a Taiwan-based Shopify consultancy with no notable abuse signal. It serves merchants building legitimate Shopify stores and does not expose consumer-facing surfaces that would facilitate fraud.

Aksara CMS.description: Aksara CMS is a CodeIgniter based CRUD toolkit.
Aksara CMS.history: Aksara CMS is an Indonesian open-source CMS and CRUD toolkit built on CodeIgniter by developer Abydahana. The project focuses on making web application development easier for government, organizations, and companies adapting to digital transformation. It supports both headless RESTful API and traditional browser-based output modes.
Aksara CMS.summary_abuse: Aksara CMS is a self-hosted open-source CMS with no notable abuse signal. As a developer toolkit installed on users' own servers, it does not expose managed hosting surfaces that would be exploited for phishing or spam at scale.

Albacross.description: Albacross is a lead generation and account intelligence platform. It helps marketing and sales teams identify their ideal customers visiting their website and gives them the insights they need to generate more qualified leads, make prospecting more efficient and close more deals.
Albacross.history: Albacross was founded in 2014 in Stockholm, Sweden as a B2B lead generation platform that identifies anonymous website visitors by matching IP addresses to company records. It raised approximately $4.83M in funding and grew to serve thousands of customers globally. The platform integrates with CRMs like Salesforce and HubSpot to help sales teams identify prospect companies visiting their websites without requiring those visitors to fill in any forms.
Albacross.summary_abuse: Albacross operates in a grey area of privacy by identifying anonymous website visitors using IP-to-company matching without explicit consent from those visitors. This practice has raised GDPR concerns from privacy advocates, as it processes personal data (IP addresses tied to individuals) without direct consent. The company claims GDPR compliance but the core tracking functionality is controversial under European privacy law.

Alboom Proof.description: Alboom Proof is a platform that provides client photo galleries, photo sales, and proofing services in one location, enabling photographers and visual artists to deliver their work.
Alboom Proof.history: Alboom Proof is a photo and video delivery tool for photographers, developed by Alboom, a Brazilian company founded in April 2015 in Sao Paulo by wedding photographer Rafael Bigarelli along with Arquimedes Ferreira and Marcelo Tesser. The platform grew from Prosite, an online portfolio product, and expanded to include proofing tools for client photo selection. By 2021, Alboom reached 100,000 clients across Latin America and beyond.
Alboom Proof.summary_abuse: No significant abuse incidents or security issues have been reported for Alboom Proof. The platform is a niche photography SaaS primarily serving Latin American photographers and studios. Formal compliance certifications are not publicly listed, but the platform has a clean public record and serves a professional photography clientele.

Alboom Prosite.description: Alboom Prosite is a website and landing page builder for businesses.
Alboom Prosite.history: Alboom Prosite is the website builder product of Alboom, launched as the company's first product in April 2015 in Sao Paulo, Brazil. Created by wedding photographer Rafael Bigarelli with co-founders Arquimedes Ferreira and Marcelo Tesser, Prosite was designed to give photographers a professional online portfolio and website. The platform features drag-and-drop editing, SEO tools, and photography-specific galleries, and served as the foundation upon which Alboom expanded its suite of tools.
Alboom Prosite.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents or security concerns have been publicly reported for Alboom Prosite. The platform is a niche website builder targeting photographers and creatives, primarily in Latin America. Formal compliance certifications such as SOC2 or GDPR are not publicly advertised, reflecting its focus on the regional market.

Alchemer Mobile.description: Alchemer Mobile, formerly known as Apptentive, is a tool optimized for mobile, consolidating app-store ratings, reviews, in-app customer feedback, emotion data, and key metrics in one dashboard.
Alchemer Mobile.history: Originally founded as Apptentive in 2011 in Seattle, Washington by Robi Ganguly, Sky Kelsey, Mike Saffitz, and Andrew Wooster, the company graduated from Techstars Seattle in 2012. Apptentive built a mobile SDK that enables apps to collect in-app feedback and conduct surveys, serving hundreds of millions of devices for major brands. In January 2023, KKR-backed Alchemer acquired Apptentive and rebranded it as Alchemer Mobile, integrating it into Alchemer's broader customer experience management platform.
Alchemer Mobile.summary_abuse: No significant abuse incidents have been reported for Alchemer Mobile or its predecessor Apptentive. The SDK is used by legitimate app developers to collect user feedback in-app. The platform collects user sentiment data and responses, which carries inherent privacy considerations, but no data misuse incidents have been publicly reported.

AlertifyJS.description: AlertifyJS is a javascript framework for developing browser dialogs and notifications.
AlertifyJS.history: AlertifyJS is a JavaScript framework created by Mohammad Younes (MohammadYounes) for building browser dialogs and notification toasts. The library provides a clean API for alerts, confirm dialogs, and notification messages, styled with CSS. It was released on GitHub and quickly became a popular alternative to native browser dialogs. The project is licensed under GPLv3. A separate fork called alertify.js exists under the MIT license maintained by the alertifyjs organization.
AlertifyJS.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for AlertifyJS. It is a straightforward frontend UI utility library with no server-side components or data collection. The GPLv3 license requires derivative works to remain open source, which may limit use in some commercial products but does not create any abuse concerns.

Alexa Certified Site Metrics.description: Alexa Certified Site Metrics is an analytics service wich monitors and analyses web traffic and can be used to keep track of user behavior.
Alexa Certified Site Metrics.history: Alexa Internet was founded in 1996 by Brewster Kahle and Bruce Gilliat, initially as a web navigation service. Amazon acquired it in 1999 for $250 million in stock. The service provided website traffic rankings and analytics, including the Certified Site Metrics program where webmasters could embed JavaScript on their pages to allow direct traffic measurement. Alexa became a widely-used benchmark for website popularity. Amazon announced the shutdown of alexa.com in December 2021 and the service was fully retired on May 1, 2022.
Alexa Certified Site Metrics.summary_abuse: Alexa Internet itself had no notable abuse incidents. Its Certified Site Metrics tag was a legitimate traffic measurement script. Privacy concerns existed because the Alexa toolbar collected extensive browsing data from users who installed it, which raised questions about data collection practices. The service has been fully shut down since May 2022 and no new implementations are possible.

Alfright.description: Alfright is a service that ensures GDPR compliance for websites, enhancing privacy standards.
Alfright.history: Alfright is a small cookie consent and GDPR compliance tool for websites, primarily known in the Austrian market. It is categorized by Wappalyzer under cookie compliance technologies. The tool provides website owners with cookie consent banners and privacy management functionality to meet GDPR, ePR, and similar data protection requirements. Limited public information is available about the company's founding history and ownership structure.
Alfright.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Alfright. The tool is a cookie consent solution designed to help websites comply with privacy regulations. As a compliance tool, it is expected to handle user consent data responsibly, though independent security audits have not been publicly documented.

Algolia.description: Algolia offers a hosted web search product delivering real-time results.
Algolia.history: Algolia was founded in 2012 in Paris, France by Nicolas Dessaigne and Julien Lemoine, and later moved headquarters to San Francisco. The company built a search-as-a-service API that enables developers to add fast, relevant search to websites and apps without managing infrastructure. Algolia graduated from Y Combinator, raised $18.3M Series A in 2015, $53M Series B in 2017, and a $150M Series D in 2021 at a $2.25B valuation. It raised a total of $335M and serves thousands of companies globally.
Algolia.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Algolia. The platform is a trusted search infrastructure provider used by major brands globally. Algolia is SOC2 Type II and ISO 27001 certified and publicly documents its GDPR compliance. The platform is generally regarded as a high-trust, developer-friendly search tool.

Algolia DocSearch.description: Algolia DocSearch is a search widget specifically designed for documentation websites.
Algolia DocSearch.history: Algolia DocSearch is an open-source documentation search solution created and maintained by Algolia. It crawls documentation websites, indexes the content into Algolia, and provides a pre-built search UI component. The service is free for open-source projects and technical blogs that display the Algolia logo. DocSearch powers the search on major documentation sites including Bootstrap, React, Vue, and hundreds more open-source projects.
Algolia DocSearch.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Algolia DocSearch. The library is maintained by a well-funded, reputable company with strong security practices. By requiring the Algolia logo on free tier implementations, the platform maintains quality control over its use. The project is widely trusted across the developer community.

Ali Reviews.description: Ali reviews is a shopify app to collect reviews from customers.
Ali Reviews.history: Ali Reviews is a Shopify product reviews app developed by FireApps, a Vietnamese Shopify app company founded in 2016 with over 130 team members. The app was launched on March 24, 2017, and became FireApps' first major product. It enables Shopify merchants to import reviews from AliExpress, Amazon, Temu, and other platforms, and later expanded with AI-powered features. The app reached 40,000+ merchants and was later rebranded as Kudosi.
Ali Reviews.summary_abuse: Ali Reviews has faced concerns about its core use case of importing third-party reviews from AliExpress and Amazon, which can enable merchants to display potentially unverified or misleading reviews on their product pages. This practice may mislead consumers, and Shopify has policies around review authenticity. No major security incidents have been reported, but the review importation functionality occupies a grey area of consumer trust.

Alia.description: Alia is a Shopify app to design Email and SMS sign-up pop-up units
Alia.history: Alia (Alia Learn) is a Shopify-exclusive AI-powered popup builder founded in 2023 by three founders in their 20s, including Shaan Arora who graduated in May 2023 right after launch. Built as a bootstrapped startup, the company reached $4M ARR in under 2 years and grew to nearly 2,000 Shopify brands. Alia uses AI and machine learning trained on real merchant data to optimize popup performance, capturing email and SMS subscribers with personalized experiences.
Alia.summary_abuse: No security incidents have been reported for Alia. The platform collects email addresses and SMS phone numbers for marketing purposes, which is a standard e-commerce practice. The popup and list-building functionality is typical of marketing SaaS tools. No formal compliance certifications are publicly advertised for this early-stage startup.

Alibaba Cloud CDN.description: Alibaba Cloud CDN is a global network of servers designed to deliver high-performance, low-latency content to users around the world. It is a cloud-based service provided by Alibaba Cloud, a subsidiary of the Alibaba Group, that enables businesses to accelerate the delivery of their web content, including images, videos, and static files, to end-users.
Alibaba Cloud CDN.history: Alibaba Cloud CDN is a content delivery network service offered by Alibaba Cloud (Aliyun), the cloud computing arm of Alibaba Group founded in September 2009. The CDN service operates over 3,200 points of presence worldwide, with 2,300+ in mainland China and 900+ across 70+ countries. It is the largest CDN in Asia and powers high-traffic scenarios including Alibaba's own e-commerce platforms during major events like Singles Day.
Alibaba Cloud CDN.summary_abuse: Alibaba Cloud CDN is a legitimate enterprise infrastructure service. However, due to Alibaba's Chinese ownership and China's national security laws requiring data sharing with the government upon request, it raises concerns for users handling sensitive data destined for non-Chinese markets. No specific CDN abuse incidents have been reported, but Chinese government data access requirements remain a privacy consideration.

Alibaba Cloud Object Storage Service.description: Alibaba Cloud Object Storage Service (OSS) is a cloud-based object storage service provided by Alibaba Cloud, which allows users to store and access large amounts of data in the cloud.
Alibaba Cloud Object Storage Service.history: Alibaba Cloud Object Storage Service (OSS) is a scalable cloud object storage service offered by Alibaba Cloud, founded in 2009 in Hangzhou, China. OSS is the storage backbone for Alibaba's e-commerce platforms and has over 10 years of operational history. Compatible with AWS S3 API standards, it provides enterprise features including encryption, versioning, lifecycle management, and global replication. It is one of the largest object storage services in Asia.
Alibaba Cloud Object Storage Service.summary_abuse: Alibaba Cloud OSS is a legitimate enterprise storage service. As a Chinese-owned cloud service, it is subject to China's national security laws which may require disclosing stored data to Chinese authorities upon request. This creates geopolitical concerns for international customers storing sensitive data. No specific data breach or misuse incidents have been publicly documented for OSS.

Alibaba Cloud Verification Code.description: Alibaba Cloud Verification Code is a security feature provided by Alibaba Cloud to help protect user accounts from unauthorised access.
Alibaba Cloud Verification Code.history: Alibaba Cloud Verification Code (also known as Alibaba Cloud Captcha) is a bot-detection and human verification service provided by Alibaba Cloud, the cloud division of Alibaba Group founded in 2009. The service offers various CAPTCHA types including slider, puzzle, visual reasoning, and one-click verification. Captcha 2.0 was released as a more advanced version with a risk analysis engine. The service primarily targets the Chinese market and Alibaba Cloud customers globally.
Alibaba Cloud Verification Code.summary_abuse: Alibaba Cloud Verification Code is a legitimate CAPTCHA service. As a Chinese-owned service, it is subject to China's data laws which may allow government access to verification logs and behavioral data collected during CAPTCHA challenges. Privacy-sensitive applications outside China should consider this geopolitical risk when choosing this service over alternatives.

Alimama.description: Alimama is a data-driven marketing technology platform that enables businesses to optimize campaigns and audience engagement through advanced analytics.
Alimama.history: Alimama is Alibaba Group's online marketing technology platform, launched in November 2007. It provides digital advertising services to merchants selling on Taobao, Tmall, and other Alibaba marketplaces. The platform offers performance-based advertising products such as Taobao's P4P (pay-for-performance) system and display advertising. Alimama is a core revenue driver for Alibaba Group, processing billions of ad impressions daily across Alibaba's massive Chinese e-commerce ecosystem.
Alimama.summary_abuse: Alimama embeds extensive tracking and behavioral advertising infrastructure across Alibaba's platforms. As a Chinese advertising platform, it is subject to China's data laws and surveillance requirements. The platform has been associated with advertising tracking technologies that raise significant privacy concerns for non-Chinese visitors who encounter Alibaba marketplace pages. Regulatory risk is elevated for European and US markets.

Alinea.description: Alinea is a Git-based content management system designed specifically for integration with Next.js projects.
Alinea.history: Alinea is an open-source headless CMS built with JavaScript/TypeScript where content schema is defined in code and content lives alongside source files in the repository. The project was first publicly shown on Hacker News in September 2022, making it an early-stage open-source CMS. It is designed for the Jamstack ecosystem and allows content to be versioned and managed through Git workflows. The project is available on GitHub under the MIT license.
Alinea.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Alinea CMS. It is a self-hosted open-source tool with no external data collection or tracking. Content stays in the developer's own repository. The project is still relatively new and niche, with limited adoption but no known security issues.

Alive5.description: Alive5 is a unified inbox for teams, handling SMS, web chat, and social conversations. It was formerly known as Website Alive.
Alive5.history: Alive5 was founded in 2017 in Houston, Texas by Glenn Gutierrez and Dustin Yu, originally as Alive Chat System. The company provides a unified customer engagement platform combining SMS, live chat, and social messaging with AI-powered automation. It achieved early traction in sports teams' websites before expanding to universities, hotels, and businesses. The startup was selected for Comcast NBCUniversal LIFT Labs accelerator and has grown to over 800 customers.
Alive5.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Alive5. The platform holds SOC2 Type 2 and GDPR certifications, indicating a commitment to security and data protection. As a customer messaging platform, it handles personal contact information and communications, which it manages within standard enterprise data protection practices.

All in One Accessibility.description: All in One Accessibility is a website accessibility widget developed by Skynet Technologies to enhance compliance with WCAG 2.0, 2.1, 2.2, and ADA accessibility standards.
All in One Accessibility.history: All in One Accessibility is a WordPress plugin developed by Skynet Technologies that provides an AI-powered accessibility widget for websites to meet ADA, WCAG 2.1/2.2, EAA, and Section 508 compliance standards. The plugin offers 70+ accessibility features including screen reader support, voice navigation, color blindness modes, and 190+ language support. It is available on WordPress.org as a freemium product.
All in One Accessibility.summary_abuse: No major abuse incidents have been reported for All in One Accessibility. Accessibility overlay plugins like this have been criticized by the accessibility community for not providing genuine accessibility improvements and sometimes introducing new barriers. The plugin embeds third-party JavaScript on websites, which can collect data about user interactions. Some accessibility experts argue overlay solutions are insufficient for true WCAG compliance.

All in One SEO.description: All in One SEO optimizes a WordPress website and its content for search engines.
All in One SEO.history: All in One SEO (AIOSEO) is the original WordPress SEO plugin, first released in 2007, making it older than Yoast SEO. It was initially created as a simple meta tags plugin and grew into a full-featured SEO suite. The plugin gained renewed investment and development in the 2010s, adding features like XML sitemaps, schema markup, breadcrumbs, and social media integration. It is one of the most-installed WordPress plugins, with over 3 million active installations.
All in One SEO.summary_abuse: No significant abuse incidents have been reported for All in One SEO. It is a well-established, widely trusted WordPress SEO plugin with a long track record since 2007. The plugin is self-hosted on the user's WordPress installation, minimizing third-party data exposure. A free version is available on WordPress.org.

All in One SEO Pack.description: All in One SEO plugin optimizes WordPress website and its content for search engines.
All in One SEO Pack.history: All in One SEO Pack (AIOSEO) is the original WordPress SEO plugin, first released in 2007, predating Yoast SEO by several years. Originally a simple meta tags plugin, it evolved into a comprehensive SEO suite with XML sitemaps, schema markup, social media integration, and breadcrumbs. Now marketed as AIOSEO, it is one of the most-installed WordPress plugins with over 3 million active installations and serves millions of websites globally.
All in One SEO Pack.summary_abuse: No significant abuse incidents have been reported for All in One SEO Pack. It is a well-established, widely trusted WordPress SEO plugin with a long track record since 2007. The plugin is self-hosted on the user's WordPress installation and does not send user data to third-party servers in its standard operation.

ALL-INKL.description: ALL-INKL is a German-based web hosting provider that promises to offer high-performance services for fair prices.
ALL-INKL.history: ALL-INKL.COM was founded in 2000 and is operated by Neue Medien Münnich, headquartered in Friedersdorf, Saxony, Germany. The company is one of the largest German web hosting providers, managing over 700,000 customer websites. It operates from a high-availability data center in Dresden and runs on 100% hydroelectric green energy.
ALL-INKL.summary_abuse: ALL-INKL is a German shared web hosting provider with GDPR-compliant data handling. As a shared hosting provider, co-tenancy spam or phishing hosting is a known industry risk, but ALL-INKL's German customer base and strict terms of service limit abuse. No major abuse incidents documented.

Allbookable.description: Allbookable is an online booking solution that enables users to book services through a digital platform.
Allbookable.history: Allbookable is a small online booking and scheduling platform detected by web technology scanners. Limited public information is available about the company. It appears to be a UK-based online booking tool targeting small businesses needing appointment and reservation management functionality.
Allbookable.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Allbookable. The platform is a niche booking tool with very limited public presence and documentation. No formal compliance certifications are publicly known.

AllClients.description: AllClients is an all-in-one platform that integrates contact management and marketing automation.
AllClients.history: AllClients was developed around 2005 by Jeff Shamus and Todd Binder, two veteran technology professionals from Canada. The company specializes in simple CRM software with built-in email marketing for small businesses, and has been dedicated to helping small businesses build stronger customer relationships for approximately 20 years. The platform provides contact management, email campaigns, and follow-up automation in a straightforward interface designed for non-technical users.
AllClients.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for AllClients. The platform is a straightforward small-business CRM with email marketing features. It has been operating for two decades without notable security or privacy incidents. Formal certifications such as SOC2 or GDPR compliance are not publicly advertised.

Alli.description: Alli is artificial intelligence for search engine optimisation.
Alli.history: Alli AI is an SEO automation tool founded in 2017, headquartered in Rehoboth Beach, Delaware, USA. The platform provides bulk SEO optimization capabilities allowing agencies and businesses to deploy SEO changes across large numbers of pages simultaneously without developer involvement. It serves over 26,000 companies and focuses on automation features like bulk meta-tag editing, A/B testing for SEO changes, and schema markup deployment.
Alli.summary_abuse: No significant abuse incidents have been reported for Alli AI. The platform is an SEO tool used by agencies and businesses to automate on-page optimizations. Automated SEO tools can be misused for spammy or black-hat SEO practices, but Alli AI serves a legitimate business audience with no notable history of enforcement actions or abuse associations.

Alliance Auth.description: Alliance Auth is an access management platform designed for Eve Online groups. It controls access to applications and services based on in-game memberships.
Alliance Auth.history: Alliance Auth is an open-source authentication system built specifically for EVE Online player organizations (corporations and alliances) to manage access to third-party services like Discord, Mumble, and forums. Based on the original auth system credited to Nikdoof, the project grew into a community-maintained framework available on GitHub. It uses Django and supports EVE SSO (Single Sign-On) to authenticate players and manage role-based access to group services.
Alliance Auth.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Alliance Auth. The tool is a niche self-hosted authentication framework used exclusively within the EVE Online gaming community. It has no commercial surface, no external data collection, and is deployed by administrators on their own infrastructure for managing game community access.

AllMyLinks.description: AllMyLinks is a personal bio link page that consolidates multiple social media profiles, websites, and contact links into a single shareable online hub.
AllMyLinks.history: AllMyLinks is a link-in-bio tool launched around 2020, competing in the same space as Linktree. It allows creators and social media users to create a single landing page that aggregates links to all their profiles, content, and social media accounts. The platform gained traction among content creators and influencers looking for a free alternative to Linktree for their Instagram and TikTok bio links.
AllMyLinks.summary_abuse: Link-in-bio tools like AllMyLinks carry moderate abuse risk because bad actors sometimes use them to create convincing multi-link pages for phishing, fake giveaways, or scam storefronts targeting social media audiences. AllMyLinks has no notable published anti-abuse policy or bug bounty program. The free tier with no KYC makes it accessible to bad actors, though legitimate creator use dominates.

Alloka.description: Alloka is a platform that provides end-to-end analytics and dynamic call tracking.
Alloka.history: Alloka is a Russian call tracking and analytics platform founded in 2010, headquartered in Moscow. The platform provides cross-analytics and dynamic call tracking tools to help businesses optimize advertising spend, track phone call conversions, and train sales managers. It serves primarily the Russian-speaking market and offers integrations with popular advertising platforms.
Alloka.summary_abuse: No specific abuse incidents have been reported for Alloka. As a Russian company, it is subject to Russian data regulations that may allow government access to call tracking data. The platform processes sensitive call data including customer phone numbers and conversation analytics. GDPR compliance is not advertised, making it unsuitable for European deployments without a data processing agreement.

AlloyUI.history: AlloyUI was created in 2009 by Eduardo Lundgren and Nate Cavanaugh at Liferay, built on top of Yahoo's YUI3 JavaScript library with Bootstrap 3 for styling. It was Liferay Portal's UI framework for many years, providing a rich component library for enterprise portal applications. With the shift toward modern frontend tooling, Liferay deprecated AlloyUI in favor of Metal.js and later React-based components in Liferay 7.x. The repository is now archived and read-only on GitHub.
AlloyUI.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for AlloyUI. It is a deprecated enterprise UI library that was used internally by Liferay Portal. As an archived project with no new development, it presents no current abuse risk beyond the standard risk of using unmaintained dependencies.

Allstate.description: Allstate is a provider of auto insurance quote metrics, offering data-driven insights to assess coverage options, pricing, and risk factors.
Allstate.history: Allstate Corporation is a major American insurance company founded on April 17, 1931, originally as part of Sears, Roebuck and Co. It became publicly traded in 1993 and fully independent in June 1995. Headquartered in Glenview, Illinois, Allstate is one of the largest publicly held personal lines property and casualty insurers in the US. Its website allstate.com launched in 1996. The company is listed on the NYSE under the ticker ALL.
Allstate.summary_abuse: No notable technology-related abuse incidents have been reported for Allstate's web platform. As a major publicly-traded US insurance company with strong regulatory oversight, Allstate operates under stringent financial and data protection requirements. The website collects personal insurance-related information and uses standard marketing analytics.

Allyable.description: Allyable is an automated web accessibility solution with an AI engine.
Allyable.history: Allyable is an AI-powered digital accessibility platform founded around 2019 in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. The company markets itself as the first AI-powered digital accessibility solution and is available in the AWS Marketplace. Allyable provides enterprise-grade accessibility testing, monitoring, and remediation tools to help organizations meet WCAG, ADA, and EAA compliance requirements.
Allyable.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Allyable. The platform is a legitimate enterprise accessibility tool. Like other accessibility overlay solutions, it may face criticism from accessibility advocates who argue that automated fixes are insufficient for genuine WCAG compliance. The company serves enterprise customers with a focus on compliance.

Allyant.description: Allyant is a company specializing in accessibility solutions for digital and print documents to ensure compliance with accessibility standards.
Allyant.history: Allyant was founded in 2022 by Thompson Street Capital Partners through the combination of three globally recognized accessibility companies: T-Base Communications (document accessibility), CommonLook (PDF accessibility), and Accessible360 (digital accessibility auditing). Headquartered in Ottawa, Ontario, Canada, Allyant markets itself as the world's first comprehensive accessibility solutions company, offering a full suite of auditing, remediation, and document accessibility services.
Allyant.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Allyant. The company is a PE-backed accessibility services provider formed from legitimate established companies. It serves enterprises needing WCAG, ADA, and Section 508 compliance and has a clean public record.

AlmaLinux.description: AlmaLinux is an open-source, community-driven Linux operating system that fills the gap left by the discontinuation of the CentOS Linux stable release.
AlmaLinux.history: AlmaLinux was launched on March 30, 2021 by CloudLinux as a free, open-source, community-driven RHEL-compatible Linux distribution created to fill the void left by CentOS Linux's shift to a rolling-release model. On the same day, the AlmaLinux OS Foundation was established as a 501(c)(6) non-profit, with CloudLinux pledging $1 million annually. In July 2023, AlmaLinux shifted from being a binary rebuild of RHEL to an ABI-compatible distribution, allowing it to diverge slightly while maintaining application compatibility.
AlmaLinux.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for AlmaLinux. It is a community-governed Linux distribution maintained by a non-profit foundation with strong backing from CloudLinux and 25+ other sponsors. As an enterprise Linux OS, it is used extensively in server environments and has a clean security track record.

AlMatjar.description: AlMatjar is an online store builder designed to help users create and manage ecommerce websites.
AlMatjar.history: AlMatjar is an Egyptian e-commerce platform founded around 2019 in Cairo, Egypt. It provides Arabic-language online store builder services primarily targeting small and medium businesses in the Arab world, particularly Egypt. The platform offers tools to create and manage online stores with Arabic language support and local payment integrations. It competes in the growing Middle Eastern e-commerce market and has gained traction among Arabic-speaking merchants.
AlMatjar.summary_abuse: No significant abuse incidents or security issues have been publicly reported for AlMatjar. As a regional Arabic e-commerce platform, it operates in a less scrutinized market, and formal compliance certifications are not publicly verified. Privacy practices may not meet European standards.

Alohome.description: Alohome is a tech platform designed to enhance real estate sales conversions.
Alohome.history: Alohome is a Mexican real estate CRM and digital platform founded in 2020 by Eduardo Orozco in Mexico City. The platform serves real estate developers and professionals with tools for managing property listings, leads, and sales processes. The company was acquired by Koggi, strengthening its Latin American real estate technology presence.
Alohome.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Alohome. The platform is a niche real estate CRM targeting the Latin American market. It processes property and lead data within the real estate vertical with no documented security incidents.

Alpha Review.description: Alpha Review is an in-store ecommerce review tool developed in Korea, designed to gather and display customer feedback directly within retail environments.
Alpha Review.history: Alpha Review (now Alpha+) is a Shopify app developed by Saladlab Inc., a Korean software company. The app provides product review tools for Shopify merchants including review collection, display, and an AI analyzer for AliExpress review insights. It targets dropshippers and ecommerce merchants who need social proof tools for their product pages.
Alpha Review.summary_abuse: Alpha Review has no notable abuse incidents. Like many review apps, the AI review analyzer feature that imports AliExpress reviews may be used to populate stores with third-party reviews that consumers might mistake as genuine customer feedback, which occupies a grey area for consumer trust. No security incidents have been reported.

Alpharank.description: Alpharank is a tool focused on customer journey mapping to enhance conversion optimization specifically for banks.
Alpharank.history: Alpharank is an AI SEO optimizer for Shopify merchants, providing tools to improve organic search rankings and traffic. The platform offers AI-powered SEO analysis and optimization recommendations specifically built for the Shopify ecommerce ecosystem. Limited public information is available about the company's founding history and ownership.
Alpharank.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Alpharank. The platform is a niche SEO tool for Shopify stores. AI-assisted SEO tools can theoretically be misused for spammy content generation, but Alpharank serves a legitimate ecommerce audience with no documented abuse history.

Alpine Linux.description: Alpine Linux is a security-oriented, lightweight Linux distribution based on musl libc and busybox.
Alpine Linux.history: Alpine Linux was created in 2005 by Natanael Copa, a developer who had previously worked on Gentoo Linux. The distribution was designed for simplicity, security, and resource efficiency, originally targeting embedded systems and routers. It uses musl libc and BusyBox instead of the more common glibc and GNU Coreutils, resulting in a minimal base image of approximately 5 MB. Alpine became the dominant base image for Docker containers, adopted by major projects for its minimal attack surface and fast pull times.
Alpine Linux.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Alpine Linux. The distribution is widely trusted across the industry as the default base image for containerized applications. Its security-focused design, with position-independent executables and stack-smashing protection, makes it a preferred choice for security-conscious deployments.

Alpine.js.description: Alpine.js is a minimal framework for composing behavior directly in your markup.
Alpine.js.history: Alpine.js was created by Caleb Porzio and released in 2019 as a minimalist JavaScript framework for adding reactivity directly in HTML markup. At only 15 KB minified, it provides declarative, reactive behavior similar to Vue.js without a build step or bundler. Alpine.js became widely adopted in the Laravel and Tailwind CSS communities, and Porzio has funded its development through GitHub Sponsors. It is especially popular for adding interactivity to server-rendered HTML without committing to a full SPA framework.
Alpine.js.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Alpine.js. It is a widely-trusted, MIT-licensed JavaScript library with no server-side components or data collection. The project is maintained by Caleb Porzio with community contributors.

Altcha.description: Altcha is a spam and abuse protection solution for websites and apps, offering a privacy-friendly Captcha and other tools designed with GDPR compliance to provide strong security while safeguarding user privacy.
Altcha.history: ALTCHA is an open-source, self-hosted CAPTCHA alternative created around 2023. It uses a proof-of-work (PoW) mechanism to distinguish bots from humans without cookies, fingerprinting, or user tracking. Unlike Google reCAPTCHA, ALTCHA is fully GDPR-compliant by design, with no data sent to third parties. The project is distributed as a Web Component and is 90% smaller than reCAPTCHA. A managed cloud service is also available alongside the self-hosted option.
Altcha.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for ALTCHA. The project is a privacy-first CAPTCHA alternative explicitly designed to prevent bot abuse on websites while respecting user privacy. As a security tool, its purpose is anti-abuse rather than being a vector for abuse.

AlternC.description: AlternC is a set of software management on Linux shared hosting.
AlternC.history: AlternC originated in May 2001 in France following the closure of Altern.org, a major French free hosting provider that shut down after a legal complaint. The technical team at l'Autre Net, a non-profit self-managed hosting association, rewrote the control panel software and released it as AlternC. The project aims to promote self-hosting by individuals and small organizations through a non-technical web interface for managing web and mail servers. It is based on Debian GNU/Linux and licensed under GPLv2+.
AlternC.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for AlternC. The project is a niche French open-source web hosting control panel used by small non-profit and independent hosting providers. As a self-hosted server management tool with no cloud component, it presents no third-party data risk.

Altis.description: Altis is a digital experience platform built on top of WordPress.
Altis.history: Altis is an enterprise WordPress digital experience platform developed by Human Made, a distributed WordPress agency founded in 2011. Altis combines managed WordPress hosting with a suite of enterprise features including content management, personalization, analytics, and search. Human Made has been providing enterprise WordPress services since 2011, with Altis as their productized platform offering for large organizations needing managed WordPress at scale.
Altis.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Altis. The platform is an enterprise WordPress hosting and DXP solution serving large organizations. It requires a sales conversation and is not accessible for anonymous abuse. Human Made is a well-regarded WordPress agency with a strong track record.

Alumni Channel.description: Alumni Channel is a platform facilitating organization and communication with alumni and members.
Alumni Channel.history: Alumni Channel was founded in 1998 by Chris Gehringer in Cherry Hill, New Jersey, originally built for his own high school after studying database and coding in university. The platform provides online alumni community databases and management tools allowing schools and organizations to maintain contact with graduates. It is one of the earlier web-based alumni management platforms still in operation.
Alumni Channel.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Alumni Channel. The platform is a niche alumni database and community tool used by schools and organizations. It manages personal contact data for alumni networks which requires proper data handling.

AlumnIQ.description: AlumnIQ is a set of services to manage events, giving, email, volunteers, class agents, and more, all designed to work with your database of record.
AlumnIQ.history: AlumnIQ was founded in 2004 and is headquartered in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania. The company provides a comprehensive alumni engagement platform for higher education institutions, offering event management, directory services, volunteering, giving, and communications tools integrated with university advancement systems. Nearly 100 schools use AlumnIQ's software to improve alumni engagement and fundraising operations.
AlumnIQ.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for AlumnIQ. The platform serves a specialized higher education market managing alumni data and engagement. It handles personally identifiable information of alumni which requires appropriate data handling, though no security incidents are publicly documented.

AlvandCMS.description: Alvand is a PHP-based CMS with a modular architecture to enhance scalability and maintainability. It utilizes caching, reduces database queries and employs optimization mechanisms to improve request processing performance. The system supports RESTful APIs, user access management and built-in SEO. It features an independent templating system and dynamic cache management to minimize page load times. It's compatible with MySQL and MariaDB and includes security measures to prevent common attacks.
AlvandCMS.history: AlvandCMS is an Iranian proprietary content management system developed by Idepardazan Madita company, unveiled in April 2022 under the name Amirkabir. It is marketed as the first completely Iranian CMS with plugin support and over 250 features. The platform targets Iranian businesses as a domestic alternative to foreign CMS platforms like WordPress.
AlvandCMS.summary_abuse: No specific abuse incidents have been publicly reported for AlvandCMS. As an Iranian-made CMS, it operates outside common international compliance frameworks like GDPR. The platform is primarily used within Iran and may be subject to Iranian internet regulations and data access laws.

Amap.description: Amap is a digital cartography platform providing detailed geographic data, navigation, and location-based services across Asian regions.
Amap.history: Amap (AutoNavi) was founded in 2002 by Jun Hou, Congwu Cheng, and Jun Xiao to develop digital map data for automotive navigation. Alibaba Group acquired AutoNavi in 2014 for approximately $1.5 billion and rebranded it as Amap. The platform is China's leading digital mapping and navigation service with approximately 170 million daily active users as of 2025. In 2018, Amap became the first Chinese mapping service to reach 100 million daily users. Alibaba launched an AI-native version of Amap using its Qwen AI model in 2025.
Amap.summary_abuse: Amap is a mainstream Chinese mapping application. As a wholly owned Alibaba subsidiary, it is subject to China's national security and data laws, which require companies to cooperate with government data requests. The platform collects extensive location and behavioral data from hundreds of millions of users in China. For non-Chinese users or organizations concerned about Chinese government data access, this represents a significant privacy risk.

Amasty.description: Amasty is a developer of modules for Magento platforms.
Amasty.history: Amasty was founded in 2009 in Nicosia, Cyprus by Andrew Vashkevich, Alex Stelmakh, and Val Hrykyan. The company has grown into one of the leading Magento extension providers with 300+ extensions covering SEO, checkout, navigation, and compliance. Amasty serves over 125,000 global customers across 100+ countries and is an official Adobe Solution and Technology Partner. The company maintains offices in Cyprus, Slovakia, and Poland.
Amasty.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for Amasty. The company is a well-established Magento extensions provider with over 15 years of operation. Extensions are installed on customer-controlled Magento stores, and the company has a clean track record as an official Adobe partner.

Amaya.description: Amaya is an open-source web browser editor to create and update documents on the web.
Amaya.history: Amaya was a free, open-source web browser and WYSIWYG editor jointly developed by the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) and INRIA (the French national computer science research institute), with work beginning in 1994 and the first release in 1996. It was designed to serve as a reference implementation and testbed for W3C web standards, supporting HTML, XHTML, MathML, SVG, and CSS. The last release was version 11.4.4 on January 18, 2012, after which W3C and INRIA ended all support and development.
Amaya.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents were ever associated with Amaya. The project was a W3C reference implementation for testing web standards, used primarily by web developers and researchers. Amaya was abandoned in 2012 and should not be used in production environments today due to lack of security patches.

Amaze UI.description: Amaze UI is a mobile-first JavaScript library designed to simplify front-end development.
Amaze UI.history: Amaze UI is China's first open-source HTML5 multi-screen front-end framework, created around 2014 and oriented toward mobile-first development. It was primarily maintained by a Chinese developer community and gained popularity in China for building responsive web interfaces. The project has largely gone dormant as the Chinese and global developer communities shifted to more modern frameworks like Vue.js and React.
Amaze UI.summary_abuse: Amaze UI is a client-side JavaScript UI library with no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend framework used for building web interfaces and has no server-side or hosting component that could be exploited. No documented cases of abuse using this library have been reported.

Amazon Advertising.description: Amazon Advertising (formerly AMS or Amazon Marketing Services) is a service that works in a similar way to pay-per-click ads on Google.
Amazon Advertising.history: Amazon Advertising consolidated from Amazon Media Group, Amazon Marketing Services, and Amazon Advertising Platform, all established around 2012. In 2018 Amazon unified these into a single brand called Amazon Advertising. The platform grew to become the third-largest digital advertising platform globally behind Google and Meta, offering sponsored products, display, and DSP services.
Amazon Advertising.summary_abuse: Amazon Advertising is an ad platform that serves display and sponsored ads across Amazon properties. Ad tech platforms can be abused to serve malicious or misleading ads; Amazon has policies against deceptive advertising and maintains an enforcement team. Occasional reports of fake or counterfeit product ads appearing in sponsored results exist, but Amazon responds to reports and enforces its policies. Not known as a vector for phishing or malware delivery.

Amazon ALB.description: Amazon Application Load Balancer (ALB) distributes incoming application traffic to increase availability and support content-based routing.
Amazon ALB.history: Amazon Application Load Balancer (ALB) was launched by AWS in 2016 as the next generation of the classic Elastic Load Balancer, operating at layer 7 (HTTP/HTTPS). It added support for content-based routing, WebSockets, HTTP/2, and integration with AWS WAF and Amazon Cognito. ALB became the standard choice for modern microservices and container-based architectures on AWS, with no notable ownership changes or controversies.
Amazon ALB.summary_abuse: Amazon ALB is backend infrastructure with no meaningful direct abuse surface. Occasionally attackers route through ALB to obfuscate origin IPs, but AWS has strong AUP enforcement and automated abuse detection. The service is foundational cloud infrastructure with legitimate use overwhelmingly dominating.

Amazon Associates.description: Amazon Associates is an affiliate marketing program that allows website owners and bloggers to create links and earn referral fees when customers click through and buy products from Amazon.
Amazon Associates.history: Amazon Associates launched in July 1996, making it the first large-scale affiliate program on the web and a model for the entire affiliate marketing industry. It allows website owners to earn commissions by linking to Amazon products. Over the decades Amazon has periodically adjusted commission rates, notably cutting rates significantly in April 2020, which caused controversy among content creators. The program remains the world's largest affiliate network.
Amazon Associates.summary_abuse: Amazon Associates is occasionally abused through cookie stuffing schemes where fraudulent affiliates inject tracking cookies without genuine referrals to earn illegitimate commissions. The program has also been used by spam sites and low-quality content farms to monetize thin content. Amazon monitors for such abuse and terminates violating accounts. The overall abuse rate relative to legitimate publisher use is low.

Amazon Aurora.description: Amazon Aurora is a relational database service developed and offered by Amazon Web Services.
Amazon Aurora.history: Amazon Aurora was announced at AWS re:Invent in November 2014 and became generally available in July 2015 as a MySQL-compatible relational database. It was engineered from scratch by AWS to decouple compute from storage, delivering up to five times MySQL performance at one-tenth the cost of commercial databases. A PostgreSQL-compatible edition launched in 2017. Aurora Serverless was introduced in 2018 and Aurora Serverless v2 in 2022, adding auto-scaling capabilities.
Amazon Aurora.summary_abuse: Amazon Aurora is a managed relational database service with no notable abuse signal. It is backend infrastructure used inside AWS environments and has no consumer-facing surface that could be exploited for phishing or fraud. Access is controlled via AWS IAM and VPC, making external abuse essentially impossible.

Amazon CloudFront.description: Amazon CloudFront is a fast content delivery network (CDN) service that securely delivers data, videos, applications, and APIs to customers globally with low latency, high transfer speeds.
Amazon CloudFront.history: Amazon CloudFront launched in public beta in November 2008 as AWS's content delivery network, initially with 14 edge locations. It launched to general availability in 2010. Over the years CloudFront expanded to over 400 points of presence globally and added features such as Lambda@Edge (2017) for edge computing, AWS WAF integration, and TLS/HTTPS support. It became one of the top global CDN providers by traffic volume.
Amazon CloudFront.summary_abuse: Amazon CloudFront is occasionally used to serve malicious content because attackers can host payloads in S3 and distribute them through CloudFront to leverage its trusted domain and TLS certificate. AWS has abuse reporting mechanisms and will act on takedown requests, but response times vary. Security researchers have documented phishing pages and malware hosted on CloudFront subdomains. Overall, legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates and AWS is considered responsive to abuse reports.

Amazon CloudWatch RUM.description: Amazon CloudWatch RUM is a real-user monitoring capability that helps you identify and debug issues in the client-side on web applications and enhance end user's digital experience.
Amazon CloudWatch RUM.history: Amazon CloudWatch RUM (Real User Monitoring) was announced at AWS re:Invent in November 2021 and became generally available the same month. It is part of the broader Amazon CloudWatch observability suite. The service allows developers to monitor web application client-side performance including page load times, JavaScript errors, and HTTP failures from real user sessions.
Amazon CloudWatch RUM.summary_abuse: Amazon CloudWatch RUM is a monitoring service embedded in web applications as a JavaScript snippet. It collects performance metrics from end users and sends telemetry to AWS. It has no notable abuse signal and is not known to be used in abusive contexts. The data collected is customer-controlled and stored in the customer's AWS account.

Amazon Cognito.description: Amazon Cognito lets you add user sign-up, sign-in, and access control to your web and mobile apps. Amazon Cognito supports sign-in with social identity providers, such as Apple, Facebook, Google, and Amazon, and enterprise identity providers via SAML 2.0 and OpenID Connect.
Amazon Cognito.history: Amazon Cognito became generally available in July 2014 as AWS's managed customer identity and access management service. It provides user pools for authentication and identity pools for federated identities, allowing applications to use social identity providers like Google and Facebook. Processing over 100 billion authentications per month, Cognito grew to be a widely used auth platform for web and mobile apps built on AWS.
Amazon Cognito.summary_abuse: Amazon Cognito is a backend authentication service with no notable direct abuse signal. It is used as the authentication layer for applications and does not have a consumer-facing surface independent of the applications it powers. Occasional security research has identified misconfigurations in Cognito user pools that could expose data, but these are application-level issues rather than platform abuse.

Amazon EC2.description: Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud is a part of Amazon.com's cloud-computing platform, Amazon Web Services, that allows users to rent virtual computers on which to run their own computer applications.
Amazon EC2.history: Amazon EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud) launched in public beta in August 2006 and became generally available in October 2008, marking the beginning of modern cloud computing. It was one of the first infrastructure-as-a-service offerings and helped establish the cloud computing market. EC2 introduced concepts like on-demand virtual machines, pay-per-use pricing, and elastic scaling that became industry standards.
Amazon EC2.summary_abuse: Amazon EC2 is the world's most widely used cloud compute platform and is abused by threat actors for hosting malware, command-and-control servers, phishing pages, and conducting DDoS attacks. EC2's broad IP ranges are frequently seen in security incident reports. AWS operates an active abuse team at aws-abuse@amazon.com and automatically monitors for abuse, but the sheer scale means some malicious instances remain active for a period before detection. Overall, legitimate commercial use overwhelmingly dominates.

Amazon ECS.history: Launched by Amazon Web Services in 2014 at re:Invent as a fully managed container orchestration service. It has become one of the leading container management platforms on AWS, alongside Amazon EKS. Continuously updated with features like AWS Fargate serverless compute, ECS Anywhere for hybrid deployments, and deep integration with the broader AWS ecosystem.
Amazon ECS.summary_abuse: Amazon ECS has no notable abuse signal. It is a backend container orchestration service with no consumer-facing surface for fraud or phishing. The service requires an AWS account with billing verification, presenting a high barrier to anonymous abuse. AWS provides a comprehensive abuse reporting process, Trust Center, and compliance certifications including SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and PCI DSS. Any abusive container workloads would be subject to AWS's AUP enforcement.

Amazon EFS.description: Amazon Elastic File System is a cloud storage service provided by Amazon Web Services.
Amazon EFS.history: Amazon EFS (Elastic File System) was announced in April 2015 and became generally available in June 2016. It provides fully managed NFS-compatible shared file storage for use with AWS compute services. EFS filled the gap between EBS block storage and S3 object storage by providing POSIX-compliant file system semantics accessible from multiple EC2 instances simultaneously.
Amazon EFS.summary_abuse: Amazon EFS is backend shared file storage infrastructure with no notable abuse surface. It is accessible only within AWS VPCs and requires IAM credentials, making it inaccessible to external parties. No documented cases of EFS being exploited for abuse have been reported.

Amazon ELB.description: AWS ELB is a network load balancer service provided by Amazon Web Services for distributing traffic across multiple targets, such as Amazon EC2 instances, containers, IP addresses, and Lambda functions.
Amazon ELB.history: Amazon ELB (Elastic Load Balancing) launched in May 2009 as one of the earliest managed load balancing services. The Classic Load Balancer was eventually joined by the Application Load Balancer (2016) and Network Load Balancer (2017) under the ELB family umbrella. Classic ELB has been considered legacy since ALB launched and AWS encourages migration to newer variants.
Amazon ELB.summary_abuse: Amazon ELB is backend load balancing infrastructure with no notable direct abuse surface. As with other AWS infrastructure, it can theoretically be part of an attacker's stack, but access is credential-gated. No notable abuse patterns specific to ELB are documented.

Amazon Pay.description: Amazon Pay is an online payments processing service that is owned by Amazon. It lets you use the payment methods associated with your Amazon account to make payments for goods and services.
Amazon Pay.history: Amazon Pay launched in 2007 as Checkout by Amazon, rebranded to Amazon Payments, and then to Amazon Pay. It allows customers to use their Amazon account credentials and stored payment methods on third-party e-commerce sites. The service competes with PayPal and Stripe. Amazon briefly tested discontinuing the service for some markets but maintained and expanded it globally.
Amazon Pay.summary_abuse: Amazon Pay is a payment processing service and is occasionally targeted by fraudsters using stolen Amazon account credentials to make unauthorized purchases on third-party sites. Amazon has fraud detection mechanisms and buyer protection policies. There are documented cases of scammers requesting Amazon Pay transactions to defraud buyers in peer-to-peer marketplaces, similar to PayPal scams. The vendor enforces its AUP and has a dedicated fraud team.

Amazon S3.description: Amazon S3 or Amazon Simple Storage Service is a service offered by Amazon Web Services (AWS) that provides object storage through a web service interface.
Amazon S3.history: Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service) launched in March 2006 as the first commercially available object storage service, preceding EC2. It became foundational cloud infrastructure and the de facto standard for object storage. Over the years S3 gained versioning, lifecycle policies, encryption, public access blocks, and Intelligent-Tiering. A major outage in February 2017 took down large portions of the internet, highlighting S3's centrality to the web.
Amazon S3.summary_abuse: Amazon S3 is frequently abused for hosting malware, phishing pages, and exfiltrated data due to its global availability and trusted domain names. Security researchers regularly find publicly exposed S3 buckets containing sensitive customer data due to misconfiguration. AWS introduced S3 Block Public Access in 2018 and made it default in 2022 to combat this. Phishing kits and credential harvesters hosted on S3 have been documented by multiple security firms. AWS responds to abuse reports but the volume means some malicious content persists temporarily.

Amazon SES.description: Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) is an email service that enables developers to send mail from within any application.
Amazon SES.history: Amazon SES (Simple Email Service) launched in January 2011 as a low-cost transactional and bulk email sending service for developers. It became popular for its very low per-message pricing compared to competitors. SES introduced a sandbox mode requiring new accounts to request production access to prevent immediate spam abuse. Over the years it added features like email templates, dedicated IPs, and email receiving capabilities.
Amazon SES.summary_abuse: Amazon SES is a documented vector for spam and phishing email campaigns due to its very low pricing and AWS's trusted sending reputation. Compromised AWS accounts are frequently used to send bulk spam through SES, and threat actors exploit trial accounts before triggering abuse detection. AWS requires new accounts to start in a sandbox and graduate to production, maintains sending limits, and terminates accounts found sending spam. Security researchers have documented SES-based phishing campaigns leveraging Amazon's trusted domains. AWS has improved its abuse detection over time but SES remains a notable spam infrastructure component.

Amazon Web Services.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a comprehensive cloud services platform offering compute power, database storage, content delivery and other functionality.
Amazon Web Services.history: Launched by Amazon in March 2006, offering cloud computing services including S3 and EC2. Grew to become the world's dominant cloud platform with over 200 services, 105 Availability Zones across 33 regions. AWS generates over $100B in annual revenue, making it the largest cloud provider globally. Continues to innovate with AI, machine learning, and serverless technologies.
Amazon Web Services.summary_abuse: AWS has minimal abuse signal given its dominant position as the world's largest cloud provider. While AWS infrastructure can be abused (hosting phishing sites, malware C2), the company maintains robust anti-abuse programs including automated threat detection, abuse reporting, and proactive takedowns. AWS holds extensive compliance certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI DSS, FedRAMP) and has a published Acceptable Use Policy. The scale of legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates any abusive activity.

Amazon Webstore.description: Amazon Webstore is an all-in-one hosted ecommerce website solution.
Amazon Webstore.history: Amazon Webstore was an e-commerce platform launched by Amazon around 2010 that allowed merchants to create branded online stores powered by Amazon's infrastructure. Amazon shut down the service on July 18, 2016, directing merchants to partner platforms like Shopify. The discontinuation gave merchants a migration path but effectively ended Amazon's white-label storefront product.
Amazon Webstore.summary_abuse: Amazon Webstore was discontinued in 2016 and has no current abuse surface. During its operational period it was a standard e-commerce hosting platform with standard abuse risks such as fake storefronts, but no particularly notable abuse incidents were documented specific to this platform.

Ambassador.description: Ambassador is a marketer-friendly software that simplifies referral marketing and helps automating the enrolment, tracking, rewarding and management process.
Ambassador.history: Ambassador API Gateway was created by Datawire (later renamed Ambassador Labs) as an open-source Kubernetes-native API gateway built on top of Envoy Proxy. Development began around 2017. The project was later renamed Emissary-Ingress when it was donated to the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF). Ambassador Labs received funding from Insight Partners. In 2023 Gravitee acquired Ambassador Labs to expand its API management capabilities.
Ambassador.summary_abuse: Ambassador API Gateway is backend Kubernetes infrastructure with no notable abuse surface. It functions as an ingress controller and API gateway within Kubernetes clusters, with no consumer-facing component that could be directly exploited for phishing or fraud.

Amber.description: Amber is a web application framework written in Crystal inspired by Kemal, Rails, Phoenix and other popular application frameworks.
Amber.history: Amber Smalltalk (formerly named Jtalk) is an implementation of Smalltalk-80 that compiles to JavaScript and runs in the browser. It was created by Nicolas Petton in 2011. The project was influenced by Clamato, an earlier Smalltalk-in-browser project by Avi Bryant. Amber allows Smalltalk code to be compiled to JavaScript, enabling Smalltalk semantics in web environments.
Amber.summary_abuse: Amber is a niche programming language implementation with no notable abuse signal. It is used by a small community of Smalltalk developers and has no consumer-facing surface that could be exploited.

amCharts.description: amCharts is a JavaScript-based interactive charts and maps programming library and tool.
amCharts.history: amCharts was founded around 2006 in Vilnius, Lithuania, and has developed several major versions of its JavaScript charting library, with amCharts 5 being the current generation. It operates a dual-license model: free for non-commercial use, paid commercial licenses.
amCharts.summary_abuse: amCharts is a JavaScript charting library with no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend data visualization tool with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

American Express.description: American Express, also known as Amex, facilitates electronic funds transfers throughout the world, most commonly through branded credit cards, debit cards and prepaid cards.
American Express.history: American Express was founded in 1850 as an express mail company, pivoting to financial services and launching its first credit card in 1958. It became one of the world's largest payment networks and charge card issuers. AmEx divested Shearson Lehman and First Data over the decades and has maintained a premium brand positioning focused on rewards and travel benefits for cardholders.
American Express.summary_abuse: American Express accounts and card details are frequently targeted by phishing attacks and card-not-present fraud. Phishing sites impersonating AmEx are commonly reported. AmEx has a dedicated security team, fraud detection systems, and processes abuse reports at abuse@americanexpress.com. As a major payment network, it is targeted proportionally to its scale but is not particularly favored by fraudsters relative to peers.

Ametys.history: Ametys CMS was created in 2003 by Anyware Services, a French software company based in Labège, France. It is an open-source Java-based content management system. Since 2005 it has been widely deployed in French higher education institutions. The 4.x series introduced in 2018 brought a modernized UI and improved collaboration features.
Ametys.summary_abuse: Ametys CMS is an open-source Java CMS primarily used by French universities and public institutions. No notable abuse signal is documented. As a self-hosted CMS, security depends heavily on how administrators configure and patch the installation.

Amex Express Checkout.description: Amex Express Checkout is a service that simplifies the checkout experience by auto-filling necessary cardholder payment data into merchant checkout fields.
Amex Express Checkout.history: Amex Express Checkout is American Express's one-click checkout product for merchants, enabling AmEx cardholders to complete purchases using stored credentials without re-entering card details. It is the AmEx equivalent to PayPal Express and Visa Checkout. The product was developed as part of AmEx's digital payment strategy to reduce checkout friction on e-commerce sites.
Amex Express Checkout.summary_abuse: Amex Express Checkout is a payment button embedded in merchant checkout flows. It processes AmEx card transactions and is subject to standard payment fraud risks such as stolen card credentials. American Express's fraud detection systems monitor transactions. No specific abuse patterns are documented beyond standard payment fraud.

Amilia.description: Amilia is a membership management software.
Amilia.history: Amilia was founded in May 2009 in Montreal, Quebec, Canada, by Francois Gaouette.
