The company built a SaaS platform (SmartRec) for recreation, community, and parks and recreation organizations to manage membership registration and payment processing. Amilia serves over 1,500 organizations and 6,600 facilities across North America.
Amilia.summary_abuse: Amilia is a recreation management SaaS platform used by community organizations and parks departments. No notable abuse signal is documented. It processes payments for activity registrations, which carries standard payment fraud risk, but the platform is not known as a vector for scams or malicious activity.

Amiro.CMS.description: Amiro.CMS is a commercial content management system developed and distributed by the Russian company Amiro. Written in PHP and uses MySQL as a database.
Amiro.CMS.history: Amiro.CMS is a Russian commercial content management system developed by LLC Amiro, a company that has been active in information technology since 1998. The CMS has been sold as a standalone product since 2001, with its current configuration established around 2003. It is primarily used in the Russian-speaking market for building e-commerce websites. It has gained a modest partner ecosystem of over 500 development companies in Russia.
Amiro.CMS.summary_abuse: Amiro.CMS is a self-hosted Russian CMS product with no notable international abuse signal. As a self-hosted platform, its security posture depends on the deploying organization's configuration and patching practices. No specific documented abuse campaigns using this platform have been widely reported.

Amobee.description: Amobee is a cloud-based advertising and data management platform.
Amobee.history: Amobee was founded in 2005 in Redwood City, California by Zohar Levkovitz, Gil Shulman, and Saul Rurka. In 2012, Singtel acquired Amobee for $321 million. Amobee subsequently acquired Turn (a DSP) to expand its programmatic advertising capabilities. In July 2022, Tremor International acquired Amobee from Singtel for $239 million to build a combined end-to-end advertising technology stack.
Amobee.summary_abuse: Amobee is a programmatic advertising and DSP platform serving ad buyers. As ad-tech infrastructure, it participates in the broader ad ecosystem where ad fraud, brand safety issues, and privacy concerns are endemic. The platform collects significant user tracking data for targeting purposes, which is standard for DSPs but represents a notable privacy risk. No specific major incidents are documented beyond standard ad-tech industry concerns.

amoCRM.description: amoCRM is a web-based customer relationship management software solution.
amoCRM.history: amoCRM was founded in 2009 by Mike Tokovinin as an independent CRM startup. In October 2016, Russian enterprise software giant 1C acquired a majority 51% stake. The product has since been rebranded as Kommo in some markets while continuing to operate under the amoCRM name in others.
amoCRM.summary_abuse: amoCRM is a business CRM platform with no notable abuse signal specific to fraud or phishing. It is aimed at sales teams and small businesses with no anonymous content-hosting features that would be attractive for scammers.

AMP.description: AMP, originally created by Google, is an open-source HTML framework developed by the AMP open-source Project. AMP is designed to help webpages load faster.
AMP.history: AMP was announced by Google on October 7, 2015 aiming to speed up mobile web content. The project dropped the Accelerated Mobile Pages name in 2019 and joined the OpenJS Foundation that same year. Usage declined as Core Web Vitals replaced AMP-specific ranking bonuses.
AMP.summary_abuse: AMP is an open web standard with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history.

AMP for WordPress.description: AMP for WordPress automatically adds Accelerated Mobile Pages (Google AMP Project) functionality to your WordPress site.
AMP for WordPress.history: AMP for WordPress is the official WordPress plugin for the AMP open-source project, initially developed by Automattic and XWP with Google support starting in 2016. The plugin automatically adds AMP functionality to WordPress sites. The AMP project itself became part of the OpenJS Foundation in 2019.
AMP for WordPress.summary_abuse: AMP for WordPress is an open-source WordPress plugin with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history.

Amped.description: Amped is a popup tool designed for growth, enabling email and SMS popups to capture leads and engage users.
Amped.history: Amped was founded in 2022 by Matt Cimino in Raleigh, North Carolina, as an email and SMS popup tool for ecommerce businesses. The product launched to help Shopify merchants capture leads and grow subscriber lists. In 2024 Intuit Mailchimp acquired Amped, and Mailchimp's refreshed popup forms product was built on Amped's technology.
Amped.summary_abuse: Amped is a popup lead-capture tool used for growing email and SMS subscriber lists. Popup tools can be configured to create aggressive or deceptive capture experiences, though no specific abuse incidents are documented for Amped. Following its acquisition by Mailchimp, the product is backed by Intuit's compliance and trust infrastructure.

Amplience.description: Amplience is an API-first, headless content management platform for enterprise retail.
Amplience.history: Amplience was founded in 2008 in London, England by James Brooke and Rory Dennis. The company built a headless CMS and digital asset management (DAM) platform aimed at e-commerce retailers. Amplience raised $219 million in total funding including a Series A from Octopus Ventures in 2014. It established itself as a content platform for large retail brands seeking to publish content across multiple channels.
Amplience.summary_abuse: Amplience is an enterprise headless CMS used by large retail brands to manage and publish content. No notable abuse signal is documented. The platform is not consumer-facing in a way that could be directly exploited for phishing or fraud.

Amplify JS.description: AmplifyJS is a set of components designed to solve common web application problems with a simplistic API. Amplify's goal is to simplify all forms of data handling by providing a unified API for various data sources.
Amplify JS.history: AWS Amplify JavaScript library was announced by Amazon Web Services in November 2017 as an open-source declarative library for building cloud-connected web and mobile applications. It provides integrations with AWS services including Cognito, S3, AppSync, and API Gateway. The library is Apache-2.0 licensed and maintained by AWS engineers.
Amplify JS.summary_abuse: Amplify JS is a client-side JavaScript library for connecting web and mobile apps to AWS backends. It has no notable abuse signal. It is a development tool with no consumer-facing surface that could be directly exploited.

Amplitude.description: Amplitude is a web and mobile analytics solution with cross-platform user journey tracking, user behavior analysis and segmentation capabilities.
Amplitude.history: Amplitude was founded in 2012 by Curtis Liu and Spenser Skates through Y Combinator (YC W12). It initially started as Sonalight, a voice recognition app, before pivoting to product analytics after their internal analytics stack proved more valuable. Amplitude grew to be one of the leading product analytics platforms, going public on Nasdaq in September 2021 via a direct listing at a valuation of approximately $5 billion.
Amplitude.summary_abuse: Amplitude is a product analytics platform that collects user behavioral data from web and mobile applications. As a behavioral analytics service, it processes significant amounts of user interaction data, which carries privacy implications. The platform is governed by its privacy policy and GDPR/CCPA compliance programs. No specific documented abuse of the Amplitude platform for phishing or fraud has been widely reported.

Ampry.description: Ampry is a client acquisition marketing service designed to support law firms in expanding their reach and acquiring new clients.
Ampry.history: Ampry is a client acquisition marketing service focused on helping law firms expand their reach and generate leads. It is a niche marketing platform targeting the legal industry in the United States.
Ampry.summary_abuse: Ampry is a niche law firm marketing tool with no notable documented abuse signal. It operates as a B2B lead generation service for the legal sector.

Analysys Ark.history: Analysys Ark is a Chinese user behavior analytics and data platform developed by Analysys, a Beijing-based data analytics company. The Ark product provides event tracking and behavioral analytics for mobile apps and websites, primarily serving the Chinese market.
Analysys Ark.summary_abuse: Analysys Ark is a Chinese behavioral analytics platform. It collects user tracking data primarily from Chinese apps and websites. The platform's data handling practices are subject to Chinese data laws rather than GDPR. No specific international abuse incidents are documented.

Analytick.description: Analytick is an advanced analytics platform that collects and analyzes data on website performance and user behavior.
Analytick.history: A privacy-focused website analytics SaaS tool launched around 2020-2022 as part of the wave of GDPR-friendly Google Analytics alternatives.
Analytick.summary_abuse: A small privacy-focused website analytics platform. No notable abuse signal documented. Standard web analytics service with no consumer-facing features that would enable phishing or fraud.

AnalyticOwl.description: AnalyticOwl is a platform that offers a planning and attribution toolbox designed to support more informed advertising decisions.
AnalyticOwl.history: AnalyticOwl is a planning and attribution analytics platform focused on broadcast advertising, helping marketers measure the effectiveness of television and radio campaigns through web traffic analysis.
AnalyticOwl.summary_abuse: AnalyticOwl is a niche broadcast attribution analytics platform with no notable documented abuse signal. It is used by media buyers and broadcasters to measure campaign effectiveness.

AnalyticsConnect.description: AnalyticsConnect is a tool that tracks eCommerce data from Keap/Infusionsoft in Google Analytics, providing detailed insights and analysis.
AnalyticsConnect.history: AnalyticsConnect is a niche e-commerce analytics tool that bridges Keap (Infusionsoft) CRM data with Google Analytics to track purchase data and attribution for online stores.
AnalyticsConnect.summary_abuse: AnalyticsConnect is a small e-commerce analytics connector with no notable abuse signal. It passes purchase event data between platforms and has no consumer-facing surface enabling phishing or fraud.

Analyzati.description: Analyzati is a privacy-focused analytics tool that measures website performance while protecting user data.
Analyzati.history: A privacy-focused website analytics SaaS tool launched around 2020-2022 as part of the wave of GDPR-friendly Google Analytics alternatives.
Analyzati.summary_abuse: A small privacy-focused website analytics platform. No notable abuse signal documented. Standard web analytics service with no consumer-facing features that would enable phishing or fraud.

Analyzee.description: Analyzee is an analytics tool that allows you to gain insights into visitor behavior and optimise your website's user experience using heatmaps and other analytics features.
Analyzee.history: Analyzee is a privacy-focused website analytics platform providing visitor behavior insights. It was launched as a lightweight Google Analytics alternative emphasizing privacy compliance.
Analyzee.summary_abuse: Analyzee is a small privacy-focused web analytics tool. No documented abuse signal. Standard website analytics service with no features that would enable phishing, fraud, or spam.

Analyzz.description: Analyzz is a privacy-focused analytics platform that collects and reports website data while minimizing tracking of individual users.
Analyzz.history: A privacy-focused website analytics SaaS tool launched around 2020-2022 as part of the wave of GDPR-friendly Google Analytics alternatives.
Analyzz.summary_abuse: A small privacy-focused website analytics platform. No notable abuse signal documented. Standard web analytics service with no consumer-facing features that would enable phishing or fraud.

AndersNoren Baskerville.description: AndersNoren Baskerville is a responsive and retina-ready masonry WordPress theme for hoarders.
AndersNoren Baskerville.history: Baskerville is a free WordPress theme created by Swedish developer Anders Noren, who runs a popular portfolio of minimalist themes distributed through the official WordPress.org theme repository. His themes are known for clean typography and elegant design.
AndersNoren Baskerville.summary_abuse: AndersNoren Baskerville is a free WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a standard website template with no server-side components that could be directly exploited.

AndersNoren Fukasawa.description: AndersNoren Fukasawa is a minimal masonry style blog WordPress theme for photographers and collectors.
AndersNoren Fukasawa.history: Fukasawa is a free WordPress theme created by Swedish developer Anders Noren, who runs a popular portfolio of minimalist themes distributed through the official WordPress.org theme repository. His themes are known for clean typography and elegant design.
AndersNoren Fukasawa.summary_abuse: AndersNoren Fukasawa is a free WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a standard website template with no server-side components that could be directly exploited.

AndersNoren Hemingway.description: AndersNoren Hemingway is a clean and beautiful two-column WordPress theme for bloggers.
AndersNoren Hemingway.history: Hemingway is a free WordPress theme created by Swedish developer Anders Noren, who runs a popular portfolio of minimalist themes distributed through the official WordPress.org theme repository. His themes are known for clean typography and elegant design.
AndersNoren Hemingway.summary_abuse: AndersNoren Hemingway is a free WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a standard website template with no server-side components that could be directly exploited.

AndersNoren Hitchcock.description: AndersNoren Hitchcock is a portfolio WordPress theme for designers, photographers and other creatives.
AndersNoren Hitchcock.history: Hitchcock is a free WordPress theme created by Swedish developer Anders Noren, who runs a popular portfolio of minimalist themes distributed through the official WordPress.org theme repository. His themes are known for clean typography and elegant design.
AndersNoren Hitchcock.summary_abuse: AndersNoren Hitchcock is a free WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a standard website template with no server-side components that could be directly exploited.

AndersNoren Lovecraft.description: AndersNoren Lovecraft is a beautiful two-column WordPress theme for bloggers.
AndersNoren Lovecraft.history: Lovecraft is a free WordPress theme created by Swedish developer Anders Noren, who runs a popular portfolio of minimalist themes distributed through the official WordPress.org theme repository. His themes are known for clean typography and elegant design.
AndersNoren Lovecraft.summary_abuse: AndersNoren Lovecraft is a free WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a standard website template with no server-side components that could be directly exploited.

Anetwork.history: Anetwork was founded in 2010 by Shayan Shalileh as Blind Advertising Network, the largest CPC advertising platform in Iran. It merged with NOXEL in 2015 and relaunched as a full-service digital media agency. The company was later acquired by Digikala, Iran's largest e-commerce platform.
Anetwork.summary_abuse: Anetwork is an Iranian advertising network. Ad networks in Iran operate outside GDPR and other Western privacy frameworks, and the broader Iranian internet ecosystem has been associated with state-sponsored cyber activity. As an ad network, it deploys heavy tracking and banner ads across thousands of Iranian websites. No specific major international abuse incidents are documented, but its non-compliance with Western privacy standards represents a risk.

Anexis.description: Anexis is a software provider offering tools that streamline communication, sales, and management processes for lodging businesses.
Anexis.history: Anexis is a property management and communication software platform for hotels and accommodation providers, operating as a brand of Just Impact Group and headquartered in South Tyrol, Italy. The platform offers modular cloud-based tools including room planning, guest communication, and check-in management. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Anexis.summary_abuse: Anexis has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche B2B SaaS platform serving hotels and accommodation businesses, with no anonymous public signup and no consumer-facing content hosting features that would be attractive for phishing or fraud.

Angie.description: Angie is a drop-in replacement for the Nginx web server aiming to extend the functionality of the original version.
Angie.history: Angie is an open-source web server forked from Nginx, created around 2022 by former Nginx developers from the Russian company Web Server LLC (a former unit of NGINX Inc.). It was created as a drop-in Nginx replacement with extended functionality and is primarily targeting the Russian market following Nginx's acquisition by F5 and subsequent geopolitical complications.
Angie.summary_abuse: Angie is a web server software with no notable direct abuse signal. As server infrastructure, it can host malicious sites but has no consumer-facing surface that could itself be abused. Its Russian origin may be a consideration for organizations with strict supply chain requirements.

Angler AI.description: Angler AI is a platform that helps direct-to-consumer brands improve prospecting strategies and increase customer lifetime value through data-driven insights.
Angler AI.history: Angler AI was founded in 2022 by Indraneel Sheorey in Reseda, California. The company offers an AI-powered prospect data platform for direct-to-consumer and e-commerce brands, helping them find high-value customers using first-party data and an attribute dataset of 4,000+ purchase and demographic signals.
Angler AI.summary_abuse: Angler AI is an e-commerce audience targeting tool that analyzes customer data to identify prospective buyers. It handles consumer behavioral and purchase data, which carries privacy implications. No specific documented abuse incidents are reported. The platform is primarily a B2B tool for e-commerce marketers.

Angular.description: Angular is a TypeScript-based open-source web application framework led by the Angular Team at Google.
Angular.history: Angular (version 2+) was released by Google in September 2016 as a complete TypeScript-based rewrite of AngularJS, addressing performance and scalability limitations. It abandoned backward compatibility with AngularJS in favor of a component-based architecture. Angular is maintained by Google's Angular team with regular major releases roughly every six months. It remains one of the most widely adopted enterprise frontend frameworks.
Angular.summary_abuse: Angular is a Google-maintained frontend framework with no notable abuse signal. It is a development tool used to build web applications and has no consumer-facing surface that could be exploited independently.

Angular Gridster.description: Gridster is a JavaScript library, with a simple drag-and-drop interface, for creating intuitive, dynamic and customizable layouts in web applications.
Angular Gridster.history: Angular Gridster is an open-source Angular component library providing a draggable and resizable grid layout widget. The angular-gridster2 fork by Tiberiu Zuld became the maintained version. It is used for building dashboard-style interfaces within Angular applications.
Angular Gridster.summary_abuse: Angular Gridster is a UI component library with no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend component used to build grid-based dashboards in Angular apps.

Angular Material.description: Angular Material is a UI component library for Angular JS developers. Angular Material components assist in constructing attractive, consistent, and functional web pages and web applications.
Angular Material.history: Angular Material is Google's official Material Design component library for Angular. It launched alongside Angular 2 in 2016 and provides a comprehensive set of UI components following Google's Material Design guidelines. Maintained by Google's Angular team.
Angular Material.summary_abuse: Angular Material is a Google-maintained UI component library with no notable abuse signal. It is a development tool used to build web interfaces.

AngularDart.history: AngularDart was Google's port of Angular for the Dart programming language. It was developed alongside the TypeScript version of Angular but served a much narrower audience. Google officially deprecated AngularDart for external use and directed developers to the Flutter framework for multi-platform apps.
AngularDart.summary_abuse: AngularDart is a deprecated Google framework with no notable abuse signal. It is a development tool that was never widely adopted outside Google.

AngularJS.description: AngularJS is a JavaScript-based open-source web application framework led by the Angular Team at Google.
AngularJS.history: AngularJS (Angular 1.x) was created by Google engineers Misko Hevery and Adam Abrons and open-sourced in 2010. It became one of the most popular JavaScript frameworks of the early 2010s, introducing two-way data binding and dependency injection to web development. Google deprecated AngularJS support on December 31, 2021. Many legacy enterprise applications still run on AngularJS.
AngularJS.summary_abuse: AngularJS is a deprecated Google frontend framework with no notable abuse signal. It is a development tool used to build web applications. Its deprecation means unpatched security vulnerabilities in legacy AngularJS apps may pose a risk, but the framework itself is not abused.

Anima.description: Anima is an AI-powered tool designed to assist users in converting their designs into functional code, creating interactive prototypes, and streamlining the automation of design systems.
Anima.history: Anima was founded in 2017 by Or Arbel, Avishay Cohen, and Michal Cohen. The design-to-code platform was launched at Y Combinator in summer 2018. It raised a $10 million Series A in September 2021. The product converts Figma designs into React, Vue, and HTML code. In early 2026, IBM made a strategic investment in Anima.
Anima.summary_abuse: Anima is a design-to-code SaaS platform used by developers and designers. No notable abuse signal is documented. It is a B2B productivity tool with no consumer-facing features enabling phishing or fraud.

AnimalChat.description: AnimalChat is a messaging platform that connects pet owners and professionals, providing a flexible alternative to traditional communication methods.
AnimalChat.history: AnimalChat is a messaging and community platform connecting pet owners with veterinary professionals and other pet owners. It is a niche vertical social/messaging product for the pet care community.
AnimalChat.summary_abuse: AnimalChat is a niche pet owner messaging platform with no notable documented abuse signal.

Animate It.description: Animate It! adds CSS3 animations to your content.
Animate It.history: Animate It is a WordPress plugin that adds CSS3 animation effects to page content. It is distributed through the official WordPress.org plugin directory.
Animate It.summary_abuse: Animate It is a WordPress CSS animation plugin with no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend display plugin.

Animate.css.description: Animate.css is a ready-to-use library collection of CSS3 animation effects.
Animate.css.history: Animate.css was created by Daniel Eden in 2012 as a simple CSS3 animation library with a collection of ready-to-use animation effects. It became one of the most popular CSS animation libraries on GitHub, with tens of thousands of stars. The library provides cross-browser compatible animations with a simple class-based API.
Animate.css.summary_abuse: Animate.css is a widely used CSS animation library with no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend display tool used by developers to add visual effects to web pages.

Animation Addons.description: Animation Addons is a WordPress plugin that extends the Elementor page builder with advanced animation effects and widgets for creating visually dynamic content.
Animation Addons.history: Animation Addons is a WordPress plugin extending Elementor with additional animation effects and interaction features for website building.
Animation Addons.summary_abuse: Animation Addons is a WordPress Elementor extension plugin with no notable abuse signal. It is a website builder enhancement tool.

anime.js.description: Anime.js (/ˈæn.ə.meɪ/) is a lightweight JavaScript animation library with a simple, yet powerful API.It works with CSS properties, SVG, DOM attributes and JavaScript Objects.
anime.js.history: Anime.js was created by French developer Julian Garnier and first published on GitHub in 2016. It became one of the most popular JavaScript animation libraries. Version 4, representing a major rewrite, was released in April 2025 after approximately five years of development.
anime.js.summary_abuse: Anime.js is a frontend JavaScript animation library with no notable abuse signal. It is a client-side tool that runs in the browser and has no server-side or content-hosting surface that fraudsters would target.

Aniview Ad Server.description: Aniview Ad Server is a technology developed by Aniview, a company that specialises in providing video advertising solutions. The Aniview Ad Server is a platform designed to manage and serve video ads to publishers, advertisers, and agencies.
Aniview Ad Server.history: Aniview was founded in 2013 by Alon Carmel (founder of JDate) and Tal Melenboim. The company specializes in video advertising technology, providing a patented video ad server, video player, and video marketplace serving over 200,000 publishers worldwide. It delivers approximately 15 billion ad impressions per month.
Aniview Ad Server.summary_abuse: Aniview Ad Server is a video advertising platform delivering ads across a large network of publishers. As ad-tech infrastructure, it participates in the programmatic ecosystem where ad fraud, malvertising, and privacy tracking are known concerns. Video ad networks have been implicated in malvertising campaigns in the broader ad-tech industry. The platform collects extensive user tracking data for ad targeting purposes.

Aniview Video Ad Player.description: Aniview Video Ad Player is a video player technology developed by Aniview, a company that specialises in providing video advertising solutions.
Aniview Video Ad Player.history: Aniview Video Ad Player is Aniview's patented HTML5 video player with built-in ad serving capabilities, offered alongside the Aniview Ad Server. Founded in 2013 by Alon Carmel and Tal Melenboim, the player is deployed across thousands of publisher websites for video monetization.
Aniview Video Ad Player.summary_abuse: Aniview Video Ad Player is a JavaScript video player embedding ads into publisher video content. Like other ad-tech video players, it has been associated with privacy tracking and is embedded across many publisher sites. Video players with built-in ad serving can be vectors for malvertising. No specific major incidents are uniquely documented for Aniview's player versus the broader ad-tech industry.

Annoto.description: Annoto is a platform that transforms video viewing into an interactive, social, and collaborative experience rather than a passive individual activity.
Annoto.history: Annoto is an Israeli startup that transforms video viewing into an interactive social experience by adding collaborative annotation and discussion layers to video content. It is focused on educational and enterprise learning platforms.
Annoto.summary_abuse: Annoto is a video annotation and discussion platform used primarily in educational contexts. No notable abuse signal is documented.

AnnounceKit.description: AnnounceKit is a platform facilitating announcements for product updates and company news.
AnnounceKit.history: AnnounceKit is a product update announcement and changelog platform for SaaS companies. It provides embeddable changelog widgets and notification systems to help product teams communicate updates to users.
AnnounceKit.summary_abuse: AnnounceKit is a product changelog and announcement widget with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B communication tool for SaaS companies.

ANS.description: ANS is a UK-based IT services company specializing in cloud computing, managed services, and digital transformation solutions.
ANS.history: ANS Group was founded in 1996 in Manchester, UK and is a cloud, security and digital transformation technology provider. The company has 750 employees and offers cloud hosting, colocation, data centers, and AI-driven solutions. It holds ISO 27001 and operates primarily in the UK enterprise market.
ANS.summary_abuse: ANS is a UK enterprise cloud and managed services provider with no documented abuse history. As a business-only infrastructure provider requiring enterprise contracts, it is not a platform with consumer-facing abuse risk.

Answerbase.description: Answerbase is a management platform for customer support and ecommerce, allowing users to ask questions, receive answers, and browse content and articles to meet their demand for information about products and services.
Answerbase.history: Answerbase is a customer support Q&A and knowledge base platform for e-commerce businesses. It allows companies to manage customer questions and product-specific Q&A directly on their website.
Answerbase.summary_abuse: Answerbase is a B2B customer support Q&A platform. No notable abuse signal is documented. It is used by e-commerce businesses to manage customer questions.

AnswerDash.description: AnswerDash is a question and answer platform that serves business customers thereby reducing support costs and revealing customer needs.
AnswerDash.history: AnswerDash is a self-service customer support and Q&A platform that provides contextual help to website visitors. It was founded around 2012 as a University of Washington spinoff.
AnswerDash.summary_abuse: AnswerDash is a customer self-service Q&A widget with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B support tool that displays help content on business websites.

Ant Design.description: Ant Design is a UI library that can be used with data flow solutions and application frameworks in any React ecosystem.
Ant Design.history: Ant Design was created by Ant Group (formerly Ant Financial, an Alibaba affiliate) and first publicly released in 2015. It is the world's second most popular React UI framework by GitHub stars and is used extensively by Alibaba, Tencent, Baidu, Meituan, and other major Chinese tech companies. The design system unifies the UI language across Ant Group's enterprise products.
Ant Design.summary_abuse: Ant Design is an Alibaba-backed React UI component library with no notable abuse signal. It is a development tool used to build enterprise web interfaces.

Antavo.description: Antavo is a software platform that provides AI-driven loyalty and promotion management solutions to help businesses design, automate, and analyze customer reward programs.
Antavo.history: Antavo was founded in 2012 in London by Hungarian entrepreneurs Attila Kecsmár, Zsuzsa Kecsmár, and Gábor Csarnai. The company pivoted in 2015 to focus on enterprise loyalty program management. It raised a Series A of £8.7 million. Antavo has approximately $18M ARR and serves large retail brands with AI-driven loyalty and promotion technology.
Antavo.summary_abuse: Antavo is a B2B enterprise loyalty management platform. No notable abuse signal is documented. It is used by large retailers to manage customer loyalty programs.

Antee IPO.description: Antee is a Czech company that will make a custom-made website for you, then you manage it in CMS IPO.
Antee IPO.history: Antee is a Czech company offering website creation and management services where the company builds a custom website and the client manages it thereafter. It is a small Czech web services provider.
Antee IPO.summary_abuse: Antee IPO is a small Czech website builder service. No notable abuse signal is documented.

Anthology Encompass.description: Anthology Encompass is a constituent engagement management provider or educational institutions that provides modules to help you manage events, websites and content, data, and more.
Anthology Encompass.history: Anthology Encompass is an alumni and constituent engagement management product for higher education institutions. It was originally developed as a standalone CRM and was acquired by Anthology, a higher education technology company formed through the merger of Campus Management, Campus Labs, and Blackboard in 2021.
Anthology Encompass.summary_abuse: Anthology Encompass is an enterprise higher education CRM used by university alumni relations teams. No notable abuse signal is documented.

AntiBot.Cloud.description: AntiBot.Cloud is a PHP script and cloud service to protect websites from bots and junk traffic.
AntiBot.Cloud.history: AntiBot.Cloud is a PHP script and cloud service providing bot protection for websites. It analyzes incoming HTTP requests to detect and block automated bot traffic and spam.
AntiBot.Cloud.summary_abuse: AntiBot.Cloud is a bot protection service designed to defend websites from automated attacks. No notable abuse signal is documented.

Antsomi CDP 365.description: Antsomi CDP 365 is a AI-enabled customer data platform from Southeast Asia.
Antsomi CDP 365.history: Antsomi CDP 365 is an AI-enabled customer data platform developed by Antsomi, a Vietnamese company. It is designed for Southeast Asian businesses and provides customer data unification, segmentation, and omnichannel marketing automation capabilities.
Antsomi CDP 365.summary_abuse: Antsomi CDP 365 is a Southeast Asian customer data platform. Its data handling practices are subject to Vietnamese and local regulations rather than GDPR. No specific documented abuse incidents are reported.

AntV G2.description: AntV G2 is a highly interactive data-driven visualisation grammar for statistical charts.
AntV G2.history: AntV G2 is Alibaba's open-source data visualization grammar library, part of the AntV visualization suite. It provides a grammar-of-graphics approach to building charts and is part of the broader Ant Group open-source ecosystem.
AntV G2.summary_abuse: AntV G2 is an Alibaba open-source data visualization library with no notable abuse signal. It is a development tool for building charts in web applications.

AntV G6.description: AntV G6 is a graph visualisation framework in JavaScript.
AntV G6.history: AntV G6 is Alibaba's open-source graph visualization library for rendering complex network and relational data. It is part of the AntV visualization family and is used for knowledge graphs, flowcharts, and network topology diagrams.
AntV G6.summary_abuse: AntV G6 is an Alibaba open-source graph visualization library with no notable abuse signal. It is a development tool.

Anubis.description: Anubis is a system that evaluates the characteristics of incoming HTTP requests to determine their legitimacy and enhance website protection.
Anubis.history: Anubis is an open-source HTTP reverse proxy bot challenge system created around 2024, designed to protect web services from AI crawlers and automated bot traffic by challenging incoming requests based on their characteristics.
Anubis.summary_abuse: Anubis is an open-source bot challenge tool designed as a defensive security measure. No abuse signal is documented.

Anura.description: Anura is an ad fraud detection solution designed to identify and mitigate fraudulent activity across digital advertising platforms.
Anura.history: Anura is an ad fraud detection platform designed to identify and mitigate invalid traffic including bots, scrapers, and fraudulent ad clicks. It has been providing ad fraud detection services for advertisers and publishers.
Anura.summary_abuse: Anura is an ad fraud detection service with no notable abuse signal. It is a defensive security tool used by advertisers to protect their ad budgets from fraud.

AnyChat.description: AnyChat is a real-time conversation system designed to enable communication and message exchange between users.
AnyChat.history: AnyChat is a real-time messaging and conversation platform enabling businesses to embed chat functionality into their websites. It is a small chat widget service with limited public history available.
AnyChat.summary_abuse: AnyChat is a website chat widget. Chat platforms can be used to facilitate phishing or social engineering, though no specific abuse incidents are documented for this particular service. It provides chat infrastructure that depends on how individual site operators configure and moderate it.

AnyClip.description: AnyClip is a video engagement platform that uses an AI-driven content analysis engine to analyze and categorize video content in real-time to create personalised video feeds.
AnyClip.history: AnyClip is a video engagement platform founded around 2008 that uses AI to analyze video content and enhance it with smart chapters, search, and personalized recommendations. It serves publishers and media companies looking to improve video monetization and engagement.
AnyClip.summary_abuse: AnyClip is a video engagement and monetization platform for publishers. No specific documented abuse incidents have been reported. It embeds tracking technologies as part of video analytics.

AnythingSlider.history: AnythingSlider is a jQuery-based image and content slider plugin that was popular in the early 2010s. It has since been abandoned as jQuery-dependent sliders became replaced by CSS-only and modern JavaScript alternatives.
AnythingSlider.summary_abuse: AnythingSlider is an abandoned jQuery slider plugin with no notable abuse signal.

AOLserver.history: AOLserver originated as NaviServer, developed by NaviSoft. AOL acquired NaviSoft in 1995 and the server was renamed AOLserver. Philip Greenspun convinced AOL to open-source the code in 1999 under the AOLserver Public License. It was the first HTTP server to combine multithreading, a built-in Tcl scripting language, and database connection pooling. Development activity has declined significantly and it is largely superseded by NaviServer, a fork.
AOLserver.summary_abuse: AOLserver is a legacy open-source web server with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history. It is rarely deployed in new production systems.

AOS.description: JavaScript library to animate elements on your page as you scroll.
AOS.history: AOS (Animate on Scroll) is a JavaScript library created by Michal Sajnog (michalsnik) and released on GitHub around 2015. It enables CSS-driven scroll animation effects. The library is MIT licensed and widely used but has seen limited active development in recent years.
AOS.summary_abuse: AOS is an open-source JavaScript animation library with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history.

Apache APISIX.description: Apache APISIX is an open-source, cloud-native API gateway developed by the Apache Software Foundation. It provides a scalable and high-performance solution for managing and securing API traffic.
Apache APISIX.history: Apache APISIX was originally created by Shengsheng Wang and Ming Wen and donated to the Apache Software Foundation in 2019, becoming a top-level project in 2020. Built on Nginx and OpenResty, it is a dynamic, real-time, high-performance API gateway. It originated from the Chinese tech ecosystem and is backed by API7.ai for commercial support.
Apache APISIX.summary_abuse: Apache APISIX is backend API gateway infrastructure with no notable abuse surface. It is a self-hosted routing layer for APIs and has no consumer-facing interface that could be exploited.

Apache HTTP Server.description: Apache is a free and open-source cross-platform web server software.
Apache HTTP Server.history: Apache HTTP Server was created in 1995 by a group of webmasters building on NCSA httpd patches, forming the Apache Group. It became the most widely used web server in the world. The Apache Software Foundation was founded in 1999 to govern the project. Apache held the largest web server market share for over two decades though it has since been gradually overtaken by Nginx and Cloudflare in some market segments.
Apache HTTP Server.summary_abuse: Apache HTTP Server is foundational web server software used by a large fraction of the internet. It is occasionally targeted by vulnerabilities (notably Log4Shell affecting Apache Log4j in 2021, and various httpd CVEs over the years). Apache HTTP Server itself does not have a consumer-facing surface for abuse; however, misconfigured Apache instances can inadvertently expose sensitive files. The ASF maintains a security team and CVE disclosure process.

Apache JSPWiki.description: Apache JSPWiki is an open-source Wiki engine, built around standard JEE components (Java, servlets, JSP).
Apache JSPWiki.history: Apache JSPWiki is a feature-rich wiki engine built on Java and JSP, first released in 2001 by Janne Jalkanen. It was donated to the Apache Software Foundation and became a top-level project. It is primarily used by organizations needing a Java-based wiki system.
Apache JSPWiki.summary_abuse: Apache JSPWiki is a self-hosted Java wiki engine with no notable abuse signal. Wikis can be vandalized or spammed if not properly secured, but this is an operator-level concern.

Apache Tomcat.description: Apache Tomcat is an open-source implementation of the Java Servlet, JavaServer Pages, Java Expression Language and WebSocket technologies.
Apache Tomcat.history: Apache Tomcat was originally developed by James Duncan Davidson at Sun Microsystems and donated to the Apache Software Foundation in 1999. It is an open-source Java Servlet and JSP container and became the de facto standard servlet container. Tomcat continues to be actively maintained and is used by enterprises worldwide as the foundation for Java web applications.
Apache Tomcat.summary_abuse: Apache Tomcat is foundational Java web server infrastructure. Several significant vulnerabilities have been discovered over the years (including the critical CVE-2019-0232 and Log4Shell exposure). Tomcat servers are sometimes targeted as part of Java exploit campaigns. The ASF maintains an active security response team and publishes CVEs promptly.

Apache Traffic Server.description: Apache Traffic Server is an open-source caching and proxying server that serves as an HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 reverse proxy with caching capabilities, load balancing, request routing, SSL termination, and support for advanced HTTP features.
Apache Traffic Server.history: Apache Traffic Server was originally developed internally at Inktomi Corporation and later acquired by Yahoo. Yahoo open-sourced it and donated it to the Apache Software Foundation in 2009, where it became a top-level project. It is a high-performance HTTP proxy cache used by large-scale web infrastructure.
Apache Traffic Server.summary_abuse: Apache Traffic Server is backend caching proxy infrastructure with no notable abuse surface. It is used by large web organizations to cache and proxy HTTP traffic.

Apache Wicket.description: Apache Wicket is an open-source Java web application framework for building scalable and maintainable web applications.
Apache Wicket.history: Apache Wicket is a Java web application framework first released in 2004 by Jonathan Locke and later donated to the Apache Software Foundation. It uses a component-based model that separates HTML and Java code. It was popular in the mid-2000s in the Java enterprise ecosystem.
Apache Wicket.summary_abuse: Apache Wicket is a Java web framework with no notable abuse signal. It is a development tool for building Java web applications.

Apanio.description: Apanio is a website platform that provides integrated ecommerce features, including a shopping cart, shipping tools, and payment processing, enabling users to create and manage online stores.
Apanio.history: Apanio is an e-commerce website platform with integrated shopping cart, shipping, and payment processing. Limited public history available.
Apanio.summary_abuse: Apanio has no notable abuse signal. It is a small e-commerce website platform with no documented patterns of fraudulent storefront abuse.

APC.description: APC offers door-to-door parcel and mail delivery.
APC.history: APC (Alternative PHP Cache / Advanced PHP Cache) was a PHP opcode caching PECL extension that cached compiled PHP bytecode in shared memory to improve performance. It was widely used until PHP 5.5 when Zend OPcache was bundled into PHP core, effectively replacing APC for opcode caching. The user-cache portion lives on as APCu (APC User Cache), which stripped the opcode caching from the original and is still maintained.
APC.summary_abuse: APC is a deprecated PHP server-side caching extension with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history.

Apereo CAS.description: Apereo CAS is an open and well-documented authentication protocol. The primary implementation of the protocol is an open-source Java server component by the same name hosted here, with support for a plethora of additional authentication protocols and features.
Apereo CAS.history: Apereo CAS (Central Authentication Service) was originally developed at Yale University around 2000 as an enterprise single sign-on protocol and software. It was later transferred to Jasig and then to Apereo Foundation, a higher education technology consortium. CAS is widely used in universities worldwide for SSO across campus applications.
Apereo CAS.summary_abuse: Apereo CAS is an open-source enterprise SSO server used primarily in higher education. A notable security vulnerability (CVE-2019-10678) allowed remote code execution, but patches were quickly released. As authentication infrastructure, it is a target for credential stuffing if misconfigured, but the software itself is not inherently abusive.

ApexCharts.js.description: ApexCharts is a modern JavaScript charting library that empowers developers to build interactive data visualizations for commercial and non-commercial projects.
ApexCharts.js.history: ApexCharts.js is an open-source JavaScript charting library released in 2018. It became popular as a modern, interactive, and SVG-based alternative to older libraries like Highcharts and Chart.js. It has thousands of GitHub stars and is widely used in React, Vue, and Angular dashboards.
ApexCharts.js.summary_abuse: ApexCharts.js is an open-source chart library with no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend visualization tool.

ApexChat.description: ApexChat is a company that provides businesses with live chat software and services to facilitate real-time customer engagement, support, lead generation, and enhanced online interactions.
ApexChat.history: ApexChat is a managed live chat service for business websites providing both software and chat agent staffing services. It focuses on converting website visitors into leads through human-staffed chat.
ApexChat.summary_abuse: ApexChat is a managed live chat service. Chat services can be used to facilitate social engineering, though no specific documented abuse incidents are reported for ApexChat specifically.

ApexPages.history: Apex Pages (also known as Visualforce) is Salesforce's proprietary server-side markup language for building custom user interfaces on the Salesforce platform. It was introduced around 2007 and is a core part of Salesforce customization. Salesforce has since introduced Lightning Web Components as a more modern alternative, but Visualforce/ApexPages remains in widespread use.
ApexPages.summary_abuse: ApexPages/Visualforce is Salesforce's proprietary page rendering technology used within the Salesforce platform. No notable abuse signal is documented. It is backend enterprise development technology.

Aphix.description: Aphix is a provider of a fully integrated ecommerce and mobile ordering platform.
Aphix.history: Aphix is an Irish e-commerce and B2B ordering platform for wholesale distributors and manufacturers. It provides digital commerce solutions for businesses looking to enable online ordering for their trade customers.
Aphix.summary_abuse: Aphix is a B2B e-commerce platform for wholesale distributors. No notable abuse signal is documented.

API Spreadsheets.description: API Spreadsheets is a developer tool for integrating spreadsheet features into applications, enabling data management, automation, and real-time updates.
API Spreadsheets.history: API Spreadsheets is a SaaS platform that allows developers to use spreadsheet files as a data source via REST API. The service targets developers and no-code builders wanting to use CSV or Excel files as quick backends. It is used by 10,000+ users according to the vendor.
API Spreadsheets.summary_abuse: API Spreadsheets is a developer-focused data API tool with no documented abuse history. The platform enables CSV/Excel files to be served as APIs, which is not a common abuse vector.

Apiary.description: Apiary is an API design stack that provides tools for designing, testing, and documenting APIs, built specifically to support developers throughout the API development process.
Apiary.history: Apiary was founded in 2011 in Prague by Jakub Nesetril and Lukas Linhart as an API design and documentation platform using the API Blueprint format. Oracle acquired Apiary in January 2017 to enhance Oracle API Platform Cloud Service. Oracle announced the deprecation of Apiary in October 2019, with the service officially closing in March 2023.
Apiary.summary_abuse: Apiary was an API documentation platform that has been deprecated and shut down by Oracle. No notable abuse signal was documented during its operational period.

Apigee.description: Apigee is an API gateway management tool to exchange data across cloud services and applications
Apigee.history: Apigee was founded in 2004 as Sonoa Systems in Santa Clara, rebranded as Apigee in 2010. It went public on NASDAQ in April 2015. Google acquired Apigee for $625 million in November 2016 and integrated it into Google Cloud. Apigee is now the Google Cloud API management platform, one of the market leaders in enterprise API management.
Apigee.summary_abuse: Apigee is an enterprise API management platform with no notable abuse surface. It is backend infrastructure used by enterprises to manage, secure, and monetize APIs. Access is controlled through Google Cloud IAM.

Apisearch.description: Apisearch is a real-time search platform for ecommerce.
Apisearch.history: Apisearch is an open-source search engine for websites and e-commerce stores developed by a Spanish team. It provides a fast, distributed search engine with a focus on developer-friendly APIs.
Apisearch.summary_abuse: Apisearch is an open-source search platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a backend search infrastructure tool.

Apizee.description: Apizee is a platform for easy, real-time, multi-device video interaction services.
Apizee.history: Apizee is a French video communication platform providing WebRTC-based video, voice, and messaging APIs for businesses to embed into their websites and apps. It focuses on customer service and support use cases.
Apizee.summary_abuse: Apizee is a B2B video communication API platform. No notable documented abuse signal. It is a customer service tool used by businesses to enable video calls with their customers.

Aplazame.description: Aplazame is a consumer credit company that provides instant financing service for online purchases. It combines an overtime payment method integrated at the ecommerce checkout with marketing tools to enable ecommerce to use financing as a promotional lever to boost sales.
Aplazame.history: Aplazame is a Spanish buy-now-pay-later (BNPL) fintech company founded in 2013 in Madrid. It provides installment payment solutions for Spanish e-commerce merchants, allowing customers to split purchases over time.
Aplazame.summary_abuse: Aplazame is a Spanish BNPL payment solution. BNPL services can be abused for fraud through false identity creation to obtain credit for goods. Aplazame operates under Spanish financial regulation (Banco de España oversight) which enforces AML and identity verification requirements.

Apodle.description: Apodle is a generic business management solution designed to support a range of operational and administrative functions across various industries.
Apodle.history: Apodle is a generic business management CRM solution. Very limited public history available.
Apodle.summary_abuse: Apodle has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche CRM with no consumer-facing content hosting features.

Apollo.description: Apollo is a fully-featured caching GraphQL client with integrations for React, Angular, and more.
Apollo.history: Apollo GraphQL was created by Meteor Development Group (MDG) in 2016, shortly after Facebook open-sourced GraphQL. MDG pivoted from the Meteor framework to focus entirely on GraphQL and Apollo, eventually rebranding as Apollo GraphQL Inc. The company raised $183M in funding from Andreessen Horowitz, Insight Partners, and others. A notable licensing event occurred when Apollo switched some of its platform components (Federation v2, Router) from MIT to Elastic License v2 (ELv2), while keeping core client libraries MIT-licensed.
Apollo.summary_abuse: Apollo is a GraphQL client and server library used primarily in enterprise and developer contexts with no notable abuse history. The library runs client-side and server-side as infrastructure code with no consumer-facing surface that would attract fraudulent use. No documented abuse patterns exist. The company publishes an acceptable use policy and maintains a responsible disclosure policy.

Apollo13Themes Rife.description: Apollo13Themes Rife is a great portfolio and photography WordPress theme with 7 ready-to-use demo layouts.
Apollo13Themes Rife.history: Apollo13Themes is a tiny Polish studio founded in 2011, run by two people: Daniel (designer) and Air (programmer). The Rife theme was developed as a creative portfolio and photography WordPress theme, with the free edition approved in the WordPress.org repository in January 2019. The studio has no notable acquisition or ownership events.
Apollo13Themes Rife.summary_abuse: Rife is a WordPress theme with no documented abuse history. WordPress themes can occasionally be distributed through unofficial channels with injected malware, but no such incidents are documented for Rife. The vendor distributes through the official WordPress.org repository and their own website, with no known issues regarding spam or fraudulent stores.

ApostropheCMS.description: ApostropheCMS is a powerful website builder platform built on an enterprise open source CMS.
ApostropheCMS.history: ApostropheCMS originated at P'unk Avenue, a Philadelphia digital agency, around 2009 when the team committed to keeping a free open-source CMS edition permanently available. The project was MIT-licensed from the start. Over time, P'unk Avenue spun off a dedicated business around Apostrophe to continue development. No acquisitions or major ownership changes have occurred.
ApostropheCMS.summary_abuse: ApostropheCMS is a self-hosted open-source CMS with no notable abuse history. As a content management system, sites built on it could theoretically host malicious content, but no documented abuse patterns specific to ApostropheCMS exist. The vendor publishes an MIT-licensed codebase with active GitHub contributions and standard security reporting guidance.

Appcast.description: Appcast is a service that allows job advertisements on a 'pay-per-applicant' basis across a network of career and consumer sites, instead of the traditional per-click or per-post charges.
Appcast.history: Appcast was founded in 2014 in Lebanon, New Hampshire by Chris Forman, Dzmitry Nikitsin, and Tim McKegney as a programmatic job advertising platform enabling pay-per-applicant recruitment ads. The Stepstone Group (a Axel Springer subsidiary) acquired Appcast on July 1, 2019. Under Stepstone, Appcast expanded internationally and acquired Bayard Advertising in July 2023, adding six US offices. By 2026, Appcast had grown to over 550 employees across North America and Europe.
Appcast.summary_abuse: Appcast is a programmatic recruitment advertising platform with limited abuse exposure. Job advertising platforms can occasionally be exploited to publish fraudulent job listings as part of recruitment scams targeting job seekers, but no specific documented abuse incidents are associated with Appcast. The platform operates in a B2B context with employer-side KYC requirements and has no public reports of widespread misuse.

Appcues.description: Appcues is a solution for measuring and improving product adoption.
Appcues.history: Appcues was founded in 2014 by Jonathan Kim and Jackson Noel in Boston, Massachusetts, as a no-code user onboarding and product adoption platform. The company raised $47.9 million in total funding including a $32.1 million Series B in 2022 led by Sierra Ventures and NewSpring. It has grown to serve over 1,000 customers with notable enterprise clients including Uber, Adobe, and ServiceNow.
Appcues.summary_abuse: Appcues is a legitimate B2B SaaS product adoption platform with no notable abuse signal. It is embedded on product dashboards and only loaded for authenticated users, offering no public-facing surface that would attract phishing or scam activity. The vendor publishes an AUP and privacy policy and is GDPR compliant.

AppDynamics.description: AppDynamics is an application performance management (APM) and IT operations analytics (ITOA) company based in San Francisco.
AppDynamics.history: AppDynamics was founded in 2008 by Jyoti Bansal as an application performance management (APM) platform. Cisco acquired the company for $3.7 billion in January 2017, the day before AppDynamics was scheduled to go public on the NYSE. Under Cisco, AppDynamics was merged into Splunk's observability portfolio in 2025 following Cisco's $28B acquisition of Splunk. AppDynamics remains one of the leading APM tools for large enterprises, competing with Dynatrace and New Relic.
AppDynamics.summary_abuse: AppDynamics is an enterprise APM tool used exclusively in legitimate business contexts with no documented abuse history. As infrastructure monitoring software deployed in corporate data centres, it has no consumer-facing surface that would attract fraud or phishing. Cisco's security governance applies to the product.

Appian.description: Appian is an enterprise low-code application platform.
Appian.history: Appian Corporation was founded in 1999 by Matthew Calkins, Michael Beckley, Robert Kramer, and Marc Wilson, all former MicroStrategy employees, in McLean, Virginia. The company pioneered business process management (BPM) software and in 2013 rebranded as a low-code automation platform. Appian went public on NASDAQ (APPN) on May 25, 2017. It has remained independent with Matthew Calkins as CEO and is used by major government agencies and enterprises worldwide.
Appian.summary_abuse: Appian is an enterprise-grade low-code BPM platform primarily serving government and large enterprise clients. It has no meaningful abuse signal and its complex, sales-gated deployment model makes it unsuitable for abuse. The platform is subject to strict government security requirements and FedRAMP authorization.

Appier.description: Appier is a platform that helps clients optimize advertising ROI.
Appier.history: Appier was founded in 2012 in Taipei, Taiwan by Chih-Han Yu, Winnie Lee, and Joe Su, with a focus on AI-driven marketing and customer engagement in the Asia-Pacific region. The company expanded across APAC and listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange in 2021, initially on the Growth Market segment before moving to the Prime Market in 2022. In 2025, Appier announced an acquisition of AdCreative.ai to expand its generative AI advertising capabilities.
Appier.summary_abuse: Appier is an ad-tech and AI marketing platform operating heavily in APAC. As an advertising technology company, it collects substantial behavioral data across partner publisher networks for targeting purposes, which carries inherent privacy risk. The platform is not typically associated with direct scam or phishing abuse, but its data collection practices and cross-site tracking capabilities warrant a moderate privacy concern score. The company operates under Japanese and Taiwanese regulatory frameworks.

Appifiny.description: Appifiny is a platform that provides a streamlined solution for developing, customizing, and launching apps.
Appifiny.history: Appifiny is a small Scotland-based team of developers and e-commerce professionals building apps and themes for the Shopify platform. The company focuses on conversion-boosting tools such as real-time purchase notification popups and order fulfillment integrations. No notable ownership changes or major events are documented.
Appifiny.summary_abuse: Appifiny is a niche Shopify app developer with no notable abuse signal. Its tools are limited to storefront enhancement widgets for legitimate e-commerce merchants and do not present a meaningful abuse surface.

Appjustable.description: Appjustable is a platform offering Weebly app solutions for creating websites, enabling users to design and manage their online presence through customizable tools and features tailored to individual or business needs.
Appjustable.history: Appjustable is a small independent developer creating apps and widgets for the Weebly website builder platform, primarily pop-up and conversion tools. The product is distributed through the Weebly App Center. No notable company history, ownership changes, or funding events are documented.
Appjustable.summary_abuse: Appjustable is a minor Weebly plugin vendor with no documented abuse signal. Its popup and widget tools operate within the Weebly ecosystem and do not present significant independent abuse risk.

Apple Business Chat.description: Apple Business Chat is a service from Apple that allows your organization to directly chat with your customers using the Messages app.
Apple Business Chat.history: Apple Business Chat (rebranded as Apple Messages for Business) was announced at WWDC 2017 and launched in 2018, enabling customers to contact businesses directly through the iMessage app on Apple devices. It was part of Apple's broader push into enterprise and customer engagement. Apple requires businesses to work with approved Messaging Service Providers (MSPs) to integrate the platform.
Apple Business Chat.summary_abuse: Apple Messages for Business is an Apple-controlled platform with strict approval requirements for participating businesses, making it highly resistant to abuse. Messages are end-to-end encrypted between user devices and Apple servers. Apple's strong anti-fraud and KYC controls for business registration, combined with its bug bounty program, mean abuse risk is low.

Apple iCloud Mail.description: Apple iCloud Mail is a webmail service provided by Apple, Inc.
Apple iCloud Mail.history: Apple iCloud Mail launched in 2011 as part of Apple's iCloud platform, replacing the earlier MobileMe service. It provides free @icloud.com, @me.com, and @mac.com email addresses to Apple ID holders. iCloud itself evolved from MobileMe (2008) which replaced .Mac (2000). Apple expanded iCloud Mail with Hide My Email in 2021, allowing users to create random relay addresses to protect their real email.
Apple iCloud Mail.summary_abuse: Apple iCloud Mail accounts are occasionally used in phishing and spam campaigns due to the perceived trustworthiness of the @icloud.com domain. Apple maintains anti-abuse controls and rate limiting on outbound email, and the service requires an Apple ID with device-based verification, which limits mass account creation. Apple responds to abuse reports and maintains the domain's reputation diligently.

Apple MapKit JS.description: Apple MapKit JS lets you embed interactive maps directly into your websites across platforms and operating systems, including iOS and Android.
Apple MapKit JS.history: Apple MapKit JS was introduced at WWDC 2018, bringing Apple Maps to the web as a JavaScript library. It allows developers to embed interactive Apple Maps in websites with features including annotations, overlays, search, and directions. The free tier provides 250,000 map views per day per Apple Developer Program membership.
Apple MapKit JS.summary_abuse: Apple MapKit JS is a first-party Apple developer API with no meaningful abuse signal. Access requires an Apple Developer Program membership and JWT-based authentication, making unauthorized or abusive use difficult. Apple Maps data is not used for ad targeting.

Apple Pay.description: Apple Pay is a mobile payment and digital wallet service by Apple that allows users to make payments in person, in iOS apps, and on the web.
Apple Pay.history: Apple Pay was launched on October 20, 2014, alongside the iPhone 6 and Apple Watch, as Apple's contactless payment solution using NFC technology. It uses device-specific card numbers and dynamic security codes (tokenization) rather than transmitting actual card numbers. It has expanded to web payments (2016), peer-to-peer payments (Apple Cash, 2017), and is accepted at hundreds of millions of locations worldwide.
Apple Pay.summary_abuse: Apple Pay itself is a highly secure payment system using tokenization and biometric authentication, making it extremely resistant to direct abuse. Some fraud attempts involve social engineering users into authorizing Apple Pay transactions, but these target users rather than the platform infrastructure. Apple has strong fraud detection and is PCI-DSS compliant.

Apple Sign-in.description: Apple Sign-in is based on OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect, and provides a privacy-friendly way for users to sign in to websites and apps.
Apple Sign-in.history: Sign in with Apple was launched at WWDC 2019 and made available to end users in September 2019 with iOS 13. Apple mandated that any app offering third-party SSO (Google, Facebook) must also offer Sign in with Apple. The service is notable for its Hide My Email feature, which gives users a private relay address instead of sharing their real email, making it one of the most privacy-preserving SSO options available.
Apple Sign-in.summary_abuse: Sign in with Apple has no meaningful abuse signal. The platform does not share user data with app developers beyond a user identifier and optional email, and its Hide My Email feature actively protects user privacy. Apple's strong identity verification and developer program requirements prevent unauthorized use.

ApplicantStack.description: ApplicantStack is a full-service applicant tracking system that automates and streamlines all stages of the hiring process.
ApplicantStack.history: ApplicantStack was founded in 2009 and is an applicant tracking and onboarding software based in Cary, North Carolina. In 2018, the company was acquired by SwipeClock, a workforce management software provider. It serves over 2,500 companies primarily in the SMB segment.
ApplicantStack.summary_abuse: ApplicantStack is a B2B applicant tracking system used by HR teams. It processes candidate personal data and requires strong data handling practices. No notable abuse or security incidents are publicly documented, and its enterprise customer focus limits exposure to consumer-facing abuse scenarios.

Application Request Routing.description: Application Request Routing (ARR) is an extension to Internet Information Server (IIS), which enables an IIS server to function as a load balancer.
Application Request Routing.history: Application Request Routing (ARR) is a free IIS extension developed by Microsoft, first released around 2007. It enables IIS servers to act as load balancers and reverse proxies for HTTP traffic routing, with features including rule-based routing, server health monitoring, client affinity, and disk caching. ARR is a key component in many Windows Server-based web hosting and enterprise deployments.
Application Request Routing.summary_abuse: Application Request Routing is a backend Microsoft IIS infrastructure module with no consumer-facing surface. It has no meaningful abuse signal as it is a server-side routing component that requires Windows Server administration access to configure.

Applied CSR24.description: CSR24 is a portal login used by insurance agencies and brokerages that allows clients to access and review their policies.
Applied CSR24.history: Applied CSR24 is a self-service customer portal product developed by Applied Systems, a major insurance technology company. It allows insurance agency clients to access their policy documents, make payments, and file claims through a web and mobile portal. Applied Systems is backed by private equity and is a dominant player in insurance agency management software.
Applied CSR24.summary_abuse: Applied CSR24 is a B2B insurance client portal with no notable public abuse signal. The platform handles sensitive insurance policy data and requires integration with agency management systems, making it unsuitable as an abuse vector. Applied Systems is a well-established enterprise vendor.

APPLOVIN.history: AppLovin was founded in 2012 by Adam Foroughi, John Krystynak, and Andrew Karam in Palo Alto, California. The company went public on Nasdaq (APP) in April 2021. It grew from a mobile game marketing platform into a broader ad-tech company. In 2025, AppLovin divested its mobile games studios (Lions Studios) to Tripledot for $800 million to focus entirely on its advertising business. By mid-2025 AppLovin was valued at $123 billion.
APPLOVIN.summary_abuse: AppLovin is a large mobile ad-tech company that embeds SDKs in mobile apps to collect extensive behavioral and device data for targeted advertising. Like most mobile ad networks, it has faced scrutiny over data collection practices, especially regarding children's apps. The programmatic advertising ecosystem it operates in has documented ad fraud problems. AppLovin participates in MRC audit programs and industry anti-fraud initiatives.

AppNexus.description: AppNexus is a cloud-based software platform that enables and optimizes programmatic online advertising.
AppNexus.history: AppNexus was founded in 2007 by Brian O'Kelley, Mike Nolet, and others, drawing talent from Right Media. It grew into one of the largest independent programmatic advertising platforms. AT&T acquired AppNexus in 2018 for approximately $1.6 billion and rebranded it as Xandr. On December 21, 2021, AT&T agreed to sell Xandr to Microsoft, and the acquisition closed in June 2022. Microsoft subsequently retired the Xandr brand in 2023, folding the technology into Microsoft Advertising. In 2025, Microsoft announced the shutdown of the Xandr Invest buy-side DSP.
AppNexus.summary_abuse: AppNexus is an ad-tech platform used to serve programmatic display advertising across the web. As with all open ad exchanges, the AppNexus/Xandr platform has historically been implicated in serving malvertising — malicious ads delivered through legitimate publisher inventory. Industry researchers and HUMAN Security have flagged AppNexus inventory in malvertising campaigns. Microsoft has significantly tightened ad quality policies since the acquisition, but programmatic ad exchanges inherently carry moderate abuse risk due to the high volume and automated nature of ad serving.

Appointedd.description: Appointedd is an online booking technology.
Appointedd.history: Appointedd was founded in 2011 by Leah Hutcheon in Scotland, UK, as an online booking and scheduling platform for businesses. The company raised £2.5 million in investment and expanded to serve bookings in 167 countries for clients including Charlotte Tilbury and Mamas & Papas. Appointedd is a Certified B Corporation.
Appointedd.summary_abuse: Appointedd is a legitimate online scheduling platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform is used by verified businesses for appointment booking and does not provide open public hosting or anonymous access that would attract abuse.

Appointo.description: Appointo is a Shopify based booking engine from Sidepanda.
Appointo.history: Appointo is a Shopify appointment and booking app enabling merchants to schedule services and appointments directly from their Shopify stores. It is a relatively new entrant in the booking app market with a freemium model distributed through the Shopify App Store. No significant funding or ownership events are documented.
Appointo.summary_abuse: Appointo is a niche Shopify booking widget with no notable abuse signal. It operates within the Shopify ecosystem and does not expose any independent hosting or anonymous access surface that would attract abusers.

Appointy.description: Appointy is a cloud-based scheduling solution that helps professionals and businesses to manage their appointment scheduling activities and routines.
Appointy.history: Appointy was founded around 2007 by Nemesh Singh and Sarang Verma in Bhopal, India, as an online appointment scheduling SaaS. It gained recognition as one of India's early SaaS startups in the scheduling space and expanded to serve businesses globally with a freemium model.
Appointy.summary_abuse: Appointy is a scheduling SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is used by small businesses for customer booking and does not expose file hosting, anonymous content creation, or other abuse-prone features.

AppSell.description: AppSell is a cross-selling app designed for Wix that helps online stores recommend additional products to customers based on their shopping behavior, potentially increasing sales and improving customer engagement.
AppSell.history: AppSell is an Israeli startup headquartered in Givatayim offering upsell and cross-sell funnels specifically for Wix e-commerce stores. The company was incorporated in the UK as APPSELL LIMITED around 2013 but operates out of Israel. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are recorded.
AppSell.summary_abuse: AppSell is a Wix app for product upselling with no documented abuse history. As a checkout-layer tool embedded within Wix stores, its consumer surface is limited to upsell prompts inside existing merchant storefronts. No reports of fraud exploitation specific to AppSell exist. The tool processes purchase recommendations but relies on Wix's payment infrastructure for transactions.

Appsflyer.description: AppsFlyer is a SaaS mobile marketing analytics and attribution platform.
Appsflyer.history: AppsFlyer was founded in 2011 in Herzliya, Israel, by Oren Kaniel (CEO) and Reshef Mann (CTO), two high school friends who identified the opportunity in mobile attribution analytics. The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has raised over $300 million in funding across multiple rounds, achieving a valuation exceeding $1.6 billion. It serves over 80,000 companies globally and is the market leader in mobile attribution.
Appsflyer.summary_abuse: AppsFlyer is a leading mobile attribution and marketing analytics platform. As an ad-tech company, it tracks user behavior across mobile apps at scale, collecting significant amounts of behavioral data, which presents inherent privacy considerations. It is not typically associated with direct fraud or scam abuse, but its SDK is embedded in many apps and collects cross-app behavioral data. The company is GDPR compliant and publishes data privacy certifications.

Appstle.description: Appstle is a platform that boosts ecommerce revenue through retention-focused customer experiences.
Appstle.history: Appstle is a Shopify app developer that launched around 2020, offering a suite of tools for subscriptions, memberships, bundles, and loyalty programs. It has grown rapidly to become one of the top-rated subscription apps on the Shopify App Store with over 3,000 five-star reviews and 40,000+ merchant users. No major ownership changes are documented.
Appstle.summary_abuse: Appstle is a Shopify subscription management tool with no notable abuse signal. It operates within Shopify's verified app ecosystem and handles recurring billing for legitimate merchant subscriptions. No fraud or scam incidents are publicly documented.

Apptus.description: Apptus is an AI-powered ecommerce optimisation software provider.
Apptus.history: Apptus Technologies was founded in 2000 in Lund, Sweden by Michael Mokhberi as an e-commerce search personalization and merchandising platform. In December 2021, Apptus was acquired by Voyado, a Swedish customer experience platform, for approximately $44 million. The Apptus eSales product continued under the Voyado brand.
Apptus.summary_abuse: Apptus (now part of Voyado) is a B2B e-commerce search and personalization platform used by retailers. It processes behavioral shopping data but is not associated with consumer-facing abuse or fraud. No notable security incidents or abuse reports are documented.

AppuOnline.description: AppuOnline is a tool that provides embeddable stock charts for integration into websites or applications.
AppuOnline.history: AppuOnline (Appuonline Consultancy Services P Ltd) is an Indian website and service that provides stock market tips, BSE/NSE share price data, investment calculators, and financial tools for Indian retail investors. The company operates primarily in the Indian share market information space.
AppuOnline.summary_abuse: AppuOnline is a small Indian stock market tips and financial information website. It provides free share market signals and tips targeted at Indian retail traders. Such tip-providing services have historically been associated with pump-and-dump schemes in Indian markets, though no specific documented incidents for AppuOnline are publicly known.

Appwrite.description: Appwrite is an end-to-end backend server for web, mobile or native applications packaged as a set of Docker microservices.
Appwrite.history: Appwrite was open-sourced in October 2019 by Eldad Fux in Tel Aviv, Israel, as a backend-as-a-service (BaaS) platform providing auth, databases, storage, functions, and messaging. The company was formally incorporated in 2020 and raised $10 million in a seed round in September 2021 from Bessemer Venture Partners and Flybridge, and a further $27 million in 2022, totaling $37 million raised.
Appwrite.summary_abuse: Appwrite is an open-source backend platform that can be self-hosted or used via its cloud offering. As with any BaaS, misconfigured instances may expose user data, and the free cloud tier could theoretically be used to host malicious backend logic. However, Appwrite's open-source community and transparent codebase mean security issues are addressed quickly. No major abuse incidents are publicly documented.

Appy Pie Builder.description: Appypie is a platform that enables the creation of apps and websites using AI.
Appy Pie Builder.history: Appy Pie was founded on January 21, 2016 by Abhinav Girdhar in Noida, India, as a no-code platform enabling non-technical users to build mobile apps without coding. The company expanded into website builders, chatbots, workflow automation, and AI tools. Over 10 million users have used the platform.
