In 2025 the company launched generative AI models PixelForge and Vibeo.
Appy Pie Builder.summary_abuse: Appy Pie Builder is a no-code app and website builder that has been used to create fraudulent and scam applications. Security researchers have noted that its low barrier to entry and ease of publishing apps makes it attractive for building fake apps, phishing pages, and scam storefronts. The platform has a mixed reputation and limited enforcement. Apps published through it require separate app store review.

Appzi.description: Appzi is a customer experience software that is a user feedback and bug reporting tool.
Appzi.history: Appzi was founded in 2017 in Washington, USA, as a lightweight user feedback widget for SaaS products. The company has fewer than 10 employees and targets startups and small businesses. No notable funding rounds or ownership changes are documented.
Appzi.summary_abuse: Appzi is a small feedback widget tool with no notable abuse signal. Its limited feature set and small user base mean it does not present a meaningful abuse surface.

Aprimo.description: Aprimo is a United States-based company that develops and sells marketing automation software and digital asset management technology.
Aprimo.history: Aprimo was founded in 1998 in Indianapolis, Indiana, as one of the first Marketing Resource Management (MRM) software companies. In 2011, Teradata acquired Aprimo for approximately $525 million. In 2016, Marlin Equity Partners purchased the marketing operations division from Teradata for approximately $90 million, relaunching it as the independent Aprimo brand. The company later acquired Belgium-based ADAM Software (a DAM provider) to expand its digital asset management capabilities.
Aprimo.summary_abuse: Aprimo is an enterprise marketing operations and digital asset management platform used by large organizations. It has no notable abuse signal. The platform is sales-gated, enterprise-only, and does not present a consumer-facing surface that would attract fraudulent use.

Apruvd.description: Apruvd is a fraud protection system designed to detect and prevent unauthorized or suspicious transactions.
Apruvd.history: Apruvd was founded in May 2015 in Phoenix, Arizona, as an e-commerce fraud protection and chargeback prevention service. It uses a combination of automation, big data analysis, and human review to identify legitimate orders. The company integrates with Shopify and BigCommerce platforms.
Apruvd.summary_abuse: Apruvd is a fraud detection and chargeback guarantee service for e-commerce merchants. It is focused on preventing fraud rather than enabling it, and has no notable abuse signal. The service processes payment transaction data which requires responsible data handling.

Aptania.description: Aptania is an all-in-one customer data platform that provides web analytics, lead generation, and customer insights in a single system.
Aptania.history: Aptania was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in Windsor, United Kingdom. The company provides a customer data platform (CDP) and eCRM system designed to consolidate data from multiple digital sources including mobile, online, point of sale, and social media into a single customer view. It targets B2B and B2C organizations that need unified marketing intelligence and lead generation capabilities.
Aptania.summary_abuse: Aptania is a legitimate UK-based marketing technology startup. No credible reports of abuse, data misuse, or dark patterns. The platform is GDPR-compliant by design given its UK headquarters and focus on customer data management.

AptusShop.description: AptusShop is proprietary online store software created from scratch and developed by Aptus.pl.
AptusShop.history: AptusShop is a Polish e-commerce platform operated by Aptus.pl, a company that has been active for over 20 years helping businesses launch and run online stores. The platform was developed from scratch by the Aptus team and is purpose-built for Polish and regional European markets. More than 1,000 companies have used the software, ranging from small online shops to large retailers including those in pharmacy, books, and automotive sectors. The company offers a 3-week free trial.
AptusShop.summary_abuse: AptusShop is a legitimate Polish e-commerce software provider with two decades of operating history. No reports of abuse or unethical practices. It serves a primarily Central European customer base and complies with GDPR.

Apxium.description: Apxium is a software solution designed for automating accounts payable processes.
Apxium.history: Apxium is an Australian fintech and SaaS company that provides accounts receivable automation and payment processing tools specifically designed for accounting and professional services firms. The platform integrates with major practice management systems including MYOB, Xero, APS, and IRIS. Apxium automates invoice delivery, payment collection, and reconciliation, reducing debtor days for accounting firms. The company describes itself as a SaaS and fintech startup changing the way accounting firms manage cash flow.
Apxium.summary_abuse: Apxium is a legitimate Australian fintech startup serving the professional services sector. No reports of abuse, data misuse, or unethical billing practices. The company handles financial transactions and integrates with mainstream practice management systems.

AquilaCMS.description: AquilaCMS is a fullstack, headless CMS written in JavaScript.
AquilaCMS.history: AquilaCMS is a French open-source e-commerce CMS built on Node.js with a MERN stack (MongoDB, Express, React, Node). It was developed by a French team as an all-in-one e-commerce solution with an API-first architecture and Docker-based installation. The project is available on GitHub and targets developers seeking a modern JavaScript-based alternative to traditional PHP e-commerce platforms.
AquilaCMS.summary_abuse: AquilaCMS is an open-source e-commerce CMS that can be self-hosted by anyone. Like other open-source store platforms, it could be used to create fake or fraudulent storefronts, but this is a general risk shared by all self-hosted e-commerce software. No specific abuse incidents are documented for AquilaCMS.

Arabot.description: Arabot is a no-code chatbot platform designed to automate customer interactions and service fulfillment at scale.
Arabot.history: Arabot was founded in 2016 by Abdallah Faza and Kais Hassan in Dammam, Saudi Arabia, as an Arabic-language AI chatbot platform for enterprises in the Middle East and North Africa. The company raised $1 million in seed funding in 2019 and has developed chatbots for customers including the National Bank of Kuwait, Vodafone Qatar, and Aramex.
Arabot.summary_abuse: Arabot is an enterprise chatbot platform operating primarily in the MENA region with no notable public abuse signal. Its enterprise-gated sales model and focus on large regional enterprises make it unsuitable as an abuse vector.

Arastta.description: Arastta is a free and open-source project with contributors from all over the world.
Arastta.history: Arastta was launched in May 2015 as a community-driven open-source e-commerce platform, forked from OpenCart by the team behind MijoShop (an OpenCart-based Joomla component). It was built with modern frameworks including Symfony and Bootstrap. The GitHub repository was archived by the owner on April 26, 2022, and the project is no longer maintained.
Arastta.summary_abuse: Arastta is an abandoned open-source e-commerce platform. Like other self-hosted store software, it could be used to create fraudulent storefronts, but the project is no longer maintained and its use has declined significantly since the 2022 archival. Its abandoned status also means security vulnerabilities are unlikely to be patched.

Arbor.js.description: Arbor.js is a graph visualization library that uses web workers for performance and relies on jQuery for DOM manipulation and interaction.
Arbor.js.history: Arbor.js is a JavaScript graph visualization library using web workers and jQuery, created around 2011 by Christian Swinehart (samizdatco) and influenced by Jeffrey Bernstein's Traer Physics library. It provides a force-directed layout algorithm for network graph visualization and leaves screen rendering to the developer. The library has not received updates in many years and is effectively unmaintained.
Arbor.js.summary_abuse: Arbor.js is a client-side JavaScript graph visualization library with no consumer-facing surface or abuse potential. It is a frontend rendering tool with no backend, no data collection, and no network services.

Arc.description: Arc is a peer-to-peer CDN that pays site owners for using it. Instead of expensive servers in distant datacenters, Arc's network is comprised of browsers.
Arc.history: Arc browser was developed by The Browser Company, founded in 2019 by Josh Miller and Harsh Agrawal in New York. The browser launched for macOS in 2022 and publicly in 2023. The company raised $128 million total including a $50 million Series B in March 2024 at a $550 million valuation. In 2025, Atlassian acquired The Browser Company for $610 million. In May 2025, the company announced it would stop developing Arc to focus on a new AI browser called Dia.
Arc.summary_abuse: Arc is a Chromium-based web browser with no notable abuse signal as a technology. As a browser, it inherits Chromium's security model. It does not provide web hosting or content creation capabilities. The Atlassian acquisition provides substantial backing. No documented abuse incidents.

Arc XP.description: Arc XP is a cloud-based digital experience platform that helps enterprise companies, retail brands and media and entertainment organization create and distribute content, drive digital commerce, and deliver powerful experiences.
Arc XP.history: Arc XP (originally Arc Publishing) was developed internally by The Washington Post starting around 2013 following Jeff Bezos's acquisition of the newspaper. The platform was built entirely on AWS and initially used to power the Post's own digital operations. In 2015-2017, the Post began licensing the platform to other media companies. In April 2021, Arc Publishing rebranded as Arc XP to reflect its expansion beyond media publishing into broader enterprise digital experience use cases.
Arc XP.summary_abuse: Arc XP is an enterprise digital experience platform used by major media organizations. It has no notable abuse signal. The platform is sales-gated with enterprise contracts and is used by reputable global media companies. Its backing by The Washington Post and AWS infrastructure provides significant security oversight.

ARCaptcha.description: ARCaptcha is a CAPTCHA system designed to enhance online security by preventing automated bots from accessing websites.
ARCaptcha.history: ARCaptcha is an Iranian cybersecurity startup founded in 2019 that provides AI-powered bot protection and CAPTCHA services. The platform is used by over 300 Iranian businesses for website security. It is a knowledge-based company operating under Iranian law.
ARCaptcha.summary_abuse: ARCaptcha is an Iranian bot protection and CAPTCHA service. As an Iranian-hosted security service, it operates outside GDPR jurisdiction and has limited Western compliance documentation. The platform is used primarily by Iranian businesses. No specific abuse incidents documented, but embedding third-party scripts from Iranian servers raises data sovereignty concerns for non-Iranian websites.

ArcGIS API for JavaScript.description: ArcGIS API for JavaScript is a tool used to embed maps and tasks in web applications.
ArcGIS API for JavaScript.history: Esri released the first version of the ArcGIS API for JavaScript around 2009, based on Esri's history of GIS software development going back to 1969. Version 4.x was introduced in 2016 as a complete rewrite with 2D/3D capabilities. The 3.x API was officially retired on July 1, 2024. The library was renamed ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript with version 5.0. Esri, headquartered in Redlands, California, is privately held and is the dominant commercial GIS platform globally.
ArcGIS API for JavaScript.summary_abuse: The ArcGIS API for JavaScript is a mapping and GIS library with no meaningful abuse signal. It is a developer API for building enterprise GIS applications and has no consumer-facing surface. Esri is a trusted enterprise GIS vendor with strong security practices.

Arco Design Vue.description: Arco Design is a set of open-source UI Components for Vue, aiming to address experience issues in products.
Arco Design Vue.history: Arco Design Vue is an open-source Vue 3 UI component library created by ByteDance's GIP UED and Infrastructure Frontend teams, open-sourced in October 2021. It is based on ByteDance's internal Arco Design system developed over three years for mid- and back-end product UIs. The library provides over 70 Vue 3 components with comprehensive theming support.
Arco Design Vue.summary_abuse: Arco Design Vue is a frontend UI component library with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse potential. It is a pure client-side rendering library with no network services or data collection.

ArcSpan.description: ArcSpan is an audience monetization DMP platform enabling direct sold and programmatic deal results for the world’s finest publishers and retail media networks.
ArcSpan.history: ArcSpan Technologies was founded in 2020 by Art Muldoon and Garret Vreeland, former executives from Accordant Media (acquired by Dentsu in 2016), in New York. The company provides a first-party audience data monetization platform for publishers and retail media networks, specializing in contextual advertising targeting.
ArcSpan.summary_abuse: ArcSpan is an ad-tech platform for publisher audience monetization. As a contextual and first-party data advertising platform, it handles significant user behavioral data, which carries inherent privacy considerations. It is not directly associated with fraud or scam abuse, but operates in a sector that intersects with privacy-sensitive data practices.

Arena.description: Arena widget is an embeddable widget provided by the Arena platform, which allows users to embed live blogs directly on their website.
Arena.history: Arena was founded in 2016 by Paulo Martins and Rodrigo De Castro Reis in San Francisco, Brazil. The company provides live engagement tools including group chat, live blogs, and audience interaction platforms for media, sports, and entertainment companies. Arena has raised $15.9 million from investors including CRV, Techstars, and Redpoint Ventures.
Arena.summary_abuse: Arena is a live engagement and chat platform. Public-facing chat widgets can be used for spam or harassment in event contexts, but Arena serves B2B customers who control moderation settings. No notable platform-level abuse incidents are publicly documented.

Arengu.description: Arengu is an online form builder designed to streamline user onboarding.
Arengu.history: Arengu was founded in 2018 by Pablo Castro Valiño, Sergio Regueira, and Jacobo Vidal Abeigón in A Coruña, Spain. The Y Combinator-backed startup provides a no-code/low-code platform for building signup flows and user onboarding forms. The company has 10 employees.
Arengu.summary_abuse: Arengu is a form and signup flow builder with no notable abuse signal. Its B2B customer base and form-building focus do not create significant public abuse surfaces.

Argento.description: Argento is an optimised and a responsive Magento template designed to enhance website performance and improve user experience.
Argento.history: Argento is a premium Magento 2 theme developed by Swissuplabs, a Ukrainian Magento extension and theme developer. The theme provides multiple design styles (Flat, Stripes, Luxury, Pure2, Mall, Essence) and includes bundled extensions for SEO, search, and product display. It is sold through the Adobe Commerce Marketplace.
Argento.summary_abuse: Argento is a Magento theme sold through the Adobe Commerce Marketplace. As a self-hosted e-commerce theme, it could be installed on fraudulent storefronts, but this is an indirect risk common to all e-commerce platform themes. No direct abuse incidents involving the Argento theme itself are documented.

ARI Network Services.description: ARI Network Services provides website, software, and data solutions to help dealers, distributors, and OEMs improve their selling process.
ARI Network Services.history: ARI Network Services was founded in 1981 in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, and is a provider of ecommerce, eCatalog, and digital marketing solutions for equipment dealers, manufacturers, and distributors. The company serves 22,000+ dealers, 195 distributors, and 140 manufacturers globally. It received strategic investment from TA Associates.
ARI Network Services.summary_abuse: ARI Network Services is a B2B ecommerce and parts catalog platform for equipment dealers. No documented abuse history. The platform requires dealer contracts and is not a consumer-facing tool with anonymous access.

Arketa.description: Arketa is a business platform for the fitness and wellness industry, providing tools to manage operations and support business growth.
Arketa.history: Arketa was founded in 2020 by Josh Archer and Rachel Lea Fishman in New York. The company emerged during the COVID-19 pandemic to help fitness and wellness studios manage their operations digitally. It participated in Y Combinator and raised a $15M Series A round. The platform serves fitness studios, yoga, pilates, dance, and martial arts businesses with scheduling, payments, and client management.
Arketa.summary_abuse: Arketa is a legitimate vertical SaaS platform for the wellness and fitness industry. No credible reports of data misuse, dark patterns, or abusive business practices. It is a small but reputable startup serving a niche professional market.

Arkose Labs.description: Arkose Labs is a toolkit for fraud prevention that provides solutions to detect and mitigate malicious activity across digital platforms.
Arkose Labs.history: Arkose Labs was founded in 2013 by Kevin Gosschalk as FunCaptcha, an interactive CAPTCHA solution that uses visual puzzles to distinguish humans from bots. The company rebranded as Arkose Labs to reflect its broader fraud prevention mission. It has raised $100+ million including from PayPal Ventures, M12, SoftBank, and Sony Innovation Fund. Clients include Microsoft, Meta, Roblox, and major banks.
Arkose Labs.summary_abuse: Arkose Labs is a bot detection and fraud prevention platform, specifically designed to stop abuse rather than enable it. The platform itself is a trust signal for users and enterprises. No abuse of the platform itself is publicly documented. Its CAPTCHA challenges are embedded on high-value consumer-facing login and registration flows.

Arlo.description: Arlo is an event management tool that helps organize, schedule, and track events.
Arlo.history: Arlo was founded in 2007 by John Mitchell in Wellington, New Zealand. It is a training management system (TMS) designed for professional training providers, compliance training, and commercial training organizations. Arlo operates in over 70 countries and has processed over one billion dollars in training transactions. The platform focuses on course scheduling, registrations, and training administration rather than self-paced e-learning.
Arlo.summary_abuse: Arlo is a legitimate training management SaaS with a long operating history. No credible reports of abuse, dark patterns, or privacy violations. The company is headquartered in New Zealand and serves professional training providers globally.

ARM JS.description: ARM JS is a JavaScript library that centralizes data management and streamlines interactions with APIs.
ARM JS.history: ARM JS (arm-js-library) is a JavaScript library developed by Michael Gutierrez for centralizing API resource management in frontend applications. It is a small community library on GitHub with limited adoption.
ARM JS.summary_abuse: ARM JS is a small open-source JavaScript API management library with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history.

Armin.description: Armin is a customer experience software solution for ecommerce that centralizes interaction data, supports service management, and helps maintain online operations.
Armin.history: Armin (ChatArmin) is a WhatsApp marketing and customer communication platform that enables businesses to engage customers via WhatsApp newsletters, automated customer journeys, and direct messaging. The product appears to be an early-stage Austrian startup focused on messaging automation for marketing use cases. It targets SMBs seeking to use WhatsApp as a marketing channel.
Armin.summary_abuse: Armin is a small WhatsApp marketing automation tool. No credible reports of abuse. As a messaging platform it inherits WhatsApp policy requirements around opt-in messaging, which limits bulk spam scenarios. Low abuse risk for a niche startup.

Arnica.description: Arnica is a CRM system that consolidates customer relationship processes into a single platform.
Arnica.history: Arnica was founded in 2021 in Alpharetta, Georgia, as a developer-native application security platform. The company raised seed funding of approximately $7 to $21 million (reported variously) and has been featured as an innovative approach to AppSec by integrating security directly into developer workflows without CI/CD pipeline requirements. Arnica offers SAST, SCA, secret scanning, and IaC security with pipelineless GitHub integration, and launched an AI security feature called Arnie in 2025.
Arnica.summary_abuse: Arnica is a legitimate application security company with no reported abuse or malicious behavior. The company operates in the cybersecurity space, offers responsible disclosure, and its products are designed to improve security posture for software teams.

AroSoftware.description: AroSoftware provides real estate CRM and website design solutions.
AroSoftware.history: AroSoftware (Arosoftware Pty Ltd) is an Australian company that provides a managed website builder and CRM platform exclusively for real estate agents. The platform integrates with real estate portals to automatically display property listings, manage leads, and optimize SEO. It serves sales, property management, and commercial real estate agencies across Australia with a drag-and-drop website editor and automated content updates.
AroSoftware.summary_abuse: AroSoftware is a legitimate niche website platform serving Australian real estate agents. No reports of abuse or privacy violations. The company operates in a straightforward B2B context with no identified dark patterns.

Arreva.description: Arreva is a fundraising software that provides the ability to mobilise constituents using the donor tracking system.
Arreva.history: Arreva was formed in 2017 through a merger of DonorCommunity, founded in 2010, and Telosa Software, a nonprofit CRM company founded in 1986. The resulting company focuses on nonprofit fundraising, donor management, and online giving platforms. Arreva subsequently acquired Heritage Designs in 2019 and MaestroSoft in 2020, expanding its suite of tools for nonprofit organizations across the United States.
Arreva.summary_abuse: Arreva is a legitimate nonprofit technology vendor serving charities and nonprofits in the US. No credible reports of abuse or misconduct. The company has a long combined heritage through its predecessor firms and is considered a trusted vendor in the nonprofit software space.

ArrowChat.description: ArrowChat is a communication tool that enables users to send text messages and engage in video chats with one another.
ArrowChat.history: ArrowChat is a self-hosted PHP and jQuery-based live chat software that can be embedded on any website. It provides website owners with real-time chat capabilities for their users, including features such as video chat, chat rooms, file sharing, and mobile support. ArrowChat integrates with multiple CMS platforms including WordPress, Joomla, Drupal, and BuddyPress. It is sold as a one-time license and is deployed by the website owner on their own server.
ArrowChat.summary_abuse: ArrowChat is a legitimate self-hosted chat product. Because operators deploy it on their own servers, the vendor itself does not collect user data. No reports of abuse by the product maker. Low risk product used by website communities.

Arsys Domain Parking.description: Arsys is a Spanish domain registrar.
Arsys Domain Parking.history: Arsys was founded in 1996 in Spain and grew to become the leading web hosting provider in the Spanish market. In 2013 the company joined the IONOS Group (formerly 1&1 Internet), which is a subsidiary of United Internet AG, a major European internet services conglomerate. Arsys holds more than 760,000 domains and has over 400 support professionals. The company was an early pioneer in cloud hosting infrastructure in Europe and serves millions of customers across Spain and Europe.
Arsys Domain Parking.summary_abuse: Arsys Domain Parking is a domain parking service operated by Arsys, a mainstream Spanish hosting provider under the IONOS Group. The domain parking feature is standard in the hosting industry for inactive registered domains. No reports of abuse or malicious redirection beyond typical parked domain advertising.

Art Schema.description: Art Schema is a tool for creating websites with custom designs and data types, offering a template-free website building experience.
Art Schema.history: Art Schema is a small website creation platform originally designed to support the art community with simple website building and content management tools. The platform has since expanded to serve other autonomous and self-organized communities beyond visual art. The service is available at artschema.net and provides website templates and a content dashboard. Minimal public information is available about the company's founding team or location.
Art Schema.summary_abuse: Art Schema is a small niche website builder with very limited public footprint. No reports of abuse or malicious activity. The platform serves small creative communities and appears to operate legitimately within a narrow user base.

ArtiBot.description: ArtiBot is a chatbot designed for websites, providing automated assistance and customer engagement through conversational interactions.
ArtiBot.history: ArtiBot.ai is a free AI-powered chatbot platform for websites, targeting service providers such as accountants, real estate agents, and other professionals. The chatbot can capture leads, schedule appointments via Google Calendar integration, and accept payments through Stripe, operating 24 hours a day without human intervention. The platform markets itself as entirely free, making it accessible to small businesses and solo practitioners who need automated lead capture.
ArtiBot.summary_abuse: ArtiBot.ai is a legitimate free chatbot tool for small businesses. No reports of abuse or data misuse. The platform handles lead data and appointment scheduling which involves some privacy considerations but the service is standard for its category.

Artifactory.history: JFrog Artifactory was first released in 2008 and is the flagship product of JFrog, founded by Shlomi Ben Haim, Yoav Landman, and Fred Simon in Israel. JFrog went public on NASDAQ in September 2020 (FROG). Artifactory is the leading universal artifact repository manager used by over 80% of Fortune 100 companies for managing build artifacts, Docker images, and software packages across the DevOps pipeline.
Artifactory.summary_abuse: Artifactory is a backend DevOps artifact repository with no consumer-facing surface and no meaningful abuse signal. It requires enterprise account credentials to access and is used exclusively in software development pipelines. JFrog is a publicly traded company with strong security practices.

Artifactory Web Server.history: Artifactory Web Server refers to the HTTP header or server identification that JFrog Artifactory serves when it acts as a web server for artifact hosting. Artifactory itself was released in 2008 by JFrog. The web server component is bundled with the Artifactory product and is not a separate offering.
Artifactory Web Server.summary_abuse: Artifactory Web Server is the HTTP serving layer of the JFrog Artifactory artifact repository. It has no consumer-facing surface and no meaningful abuse signal. It is used in controlled enterprise development pipeline environments.

Artifi.description: Artifi is an ecommerce product customization platform for configuring complex products, enforcing business rules, and supporting online ordering.
Artifi.history: Artifi Labs is an e-commerce product customization and configuration platform developed by Amla Commerce, a Milwaukee, Wisconsin company founded in 2013. Amla Commerce also produces the Znode multi-channel B2B e-commerce platform. Artifi Labs specifically enables online shoppers to design and personalize products in real time with visual product configurators. The platform integrates with e-commerce stores to let customers customize items such as apparel, signage, and promotional products before purchase.
Artifi.summary_abuse: Artifi Labs is a legitimate e-commerce product customization platform from a US-based software company. No reports of abuse, data misuse, or dark patterns. The platform focuses on enabling merchants to offer configurable products to their customers.

ArtPlacer.description: ArtPlacer is a marketing tool designed to support growth in art businesses by enhancing visibility and promotional efforts.
ArtPlacer.history: ArtPlacer is an art marketing and sales platform founded to help artists, galleries, and art dealers visualize artwork in real environments using augmented reality. The platform allows users to superimpose artwork onto photos of rooms at true scale using an AR mobile app, enabling collectors to preview art on their walls before purchasing. ArtPlacer also offers virtual exhibitions and website integration widgets. The company serves a global market of visual artists and commercial galleries.
ArtPlacer.summary_abuse: ArtPlacer is a legitimate art technology SaaS serving visual artists and galleries. No reports of abuse, dark patterns, or privacy violations. The platform is used by reputable galleries and artists worldwide to facilitate art sales through augmented reality visualization.

Artplayer.js.description: Artplayer.js is an HTML5 video player that supports customizable UI, subtitles, and plugins, offering a flexible and modern video playback solution.
Artplayer.js.history: ArtPlayer.js is an open-source modern HTML5 video player library created by developer zhw2590582, first published around 2019. It supports HLS/DASH streaming, multiple subtitle formats, danmaku (bullet comments), and plugin extensions. The library is actively maintained with regular releases on npm and GitHub.
Artplayer.js.summary_abuse: ArtPlayer.js is a frontend HTML5 video player library with no backend or data collection capabilities. It has no meaningful abuse potential as a client-side rendering component.

Aruba.it.description: Aruba.it is an Italian company mainly active in the web hosting and domain registration businesses.
Aruba.it.history: Aruba S.p.A. was founded in 1994 in Florence as Technorail S.r.l. operating under the brand Technet.it. The company rebranded to Aruba.it in April 2000 and expanded to domains, email, and web hosting. By 2004 it became Aruba S.p.A. and established itself as the market leader in Italy for domain registration and web hosting. Aruba expanded across Europe including the Czech Republic, Slovakia, France, UK, Germany, and Poland. The company has served over 5 million customers and operates multiple data centers across Europe.
Aruba.it.summary_abuse: Aruba.it is a mainstream Italian hosting infrastructure provider with a 30-year track record and millions of customers across Europe. It is GDPR compliant, ISO 27001 certified, and holds a strong reputation as a trustworthy national hosting brand. No significant abuse reports.

ArvanCloud.description: ArvanCloud is a cloud services provider, offering a wide range of incorporated cloud services including CDN, DDoS mitigation, Cloud Managed DNS, Cloud Security, VoD/AoD Streaming, Live Streaming, Cloud Compute, Cloud Object Storage, and PaaS.
ArvanCloud.history: ArvanCloud (Abr Arvan) was founded in Tehran, Iran in 2015 as Iran's first domestic CDN and cloud services provider. The company became a key partner to Iran's Ministry of ICT in building the National Information Network, supporting internet censorship infrastructure. In November 2022, the EU imposed sanctions on ArvanCloud for enabling internet repression during the 2019 Iranian protests. The US Treasury OFAC also designated the company for materially assisting in internet censorship and collaborating with Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security.
ArvanCloud.summary_abuse: ArvanCloud has been designated by both the US Treasury OFAC and the European Union for materially assisting the Iranian government in internet censorship and collaborating with Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security. The company's infrastructure supported suppression of internet access during the November 2019 protests in which hundreds of Iranians were killed. It is subject to international sanctions and should be treated as a high-risk vendor for any non-Iranian enterprise. Its use by western organizations would likely violate sanctions compliance requirements.

Arwes.description: Arwes is a futuristic sci-fi UI web framework.
Arwes.history: Arwes is an open-source futuristic sci-fi UI web framework for the JavaScript ecosystem. The project was created to allow developers to build user interfaces inspired by science fiction aesthetics, with cyberprep design influences from productions like Star Citizen and Halo. Arwes delivers React 18 packages for animations, sound effects, and themed components using TypeScript. The project is available on GitHub under the arwes organization and is published as NPM packages in the @arwes scope. As of 2023 it was still in alpha.
Arwes.summary_abuse: Arwes is a purely decorative open-source UI framework with no commercial or tracking components. No abuse reports. The project is a passion project for developers who want sci-fi styled interfaces.

Arya CMS.description: Arya CMS is a lesser-known content management system that may not have a significant user base or active development community.
Arya CMS.history: ARYA is a digital signage cloud content management system developed by SpinetiX, a Swiss digital signage company founded in 2006 in Lausanne. SpinetiX ARYA is the company's SaaS offering that enables customers to manage digital display networks remotely, schedule content, and monitor players. The platform offers a free ARYA Discovery tier for individual users and paid plans for enterprise deployments with advanced features including priority scheduling, content smart sync, and monitoring dashboards.
Arya CMS.summary_abuse: Arya CMS is a digital signage SaaS developed by SpinetiX, a well-established Swiss company in the digital signage market. No reports of abuse or data misuse. The platform is used by businesses to manage public digital displays.

Aryeo.description: Aryeo is an all-in-one platform designed to manage, deliver, and organize media for real estate photographers.
Aryeo.history: Aryeo was founded in 2019 in Boston by former real estate photographers Branick Weix, Matthew Michalski, and Brendan Quinlan. The company built the leading software platform for real estate photographers and media companies, helping them manage scheduling, payment processing, file delivery, and media for listings. In October 2022, Aryeo raised $3.5M in funding. Zillow Group subsequently acquired Aryeo, integrating it into the Zillow ecosystem to provide media delivery and content management for Zillow Showcase listings.
Aryeo.summary_abuse: Aryeo is a legitimate real estate media software company now owned by Zillow Group. No reports of abuse or data misuse. The acquisition by Zillow provides additional stability and oversight. The platform handles real estate listing media and photography business operations.

Asana.description: Asana is a web and mobile application designed to help teams organize, track, and manage their work.
Asana.history: Asana was founded on December 16, 2008 by Dustin Moskovitz (Facebook co-founder) and Justin Rosenstein in San Francisco. The product launched commercially in April 2012. Asana went public in September 2020 via direct listing at a valuation of approximately $5.5 billion. In July 2025, Dan Rogers (formerly CEO of LaunchDarkly) succeeded Moskovitz as CEO.
Asana.summary_abuse: Asana is a widely-used enterprise project management platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool requiring account registration, making it unsuitable for anonymous abuse. The company holds SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and HIPAA compliance certifications and has a strong security posture.

Asapp.description: Asapp is a platform that guides agents in providing responses to queries using AI technology.
Asapp.history: ASAPP was founded in 2014 in New York City by Gustavo Sapoznik and Marcus Westin to build AI-native tools for enterprise contact centers. The company raised $400 million across three funding rounds, reaching a valuation of $1.6 billion after its Series C in May 2021. Investors include John Doerr, Fidelity, Dragoneer, and Emergence Capital. ASAPP serves large enterprise customers in telecom, financial services, and retail with AI agents and automation tools for customer service operations.
Asapp.summary_abuse: ASAPP is a legitimate enterprise AI SaaS unicorn with major institutional backing. No reports of abuse or misconduct. The company handles customer interaction data on behalf of large enterprises, implying strong data processing agreements and security controls.

aSc EduPage.description: aSc EduPage is a cloud-based school management system.
aSc EduPage.history: aSc was founded around 2002 in Bratislava, Slovakia, originally as asc Applied Software Consultants, producing school timetable scheduling software under the aSc TimeTables brand. The EduPage cloud school management system was formally launched around 2015, extending the product to cover attendance, e-learning, messaging, and grade management. The company has grown to serve over 150,000 schools in 173 countries with no known ownership changes.
aSc EduPage.summary_abuse: aSc EduPage is a school management SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is used exclusively by educational institutions for scheduling, attendance, and communication, and has no consumer-facing hosting or anonymous content-creation features that would attract fraudsters.

AsciiDoc.description: AsciiDoc is a text document format for writing documentation, slideshows, web pages, man pages and blogs. AsciiDoc files can be translated to many formats including HTML, PDF, EPUB, man page.
AsciiDoc.history: AsciiDoc was created in 2002 by Stuart Rackham as a plain text markup language for authoring technical documentation. It predates Markdown and was designed as a shorthand for DocBook XML. In 2019, the Eclipse Foundation began standardizing the AsciiDoc specification. The language gained significant adoption after Asciidoctor, a Ruby-based processor, was released in 2013.
AsciiDoc.summary_abuse: AsciiDoc is a text markup language specification with no network-facing surface and no abuse potential. It is a documentation format used by developers and technical writers.

Asciidoctor.description: Asciidoctor is an open-source text processor and publishing toolchain, written in Ruby, for converting AsciiDoc content to HTML 5, DocBook 5, and other formats.
Asciidoctor.history: Asciidoctor was first released in 2013 by Dan Allen as a fast, open-source Ruby-based text processor for parsing AsciiDoc markup and converting it to HTML, PDF, DocBook, and other formats. It was created to provide a modern, extensible alternative to the original Python-based AsciiDoc tool. Asciidoctor is now supported by the Eclipse Foundation's AsciiDoc standardization effort.
Asciidoctor.summary_abuse: Asciidoctor is a document processing library with no network-facing components and no abuse potential. It processes text files locally.

Asciinema.description: Asciinema is a free and open-source solution for recording terminal sessions and sharing them on the web.
Asciinema.history: Asciinema was started by Marcin Kulik in 2011 as ascii.io, a platform for recording and sharing terminal sessions. The project was renamed to asciinema in September 2013. Version 2.0 introduced the asciicast v2 format in 2018, and version 3.0 of the player (2022) used Rust compiled to WebAssembly for 50x faster performance.
Asciinema.summary_abuse: Asciinema is a terminal recording tool used by developers to share command-line sessions. The asciinema.org hosting platform could theoretically be used to share sensitive credential output accidentally, but this is a user error rather than platform abuse. The tool itself has no meaningful abuse potential.

Asendia.description: Asendia is an international mail joint venture of French La Poste and Swiss Post.
Asendia.history: Asendia was formed in 2012 as a joint venture between La Poste (France) and Swiss Post to provide international mail and parcel delivery services. The company specializes in cross-border e-commerce logistics, serving businesses that ship small parcels internationally at scale. Asendia leverages the postal networks of its parent companies and local delivery partners in over 200 destinations. The company offers e-PAQ branded services ranging from economical postal to premium express delivery and processes millions of cross-border shipments annually.
Asendia.summary_abuse: Asendia is a legitimate international parcel carrier jointly owned by two major European national postal services. No reports of abuse or fraudulent activity. The company is a trusted partner for cross-border e-commerce logistics used by major brands globally.

Aseqbase.description: Aseqbase is a sequence-based web development framework designed to create robust websites.
Aseqbase.history: Aseqbase (also written MiMFa Aseqbase) is a PHP-based sequence-driven web development framework available on GitHub and Packagist. It is designed for building multi-subdomain websites, single-page applications, and custom CMS setups using a layered sequential architecture. The project is maintained by the MiMFa open-source community and provides developer tools, a media hub, and image tools as part of its ecosystem.
Aseqbase.summary_abuse: Aseqbase is a small open-source PHP framework with no reported abuse. It is a niche developer tool with minimal adoption. The code is publicly available on GitHub for inspection, and no security concerns have been identified.

Asgaros Forum.description: Asgaros Forum is a lightweight and simple forum plugin for WordPress.
Asgaros Forum.history: Asgaros Forum is a free and open-source WordPress forum plugin developed by Thomas Belser. It provides a lightweight, feature-rich discussion board that integrates natively with WordPress user accounts. The plugin has been available on the official WordPress.org plugin directory and has been translated into 35 languages. It is actively maintained on GitHub and offers a clean interface for community discussions without requiring a third-party forum platform.
Asgaros Forum.summary_abuse: Asgaros Forum is a trusted open-source WordPress plugin maintained by a single developer. No reports of abuse, malicious code, or privacy violations. It is listed on the official WordPress.org repository which performs security reviews on submissions.

Ashop.description: Ashop is a hosted shopping cart software solution for creating and managing an online store.
Ashop.history: Ashop Commerce was founded in 2001 by a group of IT and sales professionals in Sydney, Australia, with incorporation in 2004. The company offers cloud-based shopping cart software for small and medium businesses to build and run online stores. Ashop has offices in Australia, the US, and the UK and has served businesses internationally since its founding. The platform competes with mainstream platforms like Shopify and 3dcart but maintains a small, dedicated customer base.
Ashop.summary_abuse: Ashop is a legitimate Australian e-commerce SaaS with over 20 years of history. No reports of abuse or data misuse. The company is a small but established player in the e-commerce platform space.

AsisteClick.description: AsisteClick is a customer service solution that uses chatbots and an omnichannel platform to streamline support across multiple communication channels.
AsisteClick.history: AsisteClick is a live chat and AI chatbot platform founded in Buenos Aires, Argentina, targeting small and medium businesses in Latin America. The platform combines human agents with AI-powered chatbots and integrates with channels including WhatsApp, web, Instagram, email, Telegram, and Facebook. It is one of the early Spanish-speaking live chat platforms in the LATAM market, offering affordable pricing starting at USD 16 per month. The company employs around 10 people and is active in multiple Spanish-speaking countries.
AsisteClick.summary_abuse: AsisteClick is a legitimate Argentinian live chat and chatbot SaaS for Latin American businesses. No reports of abuse or data misuse. The platform is a small regional player with transparent pricing and standard business messaging practices.

Askas.description: Askas is an ecommerce design and shop system from Sweden, providing tools for online store creation and management.
Askas.history: Askås was founded on February 28, 1997 by Jonas Askås in Säffle, Sweden, originally as a general web consultancy called Askås Consulting. The company pivoted to e-commerce in the early 2000s and launched its first e-commerce platform in 1999 for a cycling retailer. Over the following decades it grew steadily, achieved Gazell growth-company status in 2010 with revenue exceeding 10 million SEK, opened a Karlstad office in 2014, and now employs over 50 people serving Swedish online retailers.
Askas.summary_abuse: Askås is a Swedish e-commerce platform serving legitimate online retailers, primarily in the Nordic market. Like any e-commerce platform, it could theoretically host fake storefronts, but its client acquisition model requires direct business engagement rather than self-service signup, limiting anonymous abuse. No documented abuse incidents are associated with the platform.

AskHandle.description: AskHandle is a platform that enables the creation of custom AI agents in minutes using user data and requirements, without requiring coding.
AskHandle.history: AskHandle is an AI-powered customer support chatbot platform based in New York that enables businesses to create custom AI agents trained on their own data. The platform allows users to build chatbots from uploaded documents, PDFs, and website content without coding. AskHandle received seed investment from BDev Ventures. It targets businesses looking to automate frequently asked questions and provide 24/7 support without human agents.
AskHandle.summary_abuse: AskHandle is a small AI chatbot startup with no reported abuse or data misuse. The company provides automated customer support tools and operates in a competitive but legitimate market. Low abuse risk for an early-stage US-based SaaS.

AskNicely.description: AskNicely is a platform that measures and improves customer happiness using the Net Promoter Score (NPS) framework.
AskNicely.history: AskNicely was founded in 2014 in Auckland, New Zealand, backed by Blackbird Ventures. The company provides an AI-powered NPS and customer experience platform for multi-location service businesses. AskNicely raised $32 million in funding and serves over 1,300 companies globally with offices in Portland, Oregon, Auckland, and the Netherlands.
AskNicely.summary_abuse: AskNicely is a B2B customer feedback and NPS platform with no notable abuse signal. Its survey delivery functionality is controlled by business customers through verified integrations and does not provide anonymous mass-messaging capabilities.

AskSpot.description: AskSpot is an AI chatbot platform tailored for marketing, digital, and web development agencies.
AskSpot.history: AskSpot is an AI chatbot platform for marketing and web development agencies. It appears to have been launched post-2022. Limited public history available.
AskSpot.summary_abuse: AskSpot has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche AI chatbot platform for agencies with no documented abuse patterns.

Asksuite.description: Asksuite is a platform that enhances direct bookings and revenue for hotels and resorts by improving service experiences and boosting reservation sales productivity.
Asksuite.history: Asksuite was founded in 2017 in Florianopolis, Santa Catarina, Brazil. It is a global leader in AI reservation assistants and omnichannel communication platforms for the hospitality industry, particularly hotels, resorts, and hotel chains. The platform manages guest conversations from web, WhatsApp, and social media in a single interface. As of 2024, more than 6,000 hotels in 80+ countries use Asksuite, and the platform has served over 120 million travelers. The company has around 130 employees.
Asksuite.summary_abuse: Asksuite is a legitimate Brazilian hospitality technology company with a strong track record serving thousands of hotels globally. No reports of abuse or data misuse. The company focuses on customer service automation in the well-regulated hotel booking space.

ASP.NET Boilerplate.description: ASP.NET Boilerplate is a general purpose application framework especially designed for new modern web applications. It uses already familiar tools and implements best practices around them to provide you a SOLID development experience.
ASP.NET Boilerplate.history: ASP.NET Boilerplate was developed starting in 2013 by Halil Ibrahim Kalkan and maintained by Volosoft, a Turkish software company. The framework provides a structured, convention-based architecture for building enterprise ASP.NET web apps. Volosoft later created ABP Framework as its successor. Official support for ASP.NET Boilerplate ends May 2026.
ASP.NET Boilerplate.summary_abuse: ASP.NET Boilerplate is an open-source enterprise application framework with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history. It is now deprecated in favor of ABP Framework.

Aspin.description: Aspin is a B2B sales platform for distributors and wholesalers, offering ecommerce platforms, sales applications, and product information management tools.
Aspin.history: Aspin (Aspin Management Systems Ltd) was founded in 1984 in Romsey, UK. The company develops B2B sales software for wholesale distributors and manufacturers, primarily in the giftware, greeting cards, and leisure industries. Key products include PixSell, an iPad sales rep ordering app, InterSell, a B2B e-commerce portal, and SkooCloud, a product information management system. The company has served customers in the UK, US, Asia, Europe, and Australia for over 40 years.
Aspin.summary_abuse: Aspin is a trusted UK B2B software provider with a 40-year operating history. No reports of abuse or data misuse. The company operates in a regulated business software market and has long-standing relationships with distributors and manufacturers.

Aspio.description: Aspio is a platform that streamlines bookings and manages sales.
Aspio.history: Aspio is a Swedish appointment booking and scheduling platform founded in 2021 in Stockholm. The company targets service businesses including hair salons, barbershops, beauty salons, and clinics. Aspio was built with a Scandinavian design ethos to make booking easy for both service providers and their customers through an online platform that handles time management, payments, and customer discovery. It competes with platforms like Booksy, Zenoti, and GlossGenius.
Aspio.summary_abuse: Aspio is a legitimate Swedish appointment booking startup. No reports of abuse or data misuse. The company is GDPR compliant by virtue of its Swedish origin and focuses on legitimate scheduling and payment services for small service businesses.

Assemble.description: Assemble is a website builder designed for creating and managing film-related websites.
Assemble.history: Assemble is an open-source static site generator for Grunt.js and Node.js, created around 2012 by Jon Schlinkert. The project was used by notable organizations including H5BP, Zurb Foundation, Zurb Ink, Topcoat, and the Web Experience Toolkit. Assemble introduced a modular approach to HTML templating using Handlebars and allowed content to be defined as HTML, JSON, or YAML. The tool was widely used during the peak Grunt.js era before being largely superseded by newer build tools and frameworks.
Assemble.summary_abuse: Assemble is a legitimate open-source static site generator with no history of abuse. The project was used by reputable organizations and is fully open-source on GitHub. Its low activity in recent years reflects changing JavaScript tooling preferences rather than any negative history.

Assertive Yield.description: Assertive Yield is a SaaS company that specialises in helping SSPs (Supply-Side Platforms), publishers, and ad networks optimise their advertising revenue through real-time attribution and yield optimisation strategies.
Assertive Yield.history: Assertive Yield (Assertive Yield B.V.) was founded in 2019 in The Hague, Netherlands. The company provides real-time programmatic advertising analytics, yield management, and AI-driven optimization for digital publishers. Its platform processes over 200 billion ad impressions monthly and provides insights into header bidding, dynamic flooring, demand path optimization, and user profiling. The company publishes quarterly industry reports on programmatic advertising trends for publishers.
Assertive Yield.summary_abuse: Assertive Yield is a Netherlands-based ad tech company providing publisher-side yield analytics. The platform inherently processes significant tracking data including user profiling and behavioral signals across the programmatic ecosystem, resulting in a high tracker density score. No reports of fraudulent or deceptive practices, but the business model is inherently tracking-intensive.

Astra.description: Astra is a fast, lightweight, and highly customizable WordPress Theme.
Astra.history: Astra is a WordPress theme developed by Brainstorm Force, a company founded in 2009 by Sujay Pawar and Nikhil Chavan in Pune, India. The Astra theme was launched in May 2017 and rapidly became the most popular third-party WordPress theme on WordPress.org, surpassing one million active installations faster than any other theme. As of 2024 it powers over 1.8 million websites. Brainstorm Force also developed the Starter Templates plugin and the Spectra page builder.
Astra.summary_abuse: Astra is a widely trusted WordPress theme from Brainstorm Force with an excellent reputation in the WordPress ecosystem. No known abuse, dark patterns, or security issues have been attributed to it. The project is actively maintained and has a transparent open-source codebase on WordPress.org.

Astra Widgets.description: Astra Widgets is a handy little free plugin that lets you display address, list icons or social profiles within the Astra Theme.
Astra Widgets.history: Astra Widgets is a free WordPress plugin developed by Brainstorm Force, the Pune, India-based company behind the Astra WordPress theme. The plugin adds three simple widgets to WordPress sites: an address widget, a social profiles widget, and a list icons widget, intended primarily for use in the header, sidebar, and footer of Astra-powered websites. It is published on the official WordPress.org plugin directory and hosted on GitHub under the Brainstorm Force organization.
Astra Widgets.summary_abuse: Astra Widgets is a minimal, trusted WordPress plugin from Brainstorm Force, one of the most reputable WordPress product companies. No abuse reports. The plugin is simple and open-source with a very limited feature scope.

AstraFit.description: AstraFit is an online virtual fitting software for large and small retailers.
AstraFit.history: AstraFit was founded in 2012 by CEO Nikita Dobrynin, motivated by poor online clothing sizing experiences. The company developed an AI-powered virtual fitting room and size recommendation platform for fashion e-commerce. AstraFit has offices in Cyprus, Kiev, and the US. The platform uses body measurements to predict clothing size and lets shoppers virtually try on garments before purchasing. By 2019 it had helped over 8 million shoppers and served hundreds of fashion brands and retailers.
AstraFit.summary_abuse: AstraFit is a legitimate fashion technology company with a decade-long track record. No reports of abuse, dark patterns, or data misuse. The platform handles body measurement data and user sizing information, requiring careful privacy practices.

Astro.description: Astro is a new JavaScript-based static site builder.
Astro.history: Astro was publicly announced in June 2021 by the team behind Snowpack and Skypack (including Fred Schott). It introduced a new Islands Architecture for content-focused web development, shipping zero JavaScript by default. The Astro framework grew rapidly and formed The Astro Technology Company, raising venture capital. Astro reached version 1.0 in August 2022 and version 4.0 in late 2023.
Astro.summary_abuse: Astro is an open-source static site framework with no backend infrastructure components and no abuse potential. Sites built with Astro are no different in abuse risk than any other static website.

Astute Solutions.description: Astute Solutions is a customer engagement software.
Astute Solutions.history: Astute Solutions was founded in 1995 in Columbus, Ohio by Joseph Sanda. The company provides customer experience management software, including its flagship product ePowerCenter (later rebranded as Astute Agent) for consumer complaint handling and case management, as well as social relationship management and knowledge management tools. Astute Solutions primarily serves consumer goods companies, food and beverage manufacturers, and retailers that manage high volumes of customer contacts. The company has approximately 124 employees.
Astute Solutions.summary_abuse: Astute Solutions is a long-established US CRM software company with a 30-year history. No reports of abuse, data misuse, or unethical business practices. It serves reputable consumer goods brands with compliant customer service software.

AT Internet Analyzer.history: AT Internet was founded in France in 1996 by Alain Llorens as a web agency and shifted focus to web analytics in 1997. The Analytics Suite was used on approximately 20,000 websites. In March 2021, Piano acquired AT Internet and rebranded the analytics product as Piano Analytics. AT Internet is notably the only major analytics provider with a CNIL exemption allowing analytics collection without user consent under French law.
AT Internet Analyzer.summary_abuse: AT Internet Analyzer (now Piano Analytics) is a privacy-focused enterprise web analytics platform. It holds a CNIL exemption for cookieless analytics in France and is GDPR-compliant. No documented abuse history; the platform is used by established media companies and enterprises.

AT Internet XiTi.history: AT Internet XiTi is the legacy tag/tracker name used by AT Internet analytics, a French web analytics company founded in 1996. The XiTi brand refers to the older implementation of AT Internet's tracker. AT Internet was acquired by Piano in March 2021, and the platform was rebranded as Piano Analytics. The XiTi tag name persists on older implementations.
AT Internet XiTi.summary_abuse: AT Internet XiTi is a legacy French web analytics tracker that has been superseded by Piano Analytics. No documented abuse history. The platform is privacy-focused with GDPR compliance and a French CNIL exemption.

Atatus.description: Atatus is a full-stack observability tool that let you identify the performance bottlenecks and helps you optimise your application at the right time.
Atatus.history: Atatus was founded in 2014 by NamLabs Technologies in India as an application performance monitoring (APM) and observability platform. It provides real-time monitoring of application performance, error tracking, and infrastructure metrics. The company serves primarily SMB and mid-market customers as a more affordable alternative to Datadog and New Relic.
Atatus.summary_abuse: Atatus is a B2B APM and observability platform with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal. It collects application telemetry data from its customers' applications, which requires appropriate data governance.

Atera.description: Atera is a software solution for RMM network discovery, PSA, and remote access.
Atera.history: Atera was founded in 2016 in Tel Aviv, Israel, and opened a US office in 2024. It offers an all-in-one IT management platform combining Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM), Professional Services Automation (PSA), and remote access for MSPs and IT departments. Atera differentiated itself with a per-technician pricing model rather than per-device, making it cost-effective for MSPs.
Atera.summary_abuse: Atera is an IT management platform used by MSPs and IT departments. It has broad system access to managed endpoints which makes security posture critical. No major public abuse incidents are documented, but RMM tools as a category have been used by attackers in supply chain attacks against MSPs. Atera holds SOC 2 and ISO 27001 certifications.

aThemes Airi.description: aThemes Airi is a powerful yet lightweight and flexible WordPress theme for organization or freelancer.
aThemes Airi.history: aThemes was founded in 2013 and built a portfolio of WordPress themes including Airi, Sydney, Astrid, and others. The company was acquired by Awesome Motive (the WPBeginner parent) via the WPBeginner Growth Fund, announced in 2025.
aThemes Airi.summary_abuse: aThemes Airi is a WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It installs on the site owner's server and does not provide content-hosting or redirection features that fraudsters would exploit.

aThemes Astrid.description: aThemes Astrid is a powerful yet lightweight and flexible WordPress theme.
aThemes Astrid.history: aThemes Astrid is a WordPress theme produced by aThemes, founded in 2013. aThemes was acquired by Awesome Motive via the WPBeginner Growth Fund in 2025.
aThemes Astrid.summary_abuse: aThemes Astrid is a WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It runs on the site owner's server and offers no content-hosting features exploitable for fraud.

aThemes Hiero.description: aThemes Hiero is an awesome magazine theme for your WordPress site feature bold colors and details to the content.
aThemes Hiero.history: aThemes Hiero is a WordPress theme from aThemes, founded 2013. aThemes was acquired by Awesome Motive via the WPBeginner Growth Fund in 2025.
aThemes Hiero.summary_abuse: aThemes Hiero is a WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It runs on the site owner's server and offers no consumer-facing hosting features exploitable for fraud.

aThemes Moesia.description: aThemes Moesia is the business theme you need in order to build your presence on the Internet.
aThemes Moesia.history: aThemes Moesia is a WordPress theme from aThemes, founded 2013. aThemes was acquired by Awesome Motive via the WPBeginner Growth Fund in 2025.
aThemes Moesia.summary_abuse: aThemes Moesia is a WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It installs on the site owner's server and does not provide consumer-facing hosting features that fraudsters would exploit.

aThemes Sydney.description: aThemes Sydney is a powerful business WordPress theme that provides a fast way for companies or freelancers to create an online presence.
aThemes Sydney.history: aThemes Sydney is the flagship WordPress theme from aThemes, founded in 2013 and known as one of the most popular free WordPress business themes. aThemes was acquired by Awesome Motive via the WPBeginner Growth Fund in 2025.
aThemes Sydney.summary_abuse: aThemes Sydney is a WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It installs on the site owner's server and does not provide consumer-facing hosting features that fraudsters would exploit.

Athena Search.description: Athena Search is a customizable autocomplete, feature-rich dashboard, smart predictions, real-time reports search engine developed from scratch by Syncit Group’s.
Athena Search.history: Athena Search is an AI-powered e-commerce search and product discovery platform developed by Syncit Group, a Magento Business Solution Partner. The platform provides keyword search, textual semantic search, and visual semantic search capabilities with merchandising, autocomplete, and analytics. Syncit Group later expanded the product into a broader suite called Athena XAI which added visual search, category navigation, product recommendations, and analytics. The solution is available on the Adobe Commerce Marketplace.
Athena Search.summary_abuse: Athena Search is a legitimate e-commerce search SaaS developed by a Magento-certified partner. No reports of abuse or data misuse. The platform is a standard e-commerce enhancement tool with no identified dark patterns or malicious behavior.

Athos Commerce.description: Athos Commerce is a site search and merchandising platform designed to help ecommerce.
Athos Commerce.history: Athos Commerce was formed in January 2025 through the merger of three e-commerce product discovery companies: Searchspring (founded 2007 in San Antonio, TX), Klevu (a Finnish AI search startup), and Intelligent Reach. The combined entity is headquartered in Espoo, Finland and backed by PSG Equity. Athos Commerce provides AI-powered search, personalization, merchandising, and omnichannel product discovery for e-commerce retailers, with offices in multiple countries across North America, Europe, and Australia.
Athos Commerce.summary_abuse: Athos Commerce is a well-funded e-commerce technology company formed from reputable predecessors. No reports of abuse or data misuse. The company serves major retail brands and operates in a competitive but legitimate market for product discovery software.

Atlasmic.description: Atlasmic is a live chat and business messenger platform designed to support modern online businesses.
Atlasmic.history: Atlasmic was founded in 2020 by Ben Bitvinskas in Vilnius, Lithuania. The company offers a live chat, sales acceleration, and marketing platform for small and medium-sized businesses. Atlasmic integrates with Shopify and provides tools for lead capture, conversation management, and business analytics. The company has 1 to 10 employees and is bootstrapped without external funding.
Atlasmic.summary_abuse: Atlasmic is a legitimate Lithuanian live chat startup with no reported abuse or data misuse. The company is GDPR compliant by virtue of its EU-based operations. It is a small bootstrapped product with standard live chat functionality.

Atlassian Bitbucket.description: Bitbucket is a web-based version control repository hosting service for source code and development projects that use either Mercurial or Git revision control systems.
Atlassian Bitbucket.history: Bitbucket was founded in 2008 by Jesper Noring and Dag Agren in Australia as an independent Git and Mercurial code hosting service. Atlassian acquired Bitbucket in September 2010 for an undisclosed sum and integrated it into its developer toolchain. Bitbucket dropped support for Mercurial repositories in 2020 to focus on Git. Atlassian went public on NASDAQ in 2015 (TEAM).
html: <li>Atlassian Bitbucket <span title="[a-z0-9]+" id="product-version" data-commitid="[a-z0-9]+" data-system-build-number="[a-z0-9]+"> v([\d.]+)<\;version:\1
Atlassian Bitbucket.summary_abuse: Atlassian Bitbucket is a Git code hosting platform used by software development teams. Like other code hosting services, it can occasionally be abused to host malicious code repositories or be used in supply chain attacks. Atlassian has robust abuse reporting and takedown processes and actively monitors for malicious content. Overall abuse level is low relative to the predominantly legitimate developer user base.

Atlassian Confluence.description: Atlassian Confluence is a web-based collaboration wiki tool.
Atlassian Confluence.history: Atlassian Confluence was launched in 2004 as a team wiki and knowledge management tool built by Atlassian, founded in 2002 in Sydney, Australia by Mike Cannon-Brookes and Scott Farquhar. Atlassian went public on NASDAQ in December 2015 (TEAM). Confluence has grown to become the most widely deployed enterprise wiki platform globally.
Atlassian Confluence.summary_abuse: Atlassian Confluence is an enterprise wiki and knowledge management platform with no significant public abuse signal. Its access-controlled deployment model makes it unsuitable for anonymous abuse. Some historical CVEs (e.g., OGNL injection vulnerabilities) were significant but patched promptly. Atlassian runs an active bug bounty program.

Atlassian FishEye.history: Atlassian FishEye (originally called Cenqua FishEye) was developed by Cenqua and acquired by Atlassian in 2007. It is a source code search and repository browsing tool that supports SVN, Git, Perforce, and CVS. Atlassian officially announced the end-of-life for FishEye and its companion Crucible code review tool. Server and Data Center versions reached end-of-life in late 2024.
Atlassian FishEye.summary_abuse: Atlassian FishEye is an enterprise source code repository browser with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal. It is a backend developer tool requiring enterprise credentials.

Atlassian Jira.history: Atlassian Jira was first released in 2002 by Atlassian, co-founded in Sydney, Australia by Mike Cannon-Brookes and Scott Farquhar. Originally a bug and issue tracker, Jira expanded into project management and agile planning. Atlassian went public on NASDAQ in December 2015. Jira became the global standard issue tracker for software development teams and is used by tens of thousands of organizations worldwide.
Atlassian Jira.summary_abuse: Atlassian Jira is an enterprise project and issue tracking platform with no meaningful abuse signal. It is used exclusively by authenticated teams in controlled organizational environments. Atlassian is a publicly traded company with strong security practices and an active bug bounty program.

Atlassian Jira Issue Collector.description: Atlassian Jira Issue Collector is a tool used to download a list of websites using with email addresses, phone numbers and LinkedIn profiles.
Atlassian Jira Issue Collector.history: The Jira Issue Collector is an embeddable feedback widget developed by Atlassian as part of the Jira platform, allowing websites to collect user feedback and automatically create Jira issues. It was introduced as a Jira feature to bridge customer-facing surfaces with internal issue tracking workflows. It remains part of the active Jira product suite.
Atlassian Jira Issue Collector.summary_abuse: The Atlassian Jira Issue Collector is a feedback widget that submits user reports to Jira projects. It is an enterprise developer tool with no meaningful abuse potential. Submissions require a valid Jira project connection, limiting unauthorized use.

Atlassian Statuspage.description: Statuspage is a status and incident communication tool.
Atlassian Statuspage.history: Statuspage was founded in 2013 by Steve Klein, Danny Olinsky, and Scott Klein in New Orleans, Louisiana, as an incident communication and status page platform. Atlassian acquired Statuspage in January 2016. It became a key part of Atlassian's IT service management portfolio and is widely used by SaaS companies to communicate outages to customers.
Atlassian Statuspage.summary_abuse: Atlassian Statuspage is an incident communication platform used by organizations to communicate service outages. It has no meaningful abuse signal. Its public-facing status pages are used by legitimate vendors and do not present an abuse surface.

Atome.description: Atome is a brand that allows users to purchase products online and pay for them in monthly installments.
Atome.history: Atome was launched in December 2019 by Advance Intelligence Group, a Singapore-based AI technology company founded in 2016. Atome provides buy now pay later (BNPL) services across nine Asian markets including Singapore, Indonesia, Malaysia, Hong Kong, and others. It has partnered with over 5,000 retailers and served over 10 million customers.
Atome.summary_abuse: Atome is a BNPL payment platform, which is a sector with documented fraud risks including synthetic identity fraud, account takeovers, and fraudulent returns. BNPL platforms generally have lighter KYC requirements than traditional credit, making them attractive to fraudsters. However, Atome operates in heavily regulated Asian markets and has implemented anti-fraud controls. Specific incident reports for Atome are not widely documented.

Atomseo.description: Atomseo is a tool designed to improve website performance by reducing user bounce rates and enhancing search engine visibility.
Atomseo.history: Atomseo is a free online SEO tool suite focused on broken link detection and website technical auditing. Its primary product is a broken and redirect links checker available as a web application and Chrome browser extension. The tool crawls websites to identify internal and external links with HTTP errors, missing images, duplicate meta tags, and redirects. Atomseo offers up to 1,500 free link checks per day and supports scheduled checks with email alerts.
Atomseo.summary_abuse: Atomseo is a small, legitimate SEO utility tool with no reported abuse. The product is primarily used by website administrators and SEO professionals for technical site auditing. Low risk and straightforward use case.

ATSHOP.description: ATSHOP is an all-in-one ecommerce platform.
ATSHOP.history: ATSHOP was founded in 2018 in Bucharest, Romania as a cloud-based ecommerce platform for creating online stores. The company remains bootstrapped. It targets individual sellers and small businesses wanting to quickly launch digital product storefronts.
ATSHOP.summary_abuse: ATSHOP is a small ecommerce storefront platform primarily known in the digital goods and game key reselling communities. The platform has been associated with grey-market digital product sales in some reports, and its low-barrier signup process has made it attractive for low-quality or scam storefronts. The vendor has limited publicly documented anti-abuse procedures.

Attentive.description: Attentive is a personalised mobile messaging platform that helps retail & ecommerce brands acquire, retain, and interact with mobile shoppers.
Attentive.history: Attentive was founded in 2016 by Brian Long and Andrew Jones in New York City. Both founders previously co-founded TapCommerce (acquired by Twitter in 2014). Attentive raised $470 million in a 2021 Series E round at a $7 billion valuation, with total funding exceeding $800 million from Sequoia Capital, Coatue, and Bain Capital Ventures.
Attentive.summary_abuse: Attentive is an SMS and email marketing platform. SMS marketing platforms face risks of being used for spam campaigns and potentially phishing if the subscriber list management is poor. Attentive is a business-to-business platform requiring merchant sign-up and TCPA-compliant opt-in management. The company enforces sender verification but as with all mass-messaging tools, poor enforcement of opt-in practices can lead to unwanted messages.

Attracta.description: Attracta is a platform offering SEO tools designed to optimize website visibility and improve search engine rankings.
Attracta.history: Attracta is a US-based SEO tools platform founded around 2009. It provides link building and site optimization tools often bundled with web hosting services. No notable ownership changes documented.
Attracta.summary_abuse: Attracta has been historically associated with unsolicited link-building services that were sometimes categorized as spam SEO. Some webmasters reported it added links without clear consent when bundled with shared hosting packages. Abuse risk is moderate due to these practices.

AttractROI.description: AttractROI is a service that provides tools and resources to help businesses convert potential customers into loyal clients.
AttractROI.history: AttractROI is a comprehensive digital marketing and CRM platform built specifically for real estate professionals. The founders created the platform to solve their own real estate marketing challenges before releasing it commercially. The product provides landing page builders, lead capture forms, appointment scheduling, multi-channel follow-up campaigns via SMS, email, and Facebook Messenger, pipeline management, and Stripe payment integration. It is positioned as an all-in-one marketing funnel tool for real estate marketers.
AttractROI.summary_abuse: AttractROI is a small all-in-one marketing SaaS targeting the real estate industry. No reported abuse. The platform handles lead data and conducts automated follow-up messaging, which carries some spam risk if misused by customers, but no evidence of the vendor itself engaging in abusive practices.

Attraqt.description: Attraqt provides AI-driven search, merchandising and personalisation solutions.
Attraqt.history: Attraqt is a UK-based AI-driven search, merchandising, and personalisation platform for e-commerce, founded around 2010. It serves enterprise retailers. No notable ownership changes documented.
Attraqt.summary_abuse: Attraqt has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B e-commerce search and personalization platform sold to enterprise retailers with no consumer-facing content hosting.

Attribution.description: Attribution is a marketing analytics platform that provides marketers with insights to understand the cost, revenue and profit resulting from marketing programs.
Attribution.history: Attribution (attributionapp.com) is a marketing analytics platform providing multi-touch attribution insights. Founded approximately 2014 in the US. No notable ownership changes documented.
Attribution.summary_abuse: Attribution has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B marketing analytics platform with no consumer-facing content hosting features.

Auberge.description: Auberge is a responsive WordPress theme designed for restaurants, cafes, and recipe blogs.
Auberge.history: Auberge is a responsive WordPress theme created by WebMan Design, targeted at restaurants, cafes, and recipe blogs. It is distributed via the WordPress.org theme repository as a free theme with a premium upgrade option. No notable ownership changes or controversies are documented.
Auberge.summary_abuse: Auberge is a WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. As a self-hosted theme, its abuse surface is limited to the WordPress installations that use it rather than the theme itself. No documented cases of the theme being used for phishing or other malicious purposes.

AudioEye.description: AudioEye is an accessibility overlay which claims to provide ADA and WCAG accessibility compliance.
AudioEye.history: AudioEye was founded in 2005 as an R&D firm focused on converting web content to audio formats for accessibility. After years of R&D, the company pivoted to a scalable SaaS platform for digital accessibility compliance and became a public company on Nasdaq (AEYE) via a reverse merger.
