It is the only publicly traded digital accessibility company, combining AI automation with guidance from certified accessibility experts.
AudioEye.summary_abuse: AudioEye is a web accessibility compliance platform with no notable abuse signal. The service is used by businesses to meet ADA and WCAG accessibility requirements and does not expose features that are commonly misused for phishing or fraud.

Audiohook.description: Audiohook specializes in programmatic audio advertising.
Audiohook.history: Audiohook is a programmatic audio advertising platform founded around 2018 in the US. No notable ownership changes documented.
Audiohook.summary_abuse: Audiohook has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B audio advertising platform with no consumer-facing content hosting that would attract fraudsters.

Auglio.description: Auglio is a virtual try-on tool enabling customers to try products using camera and augmented reality before the purchase.
Auglio.history: Auglio was founded in 2022 in Komarno, Slovakia, as an augmented reality virtual try-on SaaS platform for e-commerce. It generates 3D product models from 2D product images to enable customers to virtually try on eyewear, cosmetics, and accessories. It is available as a Shopify app and as a plug-and-play integration for other e-commerce platforms.
Auglio.summary_abuse: Auglio is a niche AR virtual try-on platform for e-commerce retailers with no documented abuse signal. The service processes camera data from users' devices for real-time try-on but does not store or sell user data as a primary function. No reports of misuse for phishing or fraud have been documented.

augmented-ui.description: augmented-ui is a UI framework inspired by cyberpunk and sci-fi.
augmented-ui.history: augmented-ui was created by James0x57 (Jane Ori) and released around 2020 as an open-source CSS library for creating cyberpunk and sci-fi inspired web interfaces. No notable ownership changes or forks.
augmented-ui.summary_abuse: augmented-ui is a CSS UI styling library with no notable abuse signal. It is a pure frontend library with no server-side or content-hosting surface.

Aument.description: Aument is an ecommerce toolbox with easy to use marketing actions and workflows.
Aument.history: Aument is an Argentine e-commerce marketing automation toolbox for cart abandonment and email workflows, founded around 2020. No notable ownership changes documented.
Aument.summary_abuse: Aument has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B e-commerce email marketing platform with no documented abuse patterns.

Aura.description: Aura is an open-source UI framework built by Salesforce for developing dynamic web apps for mobile and desktop devices.
Aura.history: Aura was founded in 2017 and is based in Boston, Massachusetts. The company built an all-in-one digital safety platform combining identity theft protection, antivirus, VPN, password management, and financial fraud monitoring for individuals and families. In September 2024, Aura split into two standalone companies: Aura (consumer-facing safety) and Pango Group (multi-brand cybersecurity for enterprise and consumers). Aura has approximately $150 million ARR and was growing over 40% year-over-year.
Aura.summary_abuse: Aura is a consumer digital safety and identity theft protection service. It is designed to protect users from fraud and identity theft rather than facilitate it, and has no notable abuse signal as an attack vector. The platform collects substantial personal and financial data as part of its monitoring services, which carries privacy considerations but is used for the stated protective purpose.

Aurelia.description: Aurelia is an open-source UI JavaScript framework designed to create single page applications.
Aurelia.history: Aurelia is a JavaScript frontend framework created by Rob Eisenberg in 2015, after he left the Angular 2 core team at Google. It is the successor to Eisenberg's earlier Durandal framework and was built with a standards-first, convention-over-configuration philosophy using modern ECMAScript. Aurelia 1.0 was formally released in July 2016. Aurelia 2 was later developed as a major rewrite. Despite strong early praise, Aurelia never achieved widespread adoption compared to React, Angular, or Vue, and it has seen community decline over time.
Aurelia.summary_abuse: Aurelia is an open-source JavaScript frontend framework with no abuse signal. It is foundational frontend technology used in web applications and has no consumer-facing surface that could be exploited for phishing or fraud.

Auryc.description: Auryc is a client-side journey intelligence platform that surfaces real-time insights.
Auryc.history: Auryc was founded in 2016 by Jinlin Wang, Amod Setlur, and Feng Shao, headquartered in San Jose, California. The company built a digital experience analytics platform with session replay, heatmaps, and conversion funnel analysis for e-commerce and SaaS companies. It raised $14.6M across four funding rounds. In June 2022, Auryc was acquired by Heap Analytics, a leading digital insights platform, and its technology was subsequently integrated into Heap's product.
Auryc.summary_abuse: Auryc is a customer experience analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform records user sessions and collects behavioral data on behalf of website operators, raising standard session-replay privacy considerations, but it is not associated with spam, phishing, or fraudulent use cases.

Ausha.description: Ausha is an all-in-one platform for hosting, distributing, and monetizing podcasts.
Ausha.history: Ausha was launched in 2018 in France by iCreo Group, the company behind RadioKing, a leading French web radio creation platform. Ausha quickly became the dominant podcast hosting and marketing platform in France, serving over 7,000 podcasts including major French media outlets like Le Figaro and L'Equipe, and brands such as LVMH and BNP Paribas. The company expanded to the US market in October 2022.
Ausha.summary_abuse: Ausha is a podcast hosting platform with no significant abuse signal. As a content hosting service, it could theoretically host misleading or harmful podcasts, but it is a legitimate platform serving established French and international media companies with terms of service. No specific abuse incidents have been publicly documented.

Australia Post.description: Australia Post is the government business enterprise that provides postal services in Australia.
Australia Post.history: Australia Post is Australia's national postal service, with roots dating to 1809 under British colonial administration and formally established as a federal entity in 1901 with Australian federation. It operates as a government business enterprise owned by the Australian federal government. In the digital era, Australia Post expanded into e-commerce shipping infrastructure by offering APIs for parcel tracking, label printing, and rate calculation, integrating with Shopify and WooCommerce among others.
Australia Post.summary_abuse: Australia Post's shipping APIs and platform are primarily used by legitimate Australian businesses for parcel delivery. The brand is frequently impersonated in phishing campaigns that deceive consumers into clicking malicious links, but these attacks exploit the brand name rather than the company's own systems. Australia Post publishes public warnings about such scams and works with authorities to address them.

Auth0.description: Auth0 provides authentication and authorisation as a service.
Auth0.history: Auth0 was co-founded in 2013 by Eugenio Pace and Matias Woloski with a focus on developer-friendly authentication and identity management. The company pioneered a bottom-up, developer-centric approach to identity-as-a-service (IDaaS) that contrasted with legacy enterprise IAM vendors. Auth0 raised substantial VC funding and became a unicorn. In May 2021, Okta acquired Auth0 in an all-stock deal valued at approximately $6.5 billion, one of the largest identity sector acquisitions. Auth0 continues to operate as an independent business unit within Okta.
Auth0.summary_abuse: Auth0 is a leading identity and authentication platform with strong security practices and a mature anti-abuse posture. As infrastructure for login and authentication, it is occasionally targeted by credential stuffing or account takeover attempts against applications built on it, but Auth0 itself provides robust security features including anomaly detection and breached password detection. The company runs a public bug bounty program and responds quickly to security disclosures. No notable incidents of Auth0's own platform being compromised have been widely reported.

Auth0 Lock.description: Auth0 Lock enables you to easily add social identity providers, so that your users can log in seamlessly using any desired provider.
Auth0 Lock.history: Auth0 Lock is a client-side JavaScript library created by Auth0 (now part of Okta) to render an embeddable login and signup widget for web applications. First released around 2014 alongside the growth of Auth0's platform, it became the default integration method for Auth0 customers. Auth0 has indicated that new feature development for Lock has ceased and it is now only receiving bug fixes and security patches, with the newer Universal Login experience being the recommended path forward.
Auth0 Lock.summary_abuse: Auth0 Lock is an open-source JavaScript widget library with no notable direct abuse signal. As a client-side authentication widget, it is used by legitimate applications for login flows. The library itself is not a vector for abuse; vulnerabilities in applications using it would be implementation issues rather than library-level problems.

authorize.net.description: Authorize.net is a secure online payment gateway service that enables businesses to accept payments through various channels, such as ecommerce websites, mobile devices, and retail stores, providing a trusted platform for processing credit card and electronic cheque payments.
authorize.net.history: Authorize.Net was founded in 1996 in Utah as one of the earliest online payment gateways. It was acquired by CyberSource in November 2007 for $565 million, and subsequently became part of Visa when Visa acquired CyberSource in July 2010 for approximately $2 billion. It remains one of the most widely used payment gateway solutions in the US.
authorize.net.summary_abuse: Authorize.Net is a major payment gateway with PCI DSS compliance and strong anti-fraud controls. Occasional abuse includes fraudulent merchants attempting to process stolen cards, but as a Visa subsidiary it maintains rigorous merchant vetting, active fraud monitoring, and prompt investigation of reported abuse. Its abuse exposure is proportionate to its scale, not disproportionately high.

authorized.by.description: authorized.by is a service that enables shops to verify products and confirm their authenticity.
authorized.by.history: authorized.by is a German product authenticity verification service enabling shops to confirm and display product authenticity for consumers.
authorized.by.summary_abuse: authorized.by is a product authentication service with no notable abuse signal. It operates in the opposite direction of fraud — it is used to verify genuine products.

Authy.description: Authy is a security tool that enables two-factor authentication (2FA) for websites to help protect user accounts from unauthorized access.
Authy.history: Authy was founded in 2012 as a two-factor authentication app providing TOTP tokens with cloud backup and multi-device sync. Twilio acquired Authy in 2015 to expand its communications and security product portfolio. The app became Twilio's recommended 2FA solution for developers building on the Twilio platform. It remains a widely used authenticator app competing with Google Authenticator and Microsoft Authenticator.
Authy.summary_abuse: Authy is a two-factor authentication platform owned by Twilio with a strong security posture. In 2022, Authy experienced a data breach where attackers accessed an internal database and exposed user phone numbers. The breach affected 33 million accounts. Twilio responded by notifying affected users and improving security controls. The platform includes features specifically designed to prevent account takeover. Outside this incident, Authy is not associated with facilitating abuse by its users.

Auto HQ.description: Auto HQ is a provider of custom car dealer websites and SEO services designed to enhance visibility and attract customers.
Auto HQ.history: Auto HQ is a UK-based provider of custom car dealer websites and SEO services. No notable ownership changes documented.
Auto HQ.summary_abuse: Auto HQ has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche UK automotive dealer website builder with no documented abuse patterns.

Autocommerce.description: Autocommerce is a product recommendation app for Shopify stores, similar to Amazon, designed to enhance the shopping experience by suggesting relevant products to customers based on their browsing and purchase history.
Autocommerce.history: Autocommerce is a Brazilian e-commerce platform specialized in the automotive sector, enabling car dealerships and resellers to create virtual stores and sell vehicles online with a fully digital purchase journey. The platform integrates with inventory management systems and payment processors common in the Brazilian market.
Autocommerce.summary_abuse: Autocommerce is a niche Brazilian automotive e-commerce platform. As a platform that allows vehicle listings and online transactions, there is some inherent risk of fraudulent vehicle listings, but this is a general risk for automotive marketplaces rather than documented abuse specific to this platform.

autoComplete.js.description: autoComplete.js is a simple, pure vanilla Javascript library.
autoComplete.js.history: autoComplete.js was created by Tarek Raafat and released around 2019 as a lightweight, pure vanilla JavaScript autocomplete library. It has no backing company and is maintained independently.
autoComplete.js.summary_abuse: autoComplete.js is a frontend JavaScript autocomplete widget with no notable abuse signal. It has no server-side surface.

Autoconf.description: Autoconf is a system designed for managing car dealership operations, including inventory, sales, and customer data.
Autoconf.history: Autoconf was created by David MacKenzie at the Free Software Foundation in the summer of 1991, born out of the need to handle software portability across different Unix variants without hand-editing Makefiles. It became a cornerstone of the GNU Autotools build system alongside Automake and Libtool. The last major release was in 2012, and while development activity has slowed as Linux dominance reduced portability concerns, the project remains part of the GNU toolchain and is still widely used in legacy and complex C/C++ open-source projects.
Autoconf.summary_abuse: Autoconf is a foundational GNU build configuration tool with no consumer-facing surface or abuse signal. It is purely a developer tool used during software compilation and has no role in phishing, malware distribution, or other abusive use cases.

Autoketing.description: Autoketing is a marketing automation platform.
Autoketing.history: Autoketing is a Vietnamese company based in Hanoi that develops Shopify apps for marketing and conversion optimization. The company offers over 10 apps on the Shopify App Store including Sales Popup, SEO Image Optimizer, Currency Converter, and product review tools, targeting small and medium e-commerce merchants.
Autoketing.summary_abuse: Autoketing develops Shopify apps for marketing and sales. The apps are primarily used by legitimate small merchants. Social proof popup apps like those offered by Autoketing can theoretically display misleading or fake activity notifications, but there are no documented reports of Autoketing's apps being specifically used to deceive consumers at scale.

Autoketing Product Reviews.description: Autoketing Product Reviews is an application that allows shop owners to manage the product review section on their website.
Autoketing Product Reviews.history: Autoketing Product Reviews is a Shopify app developed by Autoketing, a Vietnamese software company. It allows Shopify merchants to collect and display product reviews and user-generated content for social proof. It is one of several Shopify apps in the Autoketing portfolio.
Autoketing Product Reviews.summary_abuse: Autoketing Product Reviews is a Shopify review collection app with no specific documented abuse. Review apps in general can be used to display fake reviews, but there are no specific reports of this app being used for review fraud at scale.

Automabots.description: Automabots is an automated intelligent workforce designed to match home buyers with suitable properties.
Automabots.history: Automabots is a real estate chatbot platform that uses AI to engage and qualify home buyers and sellers. It integrates with IDX real estate sites and can be deployed on websites, Facebook, and messaging platforms. The platform integrates with Twilio for SMS functionality.
Automabots.summary_abuse: Automabots is a real estate chatbot platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a niche B2B tool used by real estate agents and is not associated with phishing or consumer fraud.

AutoManager.description: AutoManager is a CMS system designed specifically for automotive businesses.
AutoManager.history: AutoManager is a dealer management system (DMS) provider with over 25 years of history, primarily serving independent used car dealerships in the US. The company has been backed by the Beekman Group and in September 2024 completed the acquisition of EverLogic, expanding its reach to RV, trailer, and powersports dealerships. Its core product is DeskManager, a cloud-based DMS for inventory, CRM, and accounting.
AutoManager.summary_abuse: AutoManager is a dealer management system for automotive dealerships with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B platform used by car dealerships for internal operations and does not expose consumer-facing features that could be exploited for phishing or fraud.

Automatad.description: Automatad is a digital media products company that provides a suite of programmatic monetisation solutions.
Automatad.history: Automatad is a programmatic ad monetization platform for independent publishers, built on top of Prebid header bidding technology. The company provides a header bidding wrapper integrating 50+ SSPs and DSPs, helping publishers maximize ad revenue. It operates headerbidding.co, an educational resource for the ad tech industry. No major acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Automatad.summary_abuse: Automatad is a publisher-side programmatic advertising platform that monetizes web content via header bidding. Like other ad tech platforms, it participates in the broader programmatic ecosystem which carries some risk of serving low-quality or malicious ad creatives. No specific documented incidents of Automatad being used to distribute malware or facilitate fraud have been reported.

Automated Growth.description: Automated Growth is a platform offering AI and automation tools designed to streamline business operations.
Automated Growth.history: Automated Growth is a marketing automation and AI tools platform. Very limited public history available.
Automated Growth.summary_abuse: Automated Growth has no notable abuse signal. It is a small AI marketing automation platform with no documented abuse patterns.

AutomatePro.description: AutomatePro is a workflow automation platform that streamlines routine business processes.
AutomatePro.history: AutomatePro is a UK-based provider of automated testing and DevOps tools specifically for ServiceNow enterprise platform. The company's patented test automation solution allows business users rather than developers to run automated tests, covering the full ServiceNow SDLC from requirements through deployment and monitoring.
AutomatePro.summary_abuse: AutomatePro is a B2B enterprise testing automation tool for ServiceNow with no notable abuse signal. It is used by enterprise IT teams for internal quality assurance and does not present a consumer-facing attack surface.

AutomateWoo.description: AutomateWoo is a marketing automation plugin for WooCommerce that enables online stores to create automated workflows for emails, follow-ups, and customer engagement.
AutomateWoo.history: AutomateWoo is a marketing automation plugin for WooCommerce, originally created as an independent product and later acquired by Automattic (the company behind WordPress and WooCommerce). It became part of the official WooCommerce marketplace and is sold as a premium extension. The plugin enables workflow automation including abandoned cart recovery, personalized emails, SMS notifications, and loyalty programs for WooCommerce stores.
AutomateWoo.summary_abuse: AutomateWoo is a WooCommerce marketing automation plugin with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool used by e-commerce merchants for legitimate marketing automation. As an email and SMS marketing tool, it could theoretically be misused to send spam, but it operates through individual merchants using their own customer data rather than enabling mass unsolicited messaging.

Automatic Members.description: Automic Members is an all-in-one marketing platform for fitness businesses.
Automatic Members.history: Automatic Members is an all-in-one marketing platform specifically designed for fitness businesses. Limited public history available.
Automatic Members.summary_abuse: Automatic Members has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche fitness business marketing platform with no documented abuse patterns.

Automatic.css.description: Automatic.css is a CSS framework for WordPress page builders.
Automatic.css.history: Automatic.css (ACSS) is a CSS utility framework for WordPress page builders, created by Kevin Geary of Digital Gravy and launched in November 2021. It quickly became highly popular among WordPress developers using Oxygen Builder and Bricks Builder. Notably, Automattic (the company behind WordPress) disputed the use of the word "Automatic" in the product name.
Automatic.css.summary_abuse: Automatic.css is a CSS framework tool for WordPress developers with no abuse signal. It is purely a developer productivity tool with no consumer-facing surface.

AutomaticSales.description: AutomaticSales is a platform that centralizes marketing tools, offering unlimited sales funnels, email, SMS, web chat, and additional features in one system.
AutomaticSales.history: AutomaticSales is an all-in-one AI-powered marketing platform with sales funnels, email, SMS, and web chat features. Very limited public history available.
AutomaticSales.summary_abuse: AutomaticSales has no notable abuse signal. It is a small marketing automation platform. The SMS and email features could be misused for spam, but there are no documented abuse incidents.

Automizely.description: Automizely creates and manages enterprise-level marketing automation systems including contact and CRM mappings, lead funnels, email nurture, lead-generating pages, and blog posts, and website integrations.
Automizely.history: Automizely was founded in 2018 as an all-in-one e-commerce marketing platform for Shopify merchants, offering email, SMS, pop-ups, and product recommendations. The brand was integrated into AfterShip's product family in April 2023, with Automizely's marketing products rebranded under the AfterShip umbrella. AfterShip is a Singapore-based logistics SaaS company founded in 2012.
Automizely.summary_abuse: Automizely (now part of AfterShip) is a marketing automation tool for Shopify merchants. As an email and SMS marketing platform, it could theoretically be used to send spam, but it operates under AfterShip's terms of service. No specific public reports of the platform being used for large-scale abuse have been documented.

AutoOps.description: AutoOps is an online scheduling tool that integrates with shop management softwares for auto shops.
AutoOps.history: AutoOps is an online scheduling platform for auto repair shops that integrates with Google Business Profiles to provide a booking button in Google Search and Maps. The platform connects with shop management software to sync appointments in real time. No major ownership changes are documented.
AutoOps.summary_abuse: AutoOps is an appointment scheduling tool for auto repair shops with no notable abuse signal. It is a niche B2B platform serving small auto service businesses and does not present a consumer-facing attack surface for phishing or fraud.

Autopilot.description: Autopilot is a visual marketing software that enables users to create marketing campaigns and manage lead conversions.
Autopilot.history: Autopilot was founded in 2012 in Australia as a visual customer journey and marketing automation platform. The company raised venture funding and grew to serve thousands of businesses globally. In 2022, Autopilot rebranded as Ortto to reflect its expanded customer data platform capabilities, which now combines data unification, marketing automation, and analytics into a single platform.
Autopilot.summary_abuse: Autopilot (now Ortto) is a marketing automation and customer data platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool used for legitimate email and multi-channel marketing campaigns, and is not associated with spam or phishing.

Autoptimize.description: Autoptimize is a WordPress plugin that optimises website performance by aggregating, minifying, and compressing HTML, CSS, and JavaScript files.
Autoptimize.history: Autoptimize is a free and open-source WordPress performance optimization plugin created by Belgian developer Frank Goossens (futtta), first released around 2009. It grew to over 1 million active installs on WordPress.org. A premium "Autoptimize Pro" tier was later added offering page caching, image optimization CDN, and critical CSS features.
Autoptimize.summary_abuse: Autoptimize is an open-source WordPress performance plugin with no abuse signal. It optimizes HTML, CSS, and JavaScript for page load speed and does not introduce tracking or any consumer-facing attack surface.

autoship.description: autoship is a feature that lets customers schedule recurring orders and receive savings, enabling merchants to create automated repeat purchases and manage scheduled orders.
autoship.history: Autoship is a subscription and recurring orders platform for WooCommerce and other e-commerce systems, operating through a cloud service called QPilot. Details of founding are not widely documented.
autoship.summary_abuse: Autoship is an e-commerce subscription management service with no notable abuse signal. It processes recurring orders for legitimate merchants and does not expose content-hosting or anonymous-account features that fraudsters would exploit.

AutoVitals.description: AutoVitals is a shop productivity, acquisition, and retention tool designed for the automotive industry.
AutoVitals.history: AutoVitals is a SaaS platform providing digital vehicle inspection, workflow automation, and marketing tools for independent auto repair shops in the US. The company positioned itself as a leading digital shop management solution targeting the small and medium-sized repair shop market. No major acquisitions or rebrands are documented.
AutoVitals.summary_abuse: AutoVitals is an auto repair shop management platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool used internally by automotive service businesses and does not present a consumer-facing attack surface.

Auxilia.description: Auxilia is a software solution designed to streamline donor management processes for nonprofits.
Auxilia.history: Auxilia is a donor management and fundraising platform for nonprofits, founded by former executive directors who designed it based on real nonprofit needs. The platform offers donation management, email outreach, event ticketing, peer-to-peer fundraising, and social media automation. Starting price is approximately $250 per month.
Auxilia.summary_abuse: Auxilia is a nonprofit fundraising management platform with no notable abuse signal. It handles donation processing for charitable organizations and is subject to standard payment processing security controls.

Avada AVASHIP.description: Avada AVASHIP is an order tracking Shopify app.
Avada AVASHIP.history: Avada AVASHIP is an order tracking Shopify app developed by Avada Commerce, a Vietnamese company that builds a suite of Shopify apps for e-commerce merchants. Avada Commerce was founded around 2017 and offers apps across SEO, email marketing, social proof, size charts, and shipping tracking.
Avada AVASHIP.summary_abuse: Avada AVASHIP is a Shopify order tracking app with no notable abuse signal. It is used by merchants to provide shipment tracking to their customers and is not associated with phishing or fraudulent use cases.

Avada Boost Sales.description: AVADA Boost Sales is a one-stop solution that is specially designed to increase your sales with countdown timer, trust badges, sales pop, sales boost and many more.
Avada Boost Sales.history: Avada Boost Sales is a Shopify conversion optimization app developed by Avada Commerce, a Vietnamese e-commerce app development company. The app bundles multiple conversion features including countdown timers, trust badges, sales pop notifications, and upsell tools into a single Shopify app.
Avada Boost Sales.summary_abuse: Avada Boost Sales uses social proof tactics like fake sales notification popups that can display misleading purchase notifications if not configured to show only real recent orders. This raises a moderate dark pattern concern, though the app is used for legitimate conversion optimization by most merchants.

Avada SEO.description: Avada SEO is a Shopify app built and designed following strict SEO practices.
Avada SEO.history: Avada SEO is a Shopify app for search engine optimization developed by Avada Commerce, a Vietnamese e-commerce app company. The app evolved into the Avada AI SEO Suite, which uses AI to audit pages and apply SEO fixes. It is one of the most popular SEO apps on the Shopify App Store.
Avada SEO.summary_abuse: Avada SEO is a Shopify SEO optimization app with no notable abuse signal. It helps merchants improve organic search rankings and is not associated with black-hat SEO tactics or spamming at scale.

Avada Size Chart.description: Avada Size Chart is a thoughtful app that helps online stores reduce return rates with useful size guides.
Avada Size Chart.history: Avada Size Chart is a Shopify app developed by Avada Commerce, a Vietnamese e-commerce app development company. The app allows merchants to create customizable size charts and fit recommendation guides to reduce returns and improve conversion rates for apparel and fashion products.
Avada Size Chart.summary_abuse: Avada Size Chart is a Shopify size guide app with no notable abuse signal. It is a straightforward UX enhancement tool used by fashion merchants to reduce size-related returns.

Avangate.description: Avangate (2Checkout) is a digital ecommerce platform for businesses that sell physical goods or digital products.
Avangate.history: Avangate was founded in 2006 in Romania as a digital commerce platform for software companies. In April 2017, Avangate acquired 2Checkout to expand its payment and global commerce capabilities. The combined company subsequently rebranded entirely as 2Checkout, using that name for the unified platform serving over 8,000 merchants in 200 countries. 2Checkout was later acquired by Verifone in 2020.
Avangate.summary_abuse: Avangate/2Checkout is a digital commerce and payment platform that processes transactions for software and digital goods companies globally. As a payment processor, it is occasionally used by merchants operating grey-area or potentially fraudulent digital goods businesses. The company publishes anti-fraud policies and PCI-DSS compliance but has historically received complaints about difficulty canceling subscriptions set up through its platform.

Avanser.description: Avanser allow you to track every call and enable your business to make better-informed decisions based on your phone calls.
Avanser.history: Avanser was founded in 2003 in Sydney, Australia as the first company to bring advanced call tracking to the Asia-Pacific region. The company grew to become the leading call tracking technology provider in the APAC region, serving clients including CBRE, Aston Martin, and Bank of Queensland. It has evolved its platform to include conversation analytics and AI-driven lead management.
Avanser.summary_abuse: Avanser is a call tracking and analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool used by marketing agencies and businesses to attribute phone leads to marketing channels. The platform records and transcribes calls, raising standard privacy considerations, but is not associated with fraud or phishing.

Avasam.description: Avasam is a platform that provides dropship suppliers with channel integration and automation tools to connect and manage online stores.
Avasam.history: Avasam is a UK-based dropshipping marketplace platform founded around 2017, providing automated dropshipping tools for UK and US-based suppliers. The platform connects online retailers with verified UK suppliers and integrates with Shopify, eBay, and Amazon. It positions itself as the UK's only multi-channel dropshipping marketplace.
Avasam.summary_abuse: Avasam is a dropshipping marketplace that connects retailers with UK suppliers. Dropshipping platforms carry some inherent risk of being used for fake or low-quality product listings, counterfeit goods, or misleading product descriptions. Avasam vets its suppliers but the dropshipping model itself carries these general risks. No specific documented incidents of platform-level abuse have been reported.

Avasize.history: Avasize was founded in 2017 as an AI-based size recommendation widget for fashion e-commerce, helping customers choose the correct clothing size to reduce returns. The widget integrates with platforms including PrestaShop and Adobe Commerce.
Avasize.summary_abuse: Avasize is a clothing size recommendation widget with no notable abuse signal. It is a niche UX enhancement tool for fashion e-commerce and is not associated with phishing or fraud.

Avature.description: Avature is a talent acquisition and talent management platform that provides solutions for recruitment, onboarding, and employee engagement.
Avature.history: Avature was founded in 2005 by Dimitri Boylan as an enterprise recruiting CRM and ATS platform. The company pioneered candidate relationship management (CRM) for enterprise talent acquisition. It serves over 650 enterprise customers in 164 countries including many Fortune 500 companies. Avature has remained an independent, private company and is considered the global market leader in enterprise recruiting CRM.
Avature.summary_abuse: Avature is an enterprise-only talent management platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used exclusively by large organizations for internal HR and recruiting operations and does not expose consumer-facing features that could be exploited for phishing or fraud.

AvidAI.description: AvidAI is a marketing platform that uses artificial intelligence to convert digital window-shoppers through automated campaigns, A/B testing, and personalized content optimization.
AvidAI.history: AvidAI is an AI marketing platform using automated campaigns and A/B testing for e-commerce conversion optimization. Very limited public history available.
AvidAI.summary_abuse: AvidAI has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche AI marketing platform with no documented abuse patterns.

AvidTrak.description: AvidTrak is software for call tracking, recording, and analytics, designed to monitor phone performance, log conversations, and provide insights from call data.
AvidTrak.history: AvidTrak is a US-based call tracking, recording, and analytics platform designed to help businesses monitor phone performance and attribute calls to marketing campaigns. It integrates with Google Analytics and other marketing platforms. No major ownership changes are documented.
AvidTrak.summary_abuse: AvidTrak is a B2B call tracking and analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. It records and analyzes business phone calls for marketing attribution and is not associated with fraud or phishing.

Avis Verifies.description: Avis Verifies is a complete solution for managing your customer reviews.
Avis Verifies.history: Avis Vérifiés (Avis Verifies) was founded in 2012 in Marseille, France as a verified customer review platform. It became the leading French solution for collecting, moderating, and publishing authentic post-purchase reviews, with over 26 million reviews collected. It is certified NF Service AFNOR in France, ensuring only real buyers can leave reviews. The platform was acquired by Skeepers, a French customer experience company, and now operates as part of Skeepers' portfolio.
Avis Verifies.summary_abuse: Avis Verifies is a verified customer review platform with strong authentication controls requiring proof of purchase. Its mandatory proof-of-purchase requirement significantly reduces the risk of fake reviews. As with any review platform, there is a general risk of merchants attempting to game the system, but the NF certification and strict controls mitigate this substantially.

Avizi.description: Avizi is a tourism CRM system from France designed to manage customer relationships, bookings, and marketing for travel businesses.
Avizi.history: Avizi is a French tourism CRM system designed to manage customer relationships, bookings, and marketing for tourism and hospitality businesses. The platform is tailored to the French tourism industry.
Avizi.summary_abuse: Avizi is a niche tourism CRM platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used by tourism and hospitality businesses for internal operations and customer management.

Aweber.description: AWeber is an email marketing service.
Aweber.history: AWeber was founded in 1998 by Tom Kulzer in Pennsylvania as one of the first email marketing and autoresponder platforms. The company pioneered email marketing automation for small businesses and grew to serve over 120,000 customers. AWeber has remained an independent, privately held company for over 25 years and is one of the longest-running email marketing platforms in the industry.
Aweber.summary_abuse: AWeber is an established email marketing platform. Like all bulk email services, it carries some risk of being used to send spam or phishing campaigns. AWeber publishes anti-spam policies and implements sending reputation controls, but as a mainstream email marketing tool, it is occasionally implicated when bad actors use it for unwanted mass mailings before accounts are terminated. Legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates.

Awesomplete.description: Awesomplete is a tool in the Javascript UI Libraries category of a tech stack.
Awesomplete.history: Awesomplete is an ultra-lightweight, zero-dependency JavaScript autocomplete library created by Lea Verou, a W3C CSS Working Group invited expert and author. Released in 2015 under the MIT license, it became popular for its minimal footprint and clean API. The library has seen minimal updates in recent years as the author has focused on other projects.
Awesomplete.summary_abuse: Awesomplete is an open-source autocomplete JavaScript library with no abuse signal. It is a frontend UI component with no consumer-facing surface that could be exploited.

AWIN.description: AWIN is a global affiliate marketing network.
AWIN.history: Awin was founded in 2000 in Germany and grew through acquisitions. The company was formed from the merger of zanox and Affiliate Window in 2016, unifying under the Awin brand in March 2017. In October 2017, Awin merged with affilinet. Awin is part of Axel Springer, the German media conglomerate. It now operates 1,000+ employees in 15 global offices serving thousands of advertisers and publishers.
AWIN.summary_abuse: Awin is a large affiliate marketing network. Affiliate networks are susceptible to cookie stuffing, misleading endorsements, and fraudulent publisher registrations. Awin publishes a Publisher Code of Conduct and has compliance teams to address violations. The scale of the network means occasional abuse incidents occur, but the vendor's enforcement processes are well-documented compared to smaller networks.

AWS Certificate Manager.description: AWS Certificate Manager is a service that lets you easily provision, manage, and deploy public and private Secure Sockets Layer/Transport Layer Security (SSL/TLS) certificates for use with AWS services and your internal connected resources.
AWS Certificate Manager.history: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) was launched by Amazon Web Services in 2016 to simplify SSL/TLS certificate provisioning for AWS resources. The service provides free public certificates for use with AWS-integrated services and has become the standard certificate management solution for AWS workloads.
AWS Certificate Manager.summary_abuse: AWS Certificate Manager is a foundational Amazon infrastructure service for SSL/TLS certificate management. No consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history. Free ACM certificates have been observed on some phishing pages hosted on AWS, but this is an AWS infrastructure issue rather than ACM-specific abuse.

AWS WAF Captcha.description: AWS WAF Captcha helps block unwanted bot traffic by requiring users to successfully complete challenges before their web request are allowed to reach AWS WAF protected resources.
AWS WAF Captcha.history: AWS WAF CAPTCHA is a feature of Amazon Web Services' WAF (Web Application Firewall) service, introduced in 2021 to provide bot protection with visual CAPTCHA challenges. The CAPTCHA feature integrates with AWS WAF rules to challenge suspicious requests without blocking legitimate users.
AWS WAF Captcha.summary_abuse: AWS WAF Captcha is an Amazon infrastructure bot protection service with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history. It is a security tool used by enterprises to protect web applications.

AWStats.history: AWStats (Advanced Web Statistics) was created by Laurent Destailleur of France in 1997 and remains the primary maintainer today. It is an open-source web server log analyzer distributed under GPL v3. Development started in 1997 and the first public commit appeared in 2000. AWStats processes server logs locally without sending data to third parties. The author also leads Dolibarr ERP and works for French open-source company TecLib.
AWStats.summary_abuse: AWStats is a server-side web log analyzer that processes data locally with no third-party data transmission. It has no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse history.

Awtomic.description: Awtomic is a platform that offers shoppers and merchants tools for managing subscription products and membership services.
Awtomic.history: Awtomic is a Shopify subscriptions app that allows merchants to sell recurring subscription products, bundles, wine clubs, and build-a-box offerings. It integrates with Shopify Checkout and major payment gateways. The platform targets wineries, specialty food, beauty, and coffee subscription businesses.
Awtomic.summary_abuse: Awtomic is a Shopify subscription management app with no notable abuse signal. It is used by merchants to sell legitimate subscription products and is not associated with fraud or deceptive billing practices beyond the inherent risks in any subscription commerce platform.

Axeptio.description: Axeptio is a trusted third party that collects and archive users' consent in a GDPR compliant fashion.
Axeptio.history: Axeptio was founded in 2017 in Montpellier, France as a GDPR consent management platform (CMP). The platform grew to support over 80,000 websites and received Google certification as a CMP. Axeptio gained strong market share in France and expanded internationally to cover CCPA, Quebec Law 25, and Swiss nFADP compliance requirements.
Axeptio.summary_abuse: Axeptio is a GDPR consent management platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform is specifically designed to help organizations comply with privacy regulations, and its core function of managing cookie consent is the opposite of enabling data misuse. No documented incidents of the platform being used for deceptive consent collection have been reported.

Axios.description: Promise based HTTP client for the browser and node.js
Axios.history: Axios is a Promise-based HTTP client for JavaScript, originally created by Matt Zabriskie in 2014. It became one of the most popular JavaScript libraries with over 22 million weekly npm downloads, widely adopted for making HTTP requests in both browser and Node.js environments. The project is maintained by the open-source community and hosted on GitHub under the MIT license.
Axios.summary_abuse: Axios is a foundational JavaScript HTTP client library with no abuse signal. It is used as plumbing for HTTP requests in web applications and has no consumer-facing surface. Axios itself cannot be used for phishing or fraud; it is simply a low-level networking utility.

Azion.history: Azion was founded in 2011 in Brazil and is headquartered in San Francisco with additional offices in Brazil and Singapore. The company provides a global edge computing platform combining CDN, serverless edge functions, security, and observability. It started as a Brazilian CDN focused on LATAM markets and grew into a global edge platform serving over 40,000 e-commerce sites and major banks in Latin America.
Azion.summary_abuse: Azion is an edge computing and CDN platform. Like other CDN and edge infrastructure providers, its services can be used to host or accelerate content that may include malicious sites, though Azion maintains acceptable use policies and abuse reporting procedures. Its primary market is legitimate e-commerce and banking in Latin America, and no specific widespread abuse incidents have been reported.

Azko CMS.description: Azko CMS is a content management system developed by Azko (Septeo Group) specifically launched for lawyers in France.
Azko CMS.history: Azko CMS is a content management system developed by Azko, a subsidiary of the Septeo Group, specifically designed for law firms in France. No notable ownership changes since acquisition by Septeo.
Azko CMS.summary_abuse: Azko CMS has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche CMS designed exclusively for French law firms, with no consumer-facing content hosting that would attract fraudsters.

Azoya.description: Azoya helps international brands and retailers sell directly to Chinese consumers through cross-border ecommerce.
Azoya.history: Azoya was founded in 2013 in Shenzhen, China, by Don Zhao and Alex Huang. The company operated as a cloud-based end-to-end cross-border e-commerce platform helping international brands and retailers enter the Chinese market. It offered services including e-commerce platform setup, warehousing, digital marketing, logistics, and customer support. Azoya supported over 100 retailers from more than 12 countries including La Redoute and Amcal. The company is now listed as deadpooled, indicating it is no longer actively operating.
Azoya.summary_abuse: No documented abuse incidents specific to Azoya's platform. The company focused on legitimate cross-border e-commerce enablement for established international brands entering China. As the company is now inactive (deadpooled), its platform is no longer in active use. No reported cases of platform misuse, data breaches, or fraud associated with Azoya during its operating years.

AzuraCast.description: AzuraCast is an open-source self-hosted web radio station in a box.
AzuraCast.history: AzuraCast was created around 2016 as an open-source self-hosted web radio management suite. It is licensed under AGPL-3.0 and hosted on GitHub under the AzuraCast/AzuraCast repository. The project is primarily maintained by a small community of contributors. It uses Docker for deployment and supports popular streaming backends including Icecast and Liquidsoap. No major ownership changes, acquisitions, or controversies have occurred; it remains a community-driven project with no commercial backing.
AzuraCast.summary_abuse: AzuraCast is self-hosted open-source radio broadcasting software with no meaningful abuse profile. It does not provide hosting services itself, so abuse potential is limited to the instance operator's actions. No documented cases of AzuraCast installations being used for malicious purposes. Since it is self-hosted and requires technical knowledge to deploy, it is not typically targeted by or used by low-sophistication threat actors.

Azure.description: Azure is a cloud computing service for building, testing, deploying, and managing applications and services through Microsoft-managed data centers.
Azure.history: Microsoft Azure launched in February 2010 as Windows Azure, Microsoft's cloud computing platform. In 2014 it was renamed Microsoft Azure. It is the world's second-largest cloud platform after AWS, offering over 200 products and services including compute, storage, networking, AI, databases, and developer tools. Microsoft Azure Stack allows on-premises deployments. Azure has grown to operate data centers across 60+ regions worldwide and is a cornerstone of Microsoft's commercial revenue. No major platform-level security failures define its history, though individual service-level incidents have occurred.
Azure.summary_abuse: Azure is occasionally implicated in abuse cases due to its scale: phishing pages, malware distribution, and command-and-control servers have been hosted on Azure blob storage and Azure App Services. Microsoft maintains active abuse detection and a takedown process, and responds comparably to AWS and Google Cloud. Legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates. Microsoft publishes an acceptable use policy and works with law enforcement and security researchers. Abuse is a small fraction of overall usage given the platform's massive legitimate customer base.

Azure AD B2C.description: Azure Active Directory B2C is a customer identity access management (CIAM) solution.
Azure AD B2C.history: Azure Active Directory B2C is Microsoft's customer identity and access management (CIAM) service, launched around 2015 as part of the Azure Active Directory family. It allows organizations to customize and control how customers sign up, sign in, and manage their profiles. In 2023, Microsoft began transitioning the Azure AD branding to Microsoft Entra ID, with Azure AD B2C expected to remain under the Entra External Identities umbrella. No significant controversies or ownership changes; it is a core Microsoft identity product.
Azure AD B2C.summary_abuse: Azure AD B2C is an identity platform used by legitimate enterprises to manage customer authentication. It has no notable abuse profile as a tool for bad actors, since it is a backend authentication service rather than a hosting or content platform. No documented cases of the service being used as infrastructure for phishing or scam operations. Microsoft's enterprise compliance posture and security controls make misuse very difficult.

Azure CDN.description: Azure Content Delivery Network (CDN) reduces load times, save bandwidth and speed responsiveness.
Azure CDN.history: Azure CDN is Microsoft's content delivery network service, launched as part of the Azure platform around 2010 and expanded significantly over the years. It provides global content caching and delivery through Microsoft's PoPs. Azure CDN offers multiple tiers powered by partners including Verizon and Akamai, as well as Microsoft's own CDN network. Microsoft has been consolidating CDN services under Azure Front Door and the broader Azure networking portfolio. No major controversies or ownership changes.
Azure CDN.summary_abuse: Azure CDN, like all major CDNs, is occasionally used to serve malicious content including phishing pages and malware payloads distributed from compromised or fraudulently registered origin servers. Microsoft operates abuse detection and takedown procedures consistent with other major cloud CDN providers. The vast majority of traffic is legitimate, and Microsoft's customer verification requirements limit anonymous abuse. Abuse is a small fraction of overall usage.

Azure Edge Network.description: Azure Edge Network is a global network infrastructure provided by Microsoft Azure. It is designed to deliver content, applications, and services to end-users with low latency and high performance. The Azure Edge Network consists of a combination of Azure Content Delivery Network (CDN), Azure Front Door, and Azure Traffic Manager.
Azure Edge Network.history: Azure Edge Network refers to Microsoft's edge delivery and networking infrastructure encompassing distributed PoPs worldwide for low-latency content delivery and application acceleration. It is part of the broader Azure networking portfolio that includes Azure CDN and Azure Front Door. Microsoft has been expanding its global edge presence to compete with Cloudflare and AWS CloudFront. No distinct founding date exists as it evolved from Azure's general networking infrastructure.
Azure Edge Network.summary_abuse: Azure Edge Network is infrastructure-level technology with no direct consumer surface and minimal standalone abuse profile. Like other major cloud edge networks, it can be used to cache or deliver malicious content originating from abusive customers, but Microsoft's abuse policies and enforcement apply across all Azure services. No specific abuse incidents are attributed to Azure Edge Network as a distinct product.

Azure Front Door.description: Azure Front Door is a scalable and secure entry point for fast delivery of your global web applications.
Azure Front Door.history: Azure Front Door is Microsoft's global application delivery network, offering load balancing, SSL offloading, URL-based routing, and web application firewall capabilities. It was generally available from 2019 and is positioned as Microsoft's primary entry point for global HTTP and HTTPS traffic routing. In 2022, Microsoft launched Azure Front Door Standard and Premium tiers, consolidating Azure CDN from Verizon and Akamai capabilities. No significant ownership changes or controversies.
Azure Front Door.summary_abuse: Azure Front Door is an enterprise-grade application delivery service with low inherent abuse risk. Like all CDN and reverse-proxy services, it can in theory be used to route traffic for malicious origins, but Microsoft's enterprise KYC requirements and abuse policies substantially reduce this risk. No specific high-profile abuse incidents attributable to Azure Front Door have been documented. Its WAF capabilities actively prevent rather than facilitate abuse.

Azure Monitor.description: Azure Monitor collects monitoring telemetry from a variety of on-premises and Azure sources. Azure Monitor helps you maximise the availability and performance of your applications and services.
Azure Monitor.history: Azure Monitor is Microsoft's full-stack monitoring and observability service for Azure resources and applications. It consolidates metrics, logs, alerts, and dashboards under one service, incorporating Azure Log Analytics and Application Insights. The service has been continuously expanded since around 2016, with Application Insights (launched 2015) integrated as a feature. Azure Monitor competes with Datadog, New Relic, and Dynatrace in the observability space. No notable controversies or ownership changes.
Azure Monitor.summary_abuse: Azure Monitor is a backend monitoring and observability service with no consumer-facing surface and negligible abuse profile. It is used by enterprises to monitor their own Azure workloads. No documented cases of Azure Monitor being abused for malicious purposes. Data processed by the service is governed by Microsoft's enterprise data processing agreements and GDPR/HIPAA compliance frameworks.

Azuriom.description: Azuriom is an open-source web solution for game servers, offering extensive customization through extensions.
Azuriom.history: Azuriom is an open-source web CMS for gaming communities and game servers, started in 2019. Built with the Laravel PHP framework, it is free and GPL-3.0 licensed. The project is community-driven with no commercial backing and has grown to serve over 1,800 game server websites with more than two million total users. No major ownership changes, forks, or controversies are associated with the project.
Azuriom.summary_abuse: Azuriom is a self-hosted gaming CMS with no significant abuse profile. Since it is self-hosted software for game server communities, abuse potential is limited to how individual server operators use it. No documented cases of Azuriom being used for phishing, scams, or malware distribution have been reported. Its niche gaming community focus and technical deployment requirements limit exposure to unsophisticated threat actors.
